prompts.title: 90s anime aesthetic frame
prompts.when: You want a 90s cel-animation anime look with film grain, specific color palette, and vintage animation feel.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a 90s anime animator in the cel-animation era (Evangelion, Cowboy Bebop, Trigun, Ghost in the Shell).</role>
<task>Render a 90s anime frame of {{scene_description}}.</task>
<animation>Traditional cel-animation look: crisp line art, flat color fills with minimal gradients, hand-painted background texture, visible film grain.</animation>
<palette>90s anime palette: muted primary colors, often slightly desaturated, occasional neon accent. Skin tones varied, not always pink.</palette>
<composition>Dynamic asymmetric framing. Strong diagonals. Often close-up with shallow depth of field implied by background style.</composition>
<subject>{{subject_concept}}. Wardrobe and props period-accurate to 1990s if contemporary scene.</subject>
<grain>Visible 35mm film grain. Subtle registration marks or grain patterns. Slight color fringing.</grain>
<output>16:9 PNG or 4:3 PNG.</output>
<negative>no modern digital clarity, no moe-style huge eyes, no watermark.</negative>
notes: 90s anime convention: cel-animation look + muted palette + visible film grain + dynamic asymmetric framing is the era signature.
Youmind 90s / retro anime references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/anime-manga.
antiPatterns: Do not apply modern digital sharpening.
Do not default to moe eye proportions. 90s proportions vary by series.

prompts.title: Ink wash sumi-e anime
prompts.when: You want a sumi-e ink-wash anime illustration blending traditional Japanese painting with anime character design.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an ink-wash painter blending traditional sumi-e with anime character design.</role>
<task>Render an ink-wash anime illustration of {{scene_description}}.</task>
<ink>Black ink gradations from deep black to gray wash on textured rice paper. Visible brush strokes, occasional ink bleeds and splatter. Negative space intentional.</ink>
<character>{{character_description}}. Face and key features rendered in anime line art with selective ink wash shading. Hair and clothing flow into ink wash.</character>
<color>Optional single accent color: {{accent_color: red | gold | indigo}}. Used sparingly on clothing accent, flower, or seal.</color>
<background>Minimal sumi-e environment: bamboo, branch, mountain, river, or empty space. Negative space dominant.</background>
<style>Calligraphic line work, restrained detail, deliberate negative space. Reference masters: Hokusai, Hasegawa Saburo, modern illustrators like Yuko Shimizu.</style>
<output>Vertical 2:3 or horizontal 3:1 PNG.</output>
<negative>no full color palette, no busy background, no modern digital look, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Sumi-e anime convention: ink wash gradations + negative space + selective anime line art is the fusion.
Youmind ink / Chinese style anime references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/ink-chinese-style.
antiPatterns: Do not fill the negative space. Restraint is the genre.
Do not use a full color palette. One accent at most.

prompts.title: Anime character turnaround sheet
prompts.when: You want an anime character reference sheet with multiple views, expressions, and outfit details.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an anime character designer producing a reference sheet.</role>
<task>Design a character reference sheet for {{character_name}}, a {{role_or_archetype}}.</task>
<layout>White background. Top row: 3 turnaround views (front, three-quarter, side). Second row: 3 to 4 expression studies. Third row: 2 detail callouts (one prop, one costume detail).</layout>
<character>Consistent line weight and proportions across all panels. {{character_description}}. Visible palette in small color swatches at the bottom.</character>
<expressions>Choose: {{expression_set}}. Anime stylization. Same character, different emotion.</expressions>
<details>One prop close-up: {{prop}}. One costume detail close-up: {{detail}}.</details>
<color_swatch>5 to 7 colors total, displayed as small filled rectangles with hex codes or color names.</color_swatch>
<output>Landscape 16:9 PNG or square 1:1 PNG.</output>
<negative>no photoreal, no scenery, no watermark, no other characters.</negative>
notes: Character sheet convention: consistent line weight + turnaround + expression row + detail callouts + color swatches is the production reference standard.
Youmind character sheet / reference patterns: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/character.
antiPatterns: Do not vary proportions between views.
Do not omit the color swatches. They anchor future renders.

prompts.title: Manga storyboard grid
prompts.when: You want a manga page storyboard with multiple panels showing a sequence of action.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a manga storyboard artist laying out a sequence.</role>
<task>Lay out a {{n_panel}}-panel manga storyboard for the sequence: {{sequence_description}}.</task>
<layout>Vertical manga page, white background, black panel borders. {{n_panel}} panels arranged for visual flow. No gutters between panels of equal weight.</layout>
<panel_1_top>Wide establishing panel if the sequence starts in a new location.</panel_1_top>
<panel_2_to_n_minus_1>Medium and close-up panels showing the action progression.</panel_2_to_n_minus_1>
<panel_n>Final impact panel, often larger or vertically oriented for emphasis.</panel_n>
<style>Black-and-white manga line art with screentone shading. Speed lines, impact lines, motion lines where appropriate. Optional small speech bubbles with placeholder lines.</style>
<composition>Strong directional eye flow. Action reads left-to-right, top-to-bottom.</composition>
<output>Vertical 2:3 PNG.</output>
<negative>no color, no photoreal, no panels outside the {{n_panel}} count.</negative>
notes: Manga storyboard convention: vertical page + eye flow + screentones + speed lines + impact emphasis on final panel is the production standard.
Youmind manga / storyboard references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/comic-storyboard.
antiPatterns: Do not use color. Manga storyboards are black and white.
Do not place panels randomly. Eye flow matters.

prompts.title: Children's book watercolor illustration
prompts.when: You want a children's book illustration in soft watercolor with friendly characters and warm storytelling.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a children's book illustrator in the tradition of Beatrix Potter, Tove Jansson, and modern picture-book art.</role>
<task>Illustrate the scene: {{scene_description}} for a children's book page.</task>
<style>Soft watercolor washes with visible brush texture. Friendly rounded character shapes. Slight pencil underdrawing visible at edges. Mixed media feel.</style>
<character>Optional {{character_description}}. Exaggerated head-to-body ratio. Large friendly eyes. Soft features.</character>
<color>Warm inviting palette: cream background, soft primary colors. Light source warm and diffuse.</color>
<story_moment>The image tells one beat of a story. Subject is mid-action, mid-expression. Room for the text in margins.</story_moment>
<output>Square 1:1 or landscape 4:3 PNG.</output>
<negative>no photoreal, no horror, no anime, no neon, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Children's book illustration convention: soft watercolor + friendly rounded shapes + one-story-beat composition is the genre.
Youmind children's illustration references and the Caldecott / Greenaway tradition.
antiPatterns: Do not render photoreal skin. Watercolor style is the point.
Do not include multiple story beats. One beat per illustration.

prompts.title: Vector flat illustration
prompts.when: You want a flat vector-style illustration with clean shapes and bold colors for an editorial or marketing use.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a vector illustrator working in the modern flat-design tradition.</role>
<task>Render a flat vector illustration of {{scene_description}}.</task>
<style>Clean geometric shapes. No gradients except subtle 2-step shading. Crisp edges, no texture. Bold flat color fields.</style>
<palette>Limited to {{n}} colors total. One accent reserved for the focal subject. Background in muted neutral.</palette>
<composition>Subject takes 50 to 70 percent of canvas. Supporting elements arranged for balance, not narrative.</composition>
<line>No outlines. Shape is defined by color contrast only. Optional thin single-weight strokes only if specified.</line>
<output>Square 1:1 or landscape 16:9 PNG.</output>
<negative>no photoreal, no texture, no busy background, no more than {{n}} colors, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Flat vector convention: no outline + limited palette + shape-by-color-contrast is the modern flat-design standard.
Youmind flat illustration references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/illustration.
antiPatterns: Do not add texture. Flat is the genre.
Do not exceed the color limit.

prompts.title: Isometric illustration scene
prompts.when: You want an isometric (axonometric) scene for a tech explainer, SaaS hero, or game-style UI illustration.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an isometric scene illustrator working in the 30-degree axonometric convention.</role>
<task>Render an isometric scene of {{scene_concept}}.</task>
<projection>True isometric: 30-degree angles on X and Y axes. No perspective convergence. Equal foreshortening on all three axes.</projection>
<tile>Floor and walls as a flat grid with consistent tile size. Objects placed on the grid, never floating unless intentional.</tile>
<palette>{{n}} colors max. Two shades per color for top and side faces to imply lighting. Single light direction.</palette>
<subject>Optional characters or objects in simple geometric form. Faces can be minimal. No detailed features.</subject>
<style>Clean vector-style rendering. Crisp edges. No texture. Optional subtle drop shadow under floating elements.</style>
<output>Square 1:1 or landscape 16:9 PNG.</output>
<negative>no perspective convergence, no photoreal, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Isometric convention: 30-degree axonometric + no perspective convergence + 2-shade lighting is the genre.
Youmind isometric references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/isometric.
antiPatterns: Do not add perspective convergence. Isometric has none.
Do not mix axonometric angles.

prompts.title: Editorial magazine illustration
prompts.when: You want an editorial magazine illustration that communicates a concept metaphorically, in the New Yorker / New York Times Opinion tradition.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an editorial illustrator working for a New Yorker or NYT Opinion magazine spread.</role>
<task>Render an editorial illustration that visualizes the concept: {{concept}}.</task>
<metaphor>One clear visual metaphor for the concept. Pick {{metaphor}}. The illustration should reward a second look, not a glance.</metaphor>
<style>{{style: loose ink and watercolor | clean vector | hand-cut paper collage | risograph texture | scratchboard | gouache}}.</style>
<palette>Limited to 3 to 5 colors. One accent. Slightly desaturated, editorial-feeling.</palette>
<composition>Asymmetric. Often a single dominant element with supporting detail. Negative space intentional.</composition>
<output>Vertical 2:3 PNG (magazine column ratio) or square 1:1.</output>
<negative>no photoreal, no clip art, no busy narrative, no more than 5 colors, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Editorial illustration convention: one metaphor + named style + restricted palette is the genre. The illustration earns a second look.
New Yorker and NYT Opinion illustration references; youmind editorial patterns: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/illustration.
antiPatterns: Do not illustrate literally. The metaphor is the work.
Do not exceed the color limit.

prompts.title: Risograph print illustration
prompts.when: You want a risograph print look with limited spot colors, registration imperfections, and grain.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a risograph print designer familiar with the limited-spot-color and registration-imperfection aesthetic.</role>
<task>Render a risograph-style illustration of {{scene_description}}.</task>
<colors>2 to 3 spot colors from the riso palette: {{colors: fluoro pink | teal | sun yellow | cobalt blue | mint | orange | violet}}. Each color is a separate pass with possible registration drift.</colors>
<texture>Visible risograph grain (ink dot pattern). Slight misregistration between color layers. Subtle paper texture.</texture>
<line>Hand-drawn quality with slight wobble. Optional halftone dot fills.</line>
<composition>Asymmetric, modern editorial feel. Negative space intentional.</composition>
<output>Vertical 2:3 PNG or square 1:1 PNG.</output>
<negative>no photoreal, no full color palette, no clean digital vector look, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Risograph convention: 2-3 spot colors + registration drift + halftone grain is the print signature.
Youmind riso / print illustration references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/illustration.
antiPatterns: Do not exceed 3 spot colors.
Do not request clean digital vector perfection.

prompts.title: Detailed watercolor botanical
prompts.when: You want a detailed watercolor botanical illustration of a plant, flower, or fruit.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a botanical illustrator in the tradition of Pierre-Joseph Redoute and modern watercolor botanical art.</role>
<task>Render a detailed watercolor botanical illustration of {{plant_subject}}.</task>
<specimen>One single plant specimen or small grouping. Stem, leaves, flower or fruit, root system if relevant. Multiple parts visible.</specimen>
<style>Traditional watercolor botanical: precise scientific detail, layered washes, fine brush linework for veins and textures, visible pigment behavior.</style>
<palette>Natural pigments: {{pigments: sap green | cadmium yellow | burnt sienna | quinacridone magenta | ultramarine | cobalt teal | payne's gray}}.</palette>
<background>Cream or white paper with subtle texture. No scenery.</background>
<label>Optional small handwritten Latin binomial name at the bottom in italic.</label>
<output>Vertical 2:3 or square 1:1 PNG.</output>
<negative>no photoreal, no anime, no busy background, no more than 6 pigments, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Botanical illustration convention: scientific precision + named pigments + visible watercolor behavior is the tradition.
Pierre-Joseph Redoute and the Royal Horticultural Society botanical illustration conventions.
antiPatterns: Do not skip the fine detail work. Botanical art is precise.
Do not use flat digital color.

prompts.title: Gouache poster illustration
prompts.when: You want a gouache opaque-paint illustration for a poster or editorial cover.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a gouache painter working in the modern editorial cover tradition.</role>
<task>Render a gouache poster illustration of {{scene_description}}.</task>
<paint>Opaque gouache: matte finish, no glossy highlights. Layered flat color fields with visible brushwork. Soft edges allowed at shadows.</paint>
<palette>{{palette}}. Limited to {{n}} colors mixed from primaries. Slightly desaturated, editorial.</palette>
<composition>Strong focal subject filling 50 to 70 percent of canvas. Asymmetric balance. Bold simple shapes.</composition>
<light>Soft directional light. Single source. Shadows are slightly cooler than midtones.</light>
<style>Modern editorial cover: clean, painterly, intentional. Reference: New Yorker covers, NYT Magazine covers, vintage WPA posters.</style>
<output>Vertical 2:3 PNG.</output>
<negative>no photoreal, no watercolor transparency, no busy detail, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Gouache convention: opaque matte paint + flat color fields + visible brushwork is the medium signature.
Youmind gouache / poster references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/illustration.
antiPatterns: Do not request watercolor transparency. Gouache is opaque.
Do not add busy detail.

prompts.title: Pen-and-ink crosshatch illustration
prompts.when: You want a black-and-white pen-and-ink illustration with detailed crosshatch shading.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a pen-and-ink illustrator working in the crosshatch tradition.</role>
<task>Render a pen-and-ink illustration of {{scene_description}}.</task>
<medium>Black ink on cream or white paper. Crosshatch and parallel line shading. Stippling for soft textures. No gray washes, no color.</medium>
<line>Varied line weight. Thicker contours on the subject outline, finer lines for interior detail and shading.</line>
<shading>Crosshatch density indicates shadow depth. Pure white paper reads as highlight. Pure black fills as deep shadow.</shading>
<composition>Strong central subject. Supporting detail arranged for balance. Negative space important.</composition>
<style>Classic engraving / etching aesthetic. Reference: Gustave Dore, M.C. Escher, Ralph Steadman ink.</style>
<output>Vertical 2:3 PNG or square 1:1 PNG.</output>
<negative>no color, no gray wash, no photoreal, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Pen-and-ink convention: crosshatch density as shading + varied line weight + no gray wash is the medium.
Youmind ink illustration references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/sketch-line-art.
antiPatterns: Do not add gray wash. Crosshatch IS the shading.
Do not use color.

prompts.title: Die-cut sticker illustration
prompts.when: You want a die-cut sticker design with thick outline, simple shape, and vibrant color.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a sticker designer producing a die-cut vinyl sticker.</role>
<task>Design a die-cut sticker illustration of {{subject_concept}}.</task>
<outline>Thick uniform white outline around the entire sticker shape, 8 to 12pt at 1024px. Defines the die-cut edge.</outline>
<shape>One contiguous silhouette. The die-cut follows the outline of the subject, not a rectangle.</shape>
<style>Flat vibrant colors, simple cel-shading, no gradients. Friendly cartoon proportions.</style>
<palette>{{n}} saturated colors. One accent. No muted tones.</palette>
<pose>The subject is doing {{action}} with personality. Big expression.</pose>
<background>Transparent or solid pastel background. No scenery.</background>
<output>Square 1024x1024 PNG with transparent background, or with pastel backdrop.</output>
<negative>no photoreal, no gradient, no thin outline, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Sticker convention: thick uniform outline + flat color + contiguous silhouette + no background is the die-cut standard.
StickerMule and Redbubble die-cut sticker conventions.
antiPatterns: Do not add a background scene.
Do not use a thin outline. Die-cut needs a thick edge.

prompts.title: Mid-century modern poster
prompts.when: You want a mid-century modern poster illustration in the Saul Bass / Charley Harper / Mary Blair tradition.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a mid-century modern illustrator in the tradition of Saul Bass, Charley Harper, and Mary Blair.</role>
<task>Render a mid-century modern poster illustration of {{subject}}.</task>
<style>Flat geometric shapes, simplified forms, mid-century color palette. Negative space intentional. Asymmetric composition.</style>
<palette>Mid-century palette: muted primary, mustard yellow, avocado green, burnt orange, teal, cream. Warm earthy tones with one bright accent.</palette>
<composition>Asymmetric balance, dynamic angle, graphic shapes overlap. Strong silhouettes.</composition>
<subject>{{subject_concept}} rendered in simplified geometric form. Often stylized beyond realism.</subject>
<texture>Flat color fields. No gradients. Optional subtle paper grain.</texture>
<output>Vertical 2:3 PNG.</output>
<negative>no photoreal, no modern neon palette, no busy detail, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Mid-century convention: flat geometric shapes + muted primary + asymmetric balance is the genre.
Saul Bass, Charley Harper, and Mary Blair references.
antiPatterns: Do not use modern neon palette.
Do not render photoreal. Simplification is the genre.

prompts.title: Pixar-style 3D character
prompts.when: You want a Pixar-style 3D character render with subsurface scattering, soft cinematic lighting, and stylized proportions.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a Pixar-style character TD working in the modern stylized 3D pipeline.</role>
<task>Render a Pixar-style 3D character of {{character_description}}.</task>
<proportions>Stylized: oversized head, large expressive eyes, simplified but readable hand and foot anatomy. Not realistic human proportions.</proportions>
<skin>Subsurface scattering on ears, nose, fingertips. Soft Lambertian shading with a touch of specular. Visible pore detail at close-up.</skin>
<lighting>Three-point cinematic lighting: warm key from upper-left, cool fill from lower-right, soft rim from behind. No harsh shadows.</lighting>
<render>Path-traced or ray-traced quality. High-quality GI bounce. Optional depth of field with subject sharp.</render>
<style>Modern Pixar feature-film quality. Reference: Soul, Luca, Coco, Turning Red.</style>
<output>1500x2000 PNG, 3:4 vertical.</output>
<negative>no photoreal, no horror, no anime, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Pixar convention: stylized proportions + subsurface scattering + three-point cinematic lighting is the genre signature.
Pixar RenderMan and modern stylized 3D pipeline references.
antiPatterns: Do not render realistic human proportions. Stylized is the genre.
Do not skip the subsurface scattering. It is what makes the skin read as 3D, not plastic.

prompts.title: Unreal Engine cinematic frame
prompts.when: You want an Unreal Engine 5 cinematic frame with real-time path tracing and Nanite-level detail.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a real-time CG artist working in Unreal Engine 5 with Lumen and Nanite.</role>
<task>Render an Unreal Engine 5 cinematic frame of {{scene_description}}.</task>
<engine_features>Real-time path tracing with Lumen global illumination. Nanite virtualized geometry on all static meshes. Megascans-quality materials.</engine_features>
<lighting>Directional sun light from {{angle}} with atmospheric scattering. Sky atmosphere with sun disk. Volumetric fog or clouds for depth.</lighting>
<materials>PBR materials: displacement on stone and wood, anisotropic on metals and hair, subsurface on skin and leaves.</materials>
<composition>{{shot_type}}. Depth of field with subject sharp. Lens effects: chromatic aberration, lens flare if light source in frame, film grain.</composition>
<palette>Color grade: {{grade}}. Tone-mapped with ACES Filmic or AgX. Realistic exposure.</palette>
<output>3840x2160 PNG, 16:9.</output>
<negative>no cartoon, no anime, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Unreal Engine convention: Lumen GI + Nanite geometry + Megascans materials + ACES tone mapping is the modern real-time cinematic stack.
Unreal Engine 5 documentation: https://docs.unrealengine.com and youmind 3D render references.
antiPatterns: Do not skip Lumen / GI. Flat lighting breaks the engine signature.
Do not apply cartoon shading. Realistic is the genre.

prompts.title: Voxel art scene
prompts.when: You want a Minecraft-style voxel art scene built from cubes with simple lighting and clear blocky forms.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a voxel artist working in the Minecraft / MagicaVoxel tradition.</role>
<task>Build a voxel art scene of {{scene_concept}}.</task>
<unit>All geometry built from cubic voxels of equal size. No curved surfaces, no diagonal edges, no smooth shading.</unit>
<palette>Minecraft default or custom palette: {{palette}}. Each block face is a single flat color. Per-face shading implied by lighting.</palette>
<lighting>Single directional light from upper-left. Cast shadows on block faces. No global illumination. Ambient occlusion only in crevices.</lighting>
<style>Blocky and recognizable. Reference: Minecraft dioramas, MagicaVoxel renders, Crossy Road.</style>
<composition>Scene centered on a small platform or floating island. Surrounded by simple sky background.</composition>
<output>Square 1:1 PNG or landscape 16:9 PNG.</output>
<negative>no smooth surfaces, no curves, no photoreal, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Voxel convention: cubic-only geometry + flat face shading + simple directional lighting is the genre.
Youmind voxel / pixel-art references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/pixel-art.
antiPatterns: Do not use curved surfaces. Voxels are cubes.
Do not add smooth shading.

prompts.title: Low-poly geometric scene
prompts.when: You want a low-poly 3D scene with visible facets, flat shading, and clean geometric forms.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a low-poly 3D artist working with flat-shaded faceted geometry.</role>
<task>Render a low-poly scene of {{scene_concept}}.</task>
<geometry>Visible facets and triangulated polygons. Each face flat-shaded with a single color or simple gradient. No smooth shading, no subdivision surfaces.</geometry>
<palette>Limited to {{n}} colors total. One color per material, not per face. Slight color variation across faces implies lighting direction.</palette>
<lighting>Single directional light. Visible faceted shadows. Subtle ambient in shadow areas.</lighting>
<style>Clean modern low-poly. Reference: Mobile game art, Behance low-poly scenes, Alto's Odyssey aesthetic.</style>
<composition>Subject or scene centered. Clean background gradient. Optional subtle fog for depth.</composition>
<output>Square 1:1 or landscape 16:9 PNG.</output>
<negative>no smooth shading, no photoreal, no high-poly detail, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Low-poly convention: visible facets + flat shading + limited palette is the genre signature.
Youmind 3D render / low-poly references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/3d-render.
antiPatterns: Do not apply smooth subdivision. The facets are the genre.
Do not use high-poly detail.

prompts.title: 3D render passes for compositing
prompts.when: You want a 3D render with separate passes (beauty, depth, normal, AO) for post-production compositing.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a 3D compositor working with AOV (Arbitrary Output Variable) render passes.</role>
<task>Render a 3D scene with labeled render passes for compositing.</task>
<passes>
1. Beauty: full render with all lighting and materials.
2. Diffuse: pure color, no shading.
3. Depth: grayscale ramp from camera-near to camera-far.
4. Normal: RGB-encoded surface normals (X, Y, Z).
5. AO: ambient occlusion grayscale.
6. Specular: highlights only, black background.
7. Shadow: shadow mask only, white background.
</passes>
<layout>Passes arranged in a labeled grid: 2 rows by 4 columns, last cell empty or holds reference grid.</layout>
<label>Each pass labeled in 14pt sans-serif top-left of its cell.</label>
<scene>Single scene: {{scene_description}}. Consistent across all passes.</scene>
<output>3200x1600 PNG, 2:1.</output>
<negative>no watermark, no text other than pass labels.</negative>
notes: Render pass convention: AOV pass separation + consistent scene across passes is the VFX / compositing standard.
VFX compositing pipeline references and youmind 3D render patterns.
antiPatterns: Do not vary the scene between passes. Same scene, different output.
Do not skip the labels. Compositors need to know what is what.

prompts.title: Clay render 3D character
prompts.when: You want a clay-render 3D character with matte material, soft area light, and a handcrafted feel.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a 3D clay artist using ZBrush-style sculpting and rendering.</role>
<task>Render a 3D character in clay style: {{character_description}}.</task>
<material>Matte clay material with subtle subsurface scattering on thin areas (ears, fingers, nose). No specular gloss, no metalness. Visible micro-surface texture from the sculpting process.</material>
<lighting>Soft area light from upper-left at 30 degrees. Soft fill from lower-right. Even ambient occlusion in crevices. No hard rim.</lighting>
<color>Single clay color throughout the character: {{clay_color: terracotta | porcelain | stone gray | mint | cream}}. No painted details.</color>
<imperfection>Subtle handcrafted marks: fingerprints, tool strokes, slight asymmetry. Not a perfect CGI render.</imperfection>
<background>Solid pastel {{bg_color}}.</background>
<output>1500x2000 PNG, 3:4 vertical.</output>
<negative>no photoreal, no glossy plastic, no anime, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Clay render convention: matte subsurface material + soft area light + subtle handcrafted imperfection is the genre.
ZBrush and Substance Painter clay-style rendering conventions.
antiPatterns: Do not apply glossy specular. Matte is the genre.
Do not render perfect CGI cleanliness. Handcrafted marks are the point.

prompts.title: Studio product 3D render
prompts.when: You want a high-end product 3D render on a clean studio background with HDR lighting.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a product 3D artist working with KeyShot or similar physically-based renderer.</role>
<task>Render a 3D product hero of {{product_name}} on a clean studio background.</task>
<studio>Seamless cyclorama background in {{bg_color}}. Subtle floor reflection if glossy material. Soft natural shadow under the product.</studio>
<lighting>HDRI environment for reflections. Two area lights for key and fill. Material-correct specular highlights on metal, glass, plastic, leather.</lighting>
<material>Realistic PBR material: {{material: brushed aluminum | anodized metal | glossy plastic | matte rubber | glass | leather | ceramic}}. Visible material detail.</material>
<camera>Eye-level, 85mm equivalent, f/8 for full product sharpness. Centered or rule-of-thirds framing.</camera>
<quality>Path-traced rendering. No fireflies, no noise. Production-grade quality.</quality>
<output>2048x2048 PNG, square.</output>
<negative>no watermark, no busy background, no other objects, no text.</negative>
notes: Studio product render convention: HDRI environment + PBR materials + 85mm f/8 is the commercial rendering standard.
KeyShot and Blender Cycles product rendering conventions.
antiPatterns: Do not use a busy HDRI. Subtle is the genre.
Do not exceed f/8 if the whole product must be sharp.

prompts.title: 3D character with facial expression
prompts.when: You want a 3D character render focused on a specific facial expression for a game cinematic or animation keyframe.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a 3D character facial animator working on a keyframe expression.</role>
<task>Render a 3D character expressing {{expression: joy | sorrow | anger | surprise | fear | disgust | determination | tenderness}}.</task>
<expression_anatomy>
- Joy: raised cheeks, lip corners up, eyes narrowed, brows relaxed.
- Sorrow: lip corners down, inner brows raised, eyes slightly closed.
- Anger: brows down and together, lips tight, eyes intense.
- Surprise: brows up, eyes wide, jaw dropped, lips parted.
- Fear: brows up and together, eyes wide, lips stretched horizontally.
- Disgust: upper lip raised, nose wrinkled, cheeks raised.
- Determination: brows down, jaw set, lips pressed, eyes focused.
- Tenderness: soft eyes, slight smile, relaxed brows.
</expression_anatomy>
<render>Photoreal or stylized PBR character. Subtle subsurface on skin. Visible micro-expression detail in forehead, around eyes, around mouth.</render>
<lighting>Three-point cinematic lighting. Light direction chosen to highlight the expression shape.</lighting>
<output>1500x1500 PNG, square, tight on the face.</output>
<negative>no anime, no watermark, no exaggerated cartoon expressions unless requested.</negative>
notes: Facial expression anatomy follows Paul Ekman's FACS (Facial Action Coding System) conventions adapted for 3D animation.
Youmind character animation references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/character.
antiPatterns: Do not mix expressions. Pick one and commit.
Do not exaggerate beyond the named expression's natural range.

prompts.title: Architectural exterior 3D render
prompts.when: You want an architectural exterior 3D render for a real-estate or development presentation.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an architectural visualization artist working with V-Ray or Corona Renderer.</role>
<task>Render an architectural exterior 3D visualization of {{building_concept}}.</task>
<composition>Hero building in the foreground, supporting context (landscape, neighboring buildings, sky) for scale. Camera at human eye level or slightly below for grandeur.</composition>
<lighting>{{time_of_day: golden hour | overcast midday | dusk blue hour | night with interior glow}}. Visible shadow direction and length.</lighting>
<materials>PBR materials: {{materials: concrete | wood cladding | metal panels | glass curtain wall | brick | stone}}. Material variation must read clearly.</materials>
<environment>Landscape elements: trees, paths, vehicles, people for scale. All rendered with realistic quality.</environment>
<quality>Path-traced rendering. No fireflies. High-quality post-processing: contrast, color grade, subtle bloom.</quality>
<output>3840x2160 PNG, 16:9.</output>
<negative>no cartoon, no anime, no watermark, no untextured geometry.</negative>
notes: Architectural visualization convention: hero building + context for scale + named lighting condition + PBR materials is the industry standard.
V-Ray and Corona Renderer architectural conventions.
antiPatterns: Do not render untextured geometry. PBR materials are required.
Do not skip the context. Hero in isolation reads as a model, not a place.

<wardrobe>Cultural-appropriate clothing, jewelry, accessories for the race. Mix of leather, metal, fabric, furs.</wardrobe>
<lighting>Dramatic motivated light: {{lighting}}. Visible subsurface on thin ears, horns, scales.</lighting>
<output>Vertical 3:4 PNG.</output>
<negative>no human-only anatomy, no anime, no chibi, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Fantasy race convention: anatomical accuracy to the race's defining features is what separates a fantasy portrait from a human in makeup.
Wizards of the Coast and Paizo concept art references.
antiPatterns: Do not render a fantasy race as a human in cosplay.
Do not omit the race's defining features.

prompts.title: Elder portrait with character

<expression>{{expression: quiet wisdom | warm smile | thoughtful gaze | gentle laugh | dignified reserve}}.</expression>
<eyes>Eyes are the focal point. Detailed iris, visible moisture, character lines around them (crow's feet, brow furrows).</eyes>
<background>Simple, not distracting. {{background_choice: warm interior | natural outdoor | solid color | soft gradient}}.</background>
<output>Vertical 3:4 PNG.</output>
<negative>no plastic smoothing, no youth filter, no harsh lighting, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Elder portrait convention: visible character + dignified expression + lighting that respects texture is the genre.
Youmind elder portrait references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/portrait-selfie.
antiPatterns: Do not smooth out the wrinkles. They are the story.

prompts.title: Child portrait playful

prompts.title: Original character reference sheet

<identity>{{character_concept}}. Physical traits, hair, eye color, signature features. All views consistent.</identity>
<layout>White background. Top row: front, three-quarter, side turnaround. Middle row: 3 to 4 expression studies. Bottom row: detail callouts (one accessory, one clothing detail) and color swatches.</layout>
<style>{{style: anime | cartoon | semi-realistic | 3D stylized}}. Consistent line weight and rendering across the sheet.</style>
<color>5 to 7 color swatches displayed as small filled rectangles with hex codes or names.</color>
<output>Horizontal 16:9 PNG or 3:2 sheet.</output>
<negative>no watermark, no scenery, no other characters, no text other than hex codes.</negative>

Youmind character sheet patterns: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/character.
antiPatterns: Do not vary proportions between views.

prompts.title: Brand mascot character design

prompts.prompt:

Youmind mascot references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/character.

prompts.title: Fan art tribute

prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a fan artist creating a tribute piece featuring {{ip_character}} from {{ip_name}}.</role>
<task>Render {{ip_character}} in a new scene or style, in tribute to the source IP.</task>
<character_accuracy>Preserve the character's defining features: {{defining_features}}. Stay recognizable.</character_accuracy>
<new_setting>{{new_setting}}. The character in a context they have not been depicted in, while remaining in-genre for the IP.</new_setting>
<style>{{style: cel-shaded anime | painterly oil | Pixar 3D | watercolor | ukiyo-e | art nouveau}}. Pick a style that honors but reinvents.</style>
<palette>{{palette}}. Coherent with the style chosen.</palette>
<tone>Tribute, not parody. Respectful of the source IP.</tone>
<output>Vertical 2:3 or square 1:1 PNG.</output>
<negative>no watermark, no disparaging imagery, no IP-protected brand text, no signature in frame.</negative>
notes: Fan-art convention: defining features preserved + new setting + new style is the tribute structure.
Note: avoid generating specific copyrighted characters with their exact likeness in commercial contexts; this prompt is for personal fan-art only.
antiPatterns: Do not alter defining features. The character must be recognizable.
Do not generate IP-protected logos or signature brand text.

prompts.title: IP character style recreation

<new_style>{{new_style: Tim Burton stop-motion | 90s anime | Pixar 3D | ukiyo-e | cyberpunk 2077 | Ghibli watercolor | noir ink wash}}.</new_style>
<adaptation>Adapt costume details to suit the new style's visual vocabulary. Do not slavishly copy the original rendering.</adaptation>
<background>{{background}} that matches the new style.</background>
<output>Vertical 2:3 PNG.</output>
<negative>no watermark, no IP-protected brand text in frame, no signature.</negative>
notes: Style recreation convention: preserve defining features + adapt costume to new style's visual vocabulary.
For personal fan-art exploration only. Commercial use of recreated IP characters is not advised.
antiPatterns: Do not change defining features.
Do not copy the source rendering exactly; that defeats the purpose.

prompts.title: Dynamic character action pose

<action>{{action: leaping | casting | sword strike | running | falling | battle stance}}. Weight and motion implied.</action>

<style>{{style: anime key visual | concept art oil | comic book splash | Pixar cinematic | painterly game art}}.</style>
<output>Vertical 2:3 PNG for poster, or 16:9 for horizontal splash.</output>
<negative>no static standing pose, no watermark, no flat lighting.</negative>

Youmind action / character references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/character.
antiPatterns: Do not pose statically. Motion is the genre.
Do not flatten the lighting.

prompts.title: Character emotional close-up
prompts.when: You want a tight close-up character portrait capturing a specific emotion for a story beat.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a character concept artist capturing a specific emotion beat.</role>
<task>Render a tight close-up of {{character_description}} expressing {{emotion}}.</task>
<emotion_anatomy>
- Joy: raised cheeks, lip corners up, eyes narrowed with crinkles.
- Sorrow: inner brows raised, lip corners down, eyes slightly closed.
- Anger: brows down together, lips tight, eyes intense.
- Surprise: brows up, eyes wide, jaw dropped.
- Fear: brows up together, eyes wide, lips stretched horizontally.
- Disgust: upper lip raised, nose wrinkled.
- Determination: jaw set, brows down, lips pressed, eyes focused.
- Tenderness: soft eyes, slight smile, relaxed brows.
</emotion_anatomy>
<crop>Tight close-up: forehead to chin, eyes at upper-third line. Subject fills the frame.</crop>
<lighting>Motivated dramatic light: {{lighting}}. Light shape reveals emotion contours.</lighting>
<output>Square 1:1 or vertical 2:3 PNG.</output>
<negative>no neutral expression, no watermark, no busy background.</negative>
notes: Emotional close-up convention: anatomy-driven expression + tight crop + motivated light is the genre.
Paul Ekman FACS conventions and youmind character references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/character.
antiPatterns: Do not mix two emotions. Pick one.
Do not crop too tight to lose the eye line.

prompts.title: Character group lineup

<individual_diversity>Each character has a distinct silhouette and palette. No two characters share the same color block.</individual_diversity>
<lighting>Consistent lighting across all characters. Single light source so all faces share the same direction.</lighting>
<pose>Each character in a pose that conveys their role: {{pose_set}}.</pose>
<background>Consistent background. {{background}}. Subtle visual elements that imply the world.</background>
<output>Horizontal 3:1 PNG for poster, or vertical 2:3 for stacked lineup.</output>
<negative>no watermark, no characters that look identical except for color, no text overlay.</negative>
notes: Character lineup convention: distinct silhouette per character + consistent lighting + role-conveying poses is the genre.
Youmind character lineup references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/character.
antiPatterns: Do not let silhouettes blend together.
Do not vary the lighting direction across characters.

prompts.title: Character costume variant

Youmind character design references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/character.
antiPatterns: Do not change facial features between panels.

prompts.title: Character with vehicle or mount
prompts.when: You want a character paired with their signature vehicle or mount in a unified composition.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a concept designer pairing a character with a vehicle or mount.</role>
<task>Render {{character_description}} alongside their {{vehicle_or_mount_concept}}.</task>
<unit>The character and vehicle read as a unit: complementary colors, scale, silhouette. The mount feels chosen by the character.</unit>
<composition>Subject and vehicle share the frame. {{composition: side by side | character mounting | character riding | standing before | character piloting}}. Action implied.</composition>
<scale>Scale relationship clear: mount large relative to character, vehicle proportional. Not ambiguous.</scale>
<lighting>Unified lighting across both. Same light source.</lighting>
<background>{{background}} that identifies the world: hangar, stable, garage, tarmac, forest, battlefield.</background>
<output>Horizontal 16:9 or vertical 2:3 PNG.</output>
<negative>no watermark, no floating scale, no separate lighting on subject and mount.</negative>
notes: Character-vehicle pairing convention: complementary color + scale + unified lighting makes the unit read as one composition.
Youmind character + vehicle references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/character.
antiPatterns: Do not light subject and vehicle separately.
Do not place them on different planes without depth.

prompts.title: Character in environment portrait

prompts.title: Luxury goods cinematic render

prompts.title: Food product hero macro

prompts.title: Watch or timepiece macro

<expression>One clear expression: {{expression: alert | aggressive | regal | curious | sorrowful}}.</expression>

prompts.title: Pet action candid

<light>{{light: golden hour | overcast soft | midday action | backlit sunset}}.</light>
<expression>Peak emotion: {{expression: joyful run | focused hunt | mid-catch | pure freedom}}.</expression>

prompts.title: Brutalist architecture hero

<output>2400x1600 PNG, 3:2.</output>
<negative>no watermark, no decorative elements, no human in frame, no text overlay.</negative>
notes: Brutalist convention: raw concrete as protagonist + geometric mass + shadow play is the genre.
Youmind architectural references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/photography.

prompts.title: Art deco facade hero
prompts.when: You want an art deco facade with ornamentation, geometric patterns, and glamour.

<role>You are an architectural photographer in the Chrysler Building / Empire State tradition.</role>

<facade>Visible art deco vocabulary: stepped massing, sunburst motifs, zigzags, chevrons, vertical fluting, metal spandrels, ornamental metalwork.</facade>
<palette>Warm cream, gold, deep green or blue, polished metal accents. Period-appropriate color.</palette>

Youmind art deco architectural references.

prompts.title: Modern minimal architecture
prompts.when: You want a modern minimal architecture hero shot emphasizing clean lines, open space, and material honesty.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an architectural photographer in the Tadao Ando / John Pawson tradition.</role>

<material>Honest materials: {{materials: concrete | wood | glass | steel | stone}}. Visible texture, no decoration.</material>
<geometry>Clean lines, simple volumes, clear plan. Restraint and proportion over ornament.</geometry>
<light>Natural light as protagonist: {{light: raking sun | skylight | clerestory | courtyard bounce}}. Shadow defines space.</light>
<composition>Symmetric or asymmetric with intentional balance. Camera placed to honor the geometry. Often frontal or three-quarter.</composition>
<palette>Muted natural palette: warm gray, off-white, wood tone. No bright accent unless part of the architecture.</palette>
<output>2400x1600 PNG, 3:2.</output>
<negative>no watermark, no ornament, no people (unless scale figures), no busy decoration.</negative>
notes: Modern minimal convention: honest materials + clean geometry + natural light as protagonist is the genre.
Tadao Ando and John Pawson references.
antiPatterns: Do not add ornament. Restraint is the genre.
Do not flatten the lighting.

prompts.title: Traditional Japanese architecture
prompts.when: You want a traditional Japanese architecture hero shot (temple, teahouse, garden) with material and atmosphere focus.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an architectural photographer in the tradition of Japanese architectural documentation.</role>
<task>Render a hero shot of {{traditional_japanese_structure}}.</task>
<structure>{{structure: temple | teahouse | shrine | garden pavilion | wooden bridge}}. Period-accurate Japanese construction.</structure>
<material>Natural wood: {{wood: cedar | cypress | pine | zelkova}}. Visible grain, weathering, joinery. Paper screens, tatami, stone path.</material>

<light>{{light: soft overcast | dappled through trees | dawn mist | golden hour late | moonlit}}. Soft, contemplative.</light>
<palette>Natural wood tones, soft greens, stone gray, muted earth. Atmospheric, restrained.</palette>
<composition>Honor Japanese compositional principles: asymmetry, ma (negative space), foreground-midground-background depth. Often low angle.</composition>
<output>2400x1600 PNG, 3:2.</output>
<negative>no watermark, no modern elements, no bright neon, no text overlay.</negative>

Youmind Japanese architectural references.
antiPatterns: Do not add bright neon or modern elements.

prompts.title: Futuristic concept architecture

<palette>{{palette: cool blue + teal + chrome | warm orange + magenta + chrome | monochrome with one accent}}. Sci-fi feel.</palette>

prompts.title: Photo to illustration style transfer
prompts.when: You have a photo and want it rendered in an illustration style (vector, watercolor, gouache, etc.).
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a style-transfer specialist converting a photographic input into an illustration.</role>
<task>Convert the input photo {{input_description}} into an illustration in the style {{target_style}}.</task>
<preserve>Preserve: subject identity, pose, composition, color identity of key elements. These are anchored.</preserve>
<convert>Convert: rendering technique, surface texture, line treatment, shading model, color application to {{target_style}}.</convert>
<style>{{target_style: cel-shaded anime | flat vector | watercolor | gouache | risograph | ukiyo-e | ink-and-wash | pencil sketch | storybook | Pixar 3D}}.</style>
<palette>Preserve dominant colors of the original. Adjust saturation and contrast to suit the new medium.</palette>
<negative>do not change the subject's pose, do not invent new elements, do not crop tighter than the original, do not add text, do not add watermark.</negative>
<output>Match original dimensions or scale to {{target_dimensions}}.</output>
notes: Style transfer convention: explicit preserve / convert split is the rule. Without it, the model invents changes you did not ask for.
GPT Image and Nano Banana Pro support native style transfer with strong identity preservation.
Youmind style-transfer references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/sketch-line-art.
antiPatterns: Do not change the subject's pose or composition. Only the rendering changes.
Do not invent new elements that were not in the original.

prompts.title: Photo to anime style transfer
prompts.when: You have a photo and want it rendered in anime style while preserving facial identity.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an anime style-transfer specialist.</role>
<task>Convert the input photo {{input_description}} into an anime-style illustration.</task>
<identity_preservation>Preserve facial identity: face shape, eye spacing, nose, mouth, hairline, hair color. Identity must be recognizable.</identity_preservation>
<anime_treatment>
- Eyes enlarged to anime proportion (1.5x to 2x photo eye size).
- Skin smoothed, cel-shaded.
- Hair rendered in stylized anime strands.
- Outline rendering chosen: {{outline: thin line | thick line | no outline}}.
</anime_treatment>
<style>{{style: shoujo | shounen | slice-of-life | 90s aesthetic | modern digital | watercolor anime}}.</style>
<palette>Preserve dominant colors of the photo. Skin tone in anime range.</palette>
<output>Match original dimensions or scale to {{target_dimensions}}.</output>
<negative>do not change facial structure, do not change hair color, do not add new accessories, do not add text, do not add watermark.</negative>
notes: Anime transfer convention: identity preservation + proportional anime eye enlargement + cel-shading is the genre.
Nano Banana Pro and GPT Image are strong at identity-preserving style transfer.
antiPatterns: Do not change the face structure. Identity preservation is the point.
Do not skip the outline treatment. Anime convention requires it.

prompts.title: Photo to oil painting transfer
prompts.when: You have a photo and want it rendered as a classical oil painting with visible brushwork.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an oil-painting style-transfer specialist in the classical European tradition.</role>
<task>Convert the input photo {{input_description}} into an oil painting.</task>
<brushwork>Visible brushstrokes: thicker impasto on highlights, smoother blending on mid-tones, loose strokes on edges and background.</brushwork>
<palette>Limited pigment palette: {{palette: 6 pigments including ivory black, lead white, yellow ochre, cadmium red, ultramarine, burnt sienna}}. Skin in warm mid-tones.</palette>
<style>{{school: impressionist | post-impressionist | baroque | flemish | pre-raphaelite | modern expressionist}}.</style>
<canvas>Visible canvas weave texture, faint craquelure on edges.</canvas>
<lighting>Maintain the photo's lighting direction. Convert specular highlights into impasto dabs.</lighting>
<output>Match original dimensions or scale to {{target_dimensions}}.</output>
<negative>do not change the subject, do not skip the canvas texture, do not over-smooth the brushwork, do not add text, do not add watermark.</negative>
notes: Oil painting transfer convention: visible impasto + named school + limited pigment palette is the genre.
Youmind oil painting references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/oil-painting.
antiPatterns: Do not smooth the brushwork. Visible brushstrokes are the genre.
Do not exceed the pigment count. Classical palettes are restricted.

prompts.title: Photo to pencil sketch transfer
prompts.when: You have a photo and want it rendered as a graphite pencil sketch with hatching shading.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a pencil-sketch style-transfer specialist.</role>
<task>Convert the input photo {{input_description}} into a graphite pencil sketch.</task>
<medium>Graphite pencil on cream paper. Visible graphite texture. No color, no ink, no wash.</medium>
<shading>Hatching and crosshatching. Density indicates shadow depth. Pure white paper reads as highlight. Pure graphite as deep shadow.</shading>
<line>Varied line weight. Thicker contours on subject outline, finer lines for interior detail and shading. Hand-drawn quality.</line>
<preserve>Subject identity, pose, composition, lighting direction.</preserve>
<paper>Visible cream paper texture. Subtle smudges and eraser marks suggest real sketching.</paper>
<output>Match original dimensions or scale to {{target_dimensions}}.</output>
<negative>do not add color, do not smooth to a digital clean look, do not add text, do not add watermark.</negative>
notes: Pencil sketch transfer convention: graphite + hatching + hand-drawn imperfection is the genre.
Youmind sketch / line art references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/sketch-line-art.
antiPatterns: Do not add color. Pencil is monochrome.
Do not skip the hand-drawn imperfection.

prompts.title: Photo to comic book transfer
prompts.when: You have a photo and want it rendered as a comic-book panel with bold outlines and halftone.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a comic-book style-transfer specialist.</role>
<task>Convert the input photo {{input_description}} into a comic-book panel.</task>
<outline>Bold black outlines around subjects, contours, and major shapes. Line weight varies for emphasis.</outline>
<shading>Halftone dot patterns for mid-tones. Crosshatching for shadows. Pure white for highlights. Pure black for deepest shadows.</shading>
<style>{{comic_style: marvel | DC | manga | european ligne claire | indie alternative | retro 60s marvel}}.</style>
<color>Optional limited color palette: {{n}} colors. Flat color fills with hard edges. No gradients.</color>
<preserve>Subject identity, pose, composition, lighting direction.</preserve>
<panel>Optional comic-panel border with thin black frame.</panel>
<output>Match original dimensions or scale to {{target_dimensions}}.</output>
<negative>do not add text bubbles, do not add captions, do not change pose, do not add watermark.</negative>
notes: Comic transfer convention: bold outlines + halftone + flat color is the genre.
Youmind comic / graphic novel references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/comic-graphic-novel.
antiPatterns: Do not add text bubbles or captions. Panel only.
Do not add gradients. Comic colors are flat.

prompts.title: Photo to 3D clay render transfer
prompts.when: You have a photo and want it rendered as a 3D clay sculpture with soft subsurface material.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a 3D clay style-transfer specialist.</role>
<task>Convert the input photo {{input_description}} into a 3D clay render.</task>
<material>Matte clay material with subtle subsurface scattering on thin areas (ears, fingers, nose). No specular gloss, no metalness.</material>
<lighting>Soft area light from upper left at 30 degrees. Soft fill from lower right. Even ambient occlusion in crevices. No hard rim.</lighting>
<color>Single clay color throughout: {{clay_color: terracotta | porcelain | stone gray | mint | cream}}. No painted details.</color>
<imperfection>Subtle handcrafted marks: fingerprints, tool strokes, slight asymmetry. Not perfect CGI.</imperfection>
<background>Solid pastel {{bg_color}}.</background>
<preserve>Subject identity, pose, composition.</preserve>
<output>Match original dimensions or scale to {{target_dimensions}}.</output>
<negative>do not apply glossy plastic, do not add color beyond the clay color, do not add text, do not add watermark.</negative>
notes: Clay render transfer convention: matte subsurface + soft area light + handcrafted imperfection is the genre.
Youmind 3D render / clay references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/3d-render.
antiPatterns: Do not apply glossy specular. Matte is the genre.
Do not add color beyond the clay color.

prompts.title: Photo to cyberpunk transfer
prompts.when: You have a photo and want it re-rendered in cyberpunk style with neon and rain.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a cyberpunk style-transfer specialist in the Blade Runner 2049 tradition.</role>
<task>Convert the input photo {{input_description}} into a cyberpunk scene.</task>
<lighting>Add dual neon rim lights: {{neon_color_1}} from left, {{neon_color_2}} from right. Reduce ambient. Increase contrast.</lighting>
<atmosphere>Add visible atmospheric effects: rain, fog, mist, or smoke. Light catches the particles.</atmosphere>
<palette>Desaturate natural colors, push neon primaries. Teal shadows, magenta highlights, orange accents. Cool dominant.</palette>
<detail>Add subtle tech details: holographic UI elements in background, LED strip on clothing, glowing seam on a device. Do not invent new subjects.</detail>
<preserve>Subject identity, pose, composition.</preserve>
<output>Match original dimensions or scale to {{target_dimensions}}.</output>
<negative>do not change the subject, do not add full costume change, do not add text, do not add watermark.</negative>
notes: Cyberpunk transfer convention: dual neon rim + atmospheric particle + tech detail is the genre.
Youmind cyberpunk / sci-fi references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/cyberpunk-sci-fi.
antiPatterns: Do not change the subject's pose.
Do not over-add tech details. Subtle is the genre.

prompts.title: Photo to watercolor transfer
prompts.when: You have a photo and want it rendered in soft watercolor with visible pigment behavior.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a watercolor style-transfer specialist.</role>
<task>Convert the input photo {{input_description}} into a watercolor painting.</task>
<pigment>Visible watercolor behavior: wet bleeds, pigment pooling at edges, controlled gradients, paper texture showing through washes.</pigment>
<palette>Named watercolor pigments: {{palette: indigo, burnt sienna, ochre, sap green, payne's gray, cadmium red}}. Subject colors preserved as a subset of these.</palette>
<edges>Soft edges with controlled bleeds. Optional darker contour line in selective ink. No hard digital edges.</edges>
<paper>Visible cream or white watercolor paper texture.</paper>
<preserve>Subject identity, pose, composition, lighting direction.</preserve>
<output>Match original dimensions or scale to {{target_dimensions}}.</output>
<negative>do not add digital smoothness, do not add color beyond the named pigments, do not add text, do not add watermark.</negative>
notes: Watercolor transfer convention: visible pigment behavior + named pigments + paper texture is the genre.
Youmind watercolor references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/watercolor.
antiPatterns: Do not request digital smoothness.
Do not exceed the named pigment palette.

prompts.title: Photo to mosaic tile transfer
prompts.when: You have a photo and want it rendered as a mosaic of small tiles or pixel cells.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a mosaic style-transfer specialist.</role>
<task>Convert the input photo {{input_description}} into a mosaic.</task>
<tiles>Tile shape: {{shape: square | hexagonal | circular | triangular}}. Tile size: {{size: small | medium | large}} relative to canvas. Consistent size and shape.</tiles>
<grout>Visible grout lines between tiles. Grout color: {{grout_color}}. Grout width: thin and consistent.</grout>
<color>Each tile is a single flat color sampled from the photo's local color. Per-tile color variation, not gradient.</color>
<style>{{mosaic_style: roman | byzantine | modern art | trencadis | pixel art | pointillism}}.</style>
<preserve>Subject identity, composition, lighting direction.</preserve>
<output>Match original dimensions or scale to {{target_dimensions}}.</output>
<negative>do not blend tile colors, do not remove grout, do not add text, do not add watermark.</negative>
notes: Mosaic transfer convention: consistent tile shape + visible grout + per-tile flat color is the genre.
Youmind mosaic / pixel-art references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/pixel-art.
antiPatterns: Do not blend tile colors into gradients.
Do not omit the grout.

prompts.title: Photo to stained glass transfer
prompts.when: You have a photo and want it rendered as a stained-glass window with leading and jewel-tone color.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a stained-glass style-transfer specialist.</role>
<task>Convert the input photo {{input_description}} into a stained-glass window.</task>
<leading>Heavy black leading between every color region. Leading follows the contours of shapes and features.</leading>
<glass>Jewel-tone saturated color: ruby red, sapphire blue, emerald green, amber yellow, deep purple. Limited palette, no muted tones.</glass>
<light>The glass appears lit from behind. Subject areas glow. Background darker. Highlights glow brightest.</light>
<style>{{style: medieval gothic | victorian | art nouveau | frank lloyd wright | modern abstract}}.</style>
<preserve>Subject identity, composition, lighting direction.</preserve>
<output>Match original dimensions or scale to {{target_dimensions}}.</output>
<negative>do not skip the leading, do not use muted colors, do not add text, do not add watermark.</negative>
notes: Stained-glass transfer convention: heavy leading + jewel-tone saturation + backlight glow is the genre.
Youmind illustration / stained-glass references.
antiPatterns: Do not skip the leading. It is the genre.
Do not use muted colors.

prompts.title: Inpaint add an object to a scene
prompts.when: You have an image and want a new object added at a specific location with matching lighting and perspective.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a photo retoucher performing an inpaint add-object operation.</role>
<task>Add the object {{object_description}} to the input image at {{location_in_image}}.</task>
<lighting_match>Match the scene's lighting direction, intensity, and color temperature. New object must look lit by the same source.</lighting_match>
<perspective_match>Match the scene's camera angle and depth. New object obeys the same perspective.</perspective_match>
<shadow>Cast shadow consistent with the scene's light direction. Ambient occlusion at contact points.</shadow>
<color_match>Object color must fit the scene's palette. If the scene is warm, the object should not look fluorescent.</color_match>
<preserve>Everything outside the add region must remain pixel-identical or near-identical to the input.</preserve>
<negative>do not alter existing subjects, do not change background, do not add text, do not add watermark.</negative>
notes: Inpaint add convention: explicit lighting + perspective + shadow match is the rule. Without it, the object looks pasted on.
GPT Image and Nano Banana Pro have native inpainting capability.
antiPatterns: Do not add the object with a different light direction than the scene.
Do not alter areas outside the add region.

prompts.title: Inpaint remove an object cleanly
prompts.when: You have an image and want an unwanted object removed cleanly, with the background reconstructed.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a photo retoucher performing an inpaint remove-object operation.</role>
<task>Remove the object {{object_to_remove}} from the input image at {{location_in_image}}.</task>
<background_reconstruction>The area behind the removed object must be reconstructed from the surrounding context. Match texture, pattern, lighting, color, and noise.</background_reconstruction>
<edges>No visible edge of the original object. No halo. No blurred blob. No smear.</edges>
<preserve>Everything outside the remove region must remain pixel-identical.</preserve>
<after_state>The result should look as if the object was never present.</after_state>
<negative>do not alter unrelated areas, do not change color balance globally, do not add text, do not add watermark.</negative>
notes: Inpaint remove convention: clean background reconstruction with no visible artifact is the goal.
GPT Image and Nano Banana Pro have strong native remove capabilities.
antiPatterns: Do not leave a halo or smudge where the object was.
Do not change unrelated areas.

prompts.title: Recolor a specific object
prompts.when: You have an image and want to change the color of one specific object while keeping everything else identical.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a photo retoucher performing a precise color-swap.</role>
<task>Change the color of {{object_to_recolor}} in the input image from {{old_color}} to {{new_color}}.</task>
<scope>Only the named object changes color. Every other element in the image stays pixel-identical.</scope>
<material_preservation>Preserve the object's material: matte, gloss, metallic, translucent, textured. New color must respect the material.</material_preservation>
<lighting_preservation>Highlights, shadows, ambient occlusion, reflections on the object all remain consistent with the new color.</lighting_preservation>
<negative>do not change other objects, do not change global color grading, do not introduce new elements, do not add text, do not add watermark.</negative>
notes: Recolor convention: scope-narrow + material-preserve is the rule. Global color shifts leak.
GPT Image and Nano Banana Pro support precise object recolor.
antiPatterns: Do not recolor other objects by accident.
Do not change the material. Only the hue.

prompts.title: Replace background cleanly
prompts.when: You have an image and want the background replaced with a new environment while keeping the subject intact.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a photo retoucher performing a background replacement.</role>
<task>Replace the background of the input image with {{new_background_description}}.</task>
<subject_mask>Mask the subject precisely. No bleeding of old background onto subject edges. No halo of new background into subject.</subject_mask>
<edge_quality>Hair, fur, transparent or semi-transparent edges must be preserved cleanly. No hard mask line.</edge_quality>
<lighting_match>New background must have a lighting direction and color temperature consistent with the subject's lighting. Subject's cast shadow on the new ground is preserved.</lighting_match>
<perspective_match>New background must be at a depth and angle consistent with the subject's camera.</perspective_match>
<preserve>Subject identity, pose, and detail remain pixel-identical.</preserve>
<negative>do not alter the subject, do not introduce new subjects, do not add text, do not add watermark.</negative>
notes: Background replace convention: precise mask + edge preservation + lighting match is the rule.
GPT Image and Nano Banana Pro support native background replacement.
antiPatterns: Do not introduce halo at the subject mask edge.
Do not change the subject.

prompts.title: Relight the scene
prompts.when: You have an image and want the lighting changed (direction, time of day, color temperature) while keeping subjects intact.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a photo retoucher performing a relight operation.</role>
<task>Relight the input image with {{new_lighting_setup}}.</task>
<lighting_change>{{new_lighting_setup: golden hour from left | blue hour overcast | single hard key from upper right | overhead fluorescent | neon from below | candle from below}}.</lighting_change>
<consistency>All surfaces and subjects must be relit coherently by the new light direction. No object should retain the old lighting.</consistency>
<color_temperature>Color temperature shift: {{kelvin}}. Cast and shadow color shift accordingly.</color_temperature>
<shadow>Cast shadows update to the new light direction. Shadow softness matches the new light size.</shadow>
<preserve>Subject identity, pose, composition, color identity of objects remain.</preserve>
<negative>do not introduce new objects, do not change the camera angle, do not add text, do not add watermark.</negative>
notes: Relight convention: coherent across all surfaces + correct cast shadow direction + color temperature shift is the rule.
GPT Image and Nano Banana Pro support relight operations.
antiPatterns: Do not leave any object lit by the old direction.
Do not change the camera angle.

prompts.title: Restore old photograph
prompts.when: You have a damaged or faded old photo and want it restored with face detail recovered.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a photo restoration specialist.</role>
<task>Restore the input damaged photograph {{input_description}}.</task>
<damage_to_repair>Remove: {{damage: scratches | tears | dust spots | water damage | fading | silvering | mold | creases | emulsion damage}}.</damage_to_repair>
<face_recovery>Recover facial detail on subjects. Eyes, nose, mouth must be reconstructed with realistic detail. Do not invent new features.</face_recovery>
<tone_recovery>If faded, recover the original tonal range. Do not push to oversaturated. Stop at plausible period-appropriate color.</tone_recovery>
<preservation>Preserve: composition, subject identity, period-accurate clothing, era-correct hairstyle and makeup. Do not modernize.</preservation>
<output>Same dimensions as input or upscale to {{target_dimensions}}.</output>
<negative>do not invent features, do not modernize the image, do not over-smooth the face, do not add text, do not add watermark.</negative>
notes: Restoration convention: damage removal + face detail recovery + period preservation is the rule.
Photo restoration references and youmind restoration patterns.
antiPatterns: Do not invent facial features that were not in the original.
Do not modernize the period.

prompts.title: Upscale and enhance detail
prompts.when: You have a small or low-detail image and want it upscaled with recovered detail.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an image upscaling and detail enhancement specialist.</role>
<task>Upscale the input image {{input_description}} to {{target_dimensions}} with recovered detail.</task>
<detail_recovery>Recover plausible detail in upscaled regions: skin texture, fabric weave, hair strands, foliage, architectural texture. Match the style and grain of the original.</detail_recovery>
<edge_quality>Sharpen edges without halos. No over-sharpening artifacts.</edge_quality>
<preserve>Composition, colors, lighting, subject identity unchanged. Only resolution and detail change.</preserve>
<anti_artifacts>Avoid: plastic skin, halos at edges, oversaturation, AI-style smoothing.</anti_artifacts>
<output>{{target_dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>do not change composition, do not change subject, do not introduce modern style artifacts, do not add text, do not add watermark.</negative>
notes: Upscale convention: detail recovery matching original grain + edge sharpness without halo is the rule.
Modern upscalers like GPT Image and Real-ESRGAN conventions.
antiPatterns: Do not over-sharpen into halos.
Do not introduce AI-smoothed skin.

prompts.title: Deblur and sharpen focus
prompts.when: You have a blurry or out-of-focus image and want it sharpened back to crisp detail.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an image deblurring specialist.</role>
<task>Deblur the input image {{input_description}} to recover sharp detail.</task>
<blur_type>Original blur: {{blur: motion blur | out-of-focus | gaussian | radial | chromatic | zoom blur}}.</blur_type>
<recovery>Recover sharp edges and detail without introducing halos, ringing, or over-sharpened artifacts.</recovery>
<anti_artifacts>Avoid: sharpening halos, noise amplification, plastic-looking surfaces.</anti_artifacts>
<preserve>Composition, color, lighting, subject identity unchanged.</preserve>
<output>Same dimensions as input or {{target_dimensions}}.</output>
<negative>do not change composition, do not introduce sharpening halos, do not change subject, do not add text, do not add watermark.</negative>
notes: Deblur convention: sharp recovery without halo or ringing is the rule.
Modern deblurring references and youmind edit patterns.
antiPatterns: Do not introduce sharpening halos.
Do not amplify noise into grain.

prompts.title: Outpaint / extend the canvas
prompts.when: You have an image and want the canvas extended in one or more directions to a new aspect ratio.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an image outpainting specialist.</role>
<task>Extend the input image {{input_description}} to {{target_aspect_ratio}} by generating new content at the {{direction: top | bottom | left | right | all}}.</task>
<continuation>The extended area must continue the scene's style, lighting, color, perspective, and texture seamlessly.</continuation>
<edge_blend>The boundary between original and extended area must be invisible. No seam, no color shift, no lighting mismatch.</edge_blend>
<perspective>New content obeys the same perspective and vanishing point as the original.</perspective>
<preserve>The original image area must remain pixel-identical or near-identical.</preserve>
<output>{{target_aspect_ratio}} PNG.</output>
<negative>do not alter the original area, do not introduce a visible seam, do not change style, do not add text, do not add watermark.</negative>
notes: Outpaint convention: seamless continuation + edge blend + perspective match is the rule.
GPT Image and Nano Banana Pro support native outpainting.
antiPatterns: Do not leave a visible seam at the original boundary.
Do not introduce style shift in the extended area.

prompts.title: Remove crowds from landmark photo
prompts.when: You have a landmark or scene photo crowded with tourists and want the crowds removed.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a photo retoucher specializing in crowd removal from landmark photography.</role>
<task>Remove all visible tourists and transient people from the input image {{input_description}}.</task>
<keep>Preserve: {{landmark_or_scene}}, permanent architecture, vegetation, sky, ground, signage. Any fixed elements must remain pixel-identical.</keep>
<remove>All visible tourists, tour groups, passersby, vehicles in motion. Removed cleanly with no ghosting.</remove>
<background_reconstruction>Behind removed people, reconstruct the environment from context: ground, walls, signs, vegetation, sky. Match texture and lighting.</background_reconstruction>
<preservation>The scene must look as if no tourists were ever present. Long exposure feel without the long exposure.</preservation>
<output>Same dimensions or {{target_dimensions}}.</output>
<negative>do not remove permanent people (statues, fixed figures), do not alter the landmark, do not introduce artifacts, do not add text, do not add watermark.</negative>
notes: Crowd removal convention: identify transient vs permanent + reconstruct background cleanly is the rule.
GPT Image and Nano Banana Pro support crowd removal.
antiPatterns: Do not remove statues or permanent figures.
Do not leave ghosts where people were.

prompts.title: Poster with realistic text
prompts.when: You want a poster with literal, readable text rendered by the image model.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a poster designer producing a final image with literal text rendered by the model.</role>
<task>Design a poster for {{event_or_topic}} with literal text rendering.</task>
<headline_text>The literal headline: '{{headline_text}}'. Place this exactly once in the image.</headline_text>
<font>Typeface: {{font_choice: bold condensed sans | elegant serif | display script | modernist geometric | retro display}}. Weight: {{weight}}.</font>
<size>Headline at {{size}} relative to canvas. Subordinate text at 1/3 to 1/4 the size.</size>
<color>Headline in {{headline_color}}, on background {{bg_color}} with sufficient contrast.</color>
<placement>Text at {{placement: top-center | center | bottom-third | rule-of-thirds intersection}}.</placement>
<secondary_text>Optional supporting text: '{{secondary_text}}' in smaller weight below the headline.</secondary_text>
<constraints>
- Render the text exactly as written. Do not paraphrase.
- No extra text, no watermark, no auto-generated labels.
- No garbled or partial letters.
</constraints>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
notes: Text-in-image convention: literal quoted text + explicit font + explicit placement is the rule.
GPT Image 2 and Nano Banana Pro are the strongest at literal text rendering.
OpenAI image prompting guide: https://cookbook.openai.com/examples/multimodal/image-gen-models-prompting-guide.
antiPatterns: Do not paraphrase the text.
Do not request multiple typefaces. One is the rule for legible text.

prompts.title: Billboard with realistic text
prompts.when: You want a photoreal billboard scene with readable text on the billboard.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an outdoor advertising photographer capturing a billboard in situ.</role>
<task>Render a photoreal billboard for {{advertiser_or_message}} mounted at {{location}}.</task>
<billboard_text>The literal billboard text: '{{billboard_text}}'. Place this exactly once on the billboard face.</billboard_text>
<font>Typeface: {{font_choice}}. Bold, readable from a distance.</font>
<billboard_frame>Real billboard structure: {{structure: standard roadside panel | digital LED | transit shelter | building wrap | highway overhead}}.</billboard_frame>
<scene>Scene around the billboard: {{scene: highway with traffic | urban intersection | rural road | stadium exterior | airport}}. Time of day: {{time}}.</scene>
<lighting>{{lighting}} on the scene and on the billboard face. Billboard legible from camera position.</lighting>
<constraints>
- Render billboard text exactly as written.
- Perspective-correct text on the billboard face.
- Billboard face slightly weathered or realistic, not flat vector.
</constraints>
<output>2400x1600 PNG, 3:2.</output>
<negative>no watermark, no garbled letters, no extra text on the billboard.</negative>
notes: Billboard convention: literal quoted text + perspective-correct rendering on the billboard face is the rule.
GPT Image 2 and Nano Banana Pro are the strongest text-rendering models.
antiPatterns: Do not skip the perspective correction.
Do not add garbled or partial letters.

prompts.title: Book cover with title and author
prompts.when: You want a book cover with title and author name rendered literally.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a book cover designer producing a final cover image.</role>
<task>Design a book cover for the book titled '{{title}}' by {{author}}.</task>
<title_text>The literal title: '{{title}}'. Place prominently on the cover.</title_text>
<author_text>The literal author name: '{{author}}'. Placed smaller, typically at the bottom.</author_text>
<font>Title typeface: {{font_choice}}. Author name in {{author_font}}. Match the genre: {{genre}}.</font>
<illustration>Optional supporting illustration: {{illustration_description}}. Title integrated into or above the illustration.</illustration>
<palette>{{palette}}. Mood: {{mood}}.</mood>
<back_cover>Optional back cover image: title and author repeated, plus a short blurb '{{blurb}}'.</back_cover>
<constraints>
- Render title and author name exactly as written.
- No extra invented text.
- No garbled letters.
</constraints>
<output>Front cover: {{dimensions}} PNG. Optional back cover: same dimensions.</output>
<negative>no watermark, no garbled text, no extra UI text.</negative>
notes: Book cover convention: literal title + author + genre-anchored typography is the rule.
Book cover typography references and youmind book / poster patterns.
antiPatterns: Do not invent subtitle text.
Do not use a typeface that fights the genre.

prompts.title: Magazine cover with cover lines
prompts.when: You want a magazine cover with masthead, cover lines, and taglines rendered literally.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a magazine cover designer producing a final cover.</role>
<task>Design a magazine cover for {{magazine_name}} on the cover story {{cover_story}}.</task>
<masthead>The literal magazine name: '{{magazine_name}}'. Place at the top in the established masthead typography.</masthead>
<cover_lines>Cover lines to render exactly: {{list_of_cover_lines}}. Each with hierarchy indicated: primary headline largest, secondary lines smaller.</cover_lines>
