<cover_image>Cover image: {{cover_image_description}}. Subject recognizable at thumbnail.</cover_image>
<palette>{{palette}}. Mood: {{mood}}.</mood>
<issue_label>Issue label: '{{issue_date}}' in small caps near the masthead.</issue_label>
<constraints>
- Render every cover line exactly as written.
- Hierarchy respected.
- No garbled letters.
</constraints>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG, vertical 2:3 typical.</output>
<negative>no watermark, no garbled text, no extra invented cover lines.</negative>
notes: Magazine cover convention: literal masthead + hierarchy of cover lines + cover image is the rule.
Magazine cover design references and youmind editorial cover patterns.
antiPatterns: Do not invent cover lines.
Do not mix hierarchy. The primary line is the largest.

prompts.title: Product with readable label
prompts.when: You want a product image with the label text readable and legible.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a product photographer capturing a labeled product.</role>
<task>Render a hero shot of {{product}} with the label readable.</task>
<label_text>The literal label text: '{{label_text}}'. Place on the product surface once.</label_text>
<label_layout>Label location: {{location: front-center | wrap-around | side panel | top cap}}. Wrap, contour, or top accordingly.</label_layout>
<font>Label font: {{font_choice}}. Should be small but legible.</font>
<lighting>Lighting must keep the label readable. No glare on the label face.</lighting>
<detail>Label texture: {{texture: matte paper | glossy film | embossed | screen-printed | metallic foil}}.</detail>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no watermark, no extra invented text on the label, no garbled letters.</negative>
notes: Product label convention: literal label text + glare-free lighting + label-texture detail is the rule.
GPT Image 2 and Nano Banana Pro are strongest at label text rendering.
antiPatterns: Do not introduce glare that hides the label.
Do not invent additional label text.

prompts.title: Handwritten sign or chalkboard
prompts.when: You want a handwritten or chalkboard sign with literal text.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a chalkboard or sign artist.</role>
<task>Render a {{sign_type: chalkboard | wooden sign | paper note | whiteboard | cafe menu board}} with handwritten text.</task>
<text>The literal text: '{{text}}'. Hand-drawn, not typed.</text>
<style>Handwriting style: {{style: cursive | print | all-caps | chalk | marker | brush}}. Style fits the sign type.</style>
<board>Background: {{surface}}. Visible texture: wood grain, chalk dust, paper fiber, whiteboard sheen.</surface>
<decoration>Optional small decoration: {{decoration: doodle | arrow | underline | star | heart | asterisk}}. Single instance.</decoration>
<lighting>Soft even light so the text is readable. No glare on glossy whiteboard.</lighting>
<constraints>
- Render the text exactly as written.
- No garbled or partial letters.
</constraints>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no watermark, no typed text, no extra invented writing.</negative>
notes: Handwritten sign convention: literal text + named handwriting style + matching surface texture is the rule.
GPT Image 2 supports hand-drawn text well.
antiPatterns: Do not use typed text. Handwritten is the genre.
Do not invent additional writing.

prompts.title: Typography hero composition
prompts.when: You want a typography-driven hero image where text is the main visual.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a typography hero designer.</role>
<task>Design a typography hero where text is the main visual element.</task>
<hero_text>The literal hero text: '{{hero_text}}'. Place exactly once, large and dominant.</hero_text>
<typeface>Single typeface, single weight. Choose: {{typeface}}. Do not mix.</typeface>
<treatment>Optional typographic treatment: {{treatment: gradient fill | outline only | shadow only | kerning spread | leading tight | character overlap | scale contrast across letters}}.</treatment>
<composition>Text as the visual hero. {{composition: centered | diagonal | anchored to a corner | laid across a horizon line | wrapping an object}}.</composition>
<background>Supporting background: {{bg: solid color | gradient | photographic blurred | texture}}. Subordinate to text.</background>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no watermark, no extra text, no garbled letters, no more than one typeface.</negative>
notes: Typography hero convention: literal text as visual subject + one typeface + supporting background is the rule.
Youmind typography / poster references.
antiPatterns: Do not mix typefaces.
Do not let the background compete with the type.

prompts.title: Street sign in environment
prompts.when: You want a street sign rendered in a realistic environment with readable text.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an urban environmental photographer capturing street signage.</role>
<task>Render a realistic street sign in {{location}}.</task>
<sign_text>The literal sign text: '{{sign_text}}'. Place exactly once on the sign face.</sign_text>
<sign_type>{{sign_type: street name | traffic | business storefront | transit station | highway | warning}}. Period-appropriate typography.</sign_type>
<environment>Real urban or rural setting: {{environment}}. Visible context buildings, road, sky, vegetation.</environment>
<lighting>{{lighting: daylight | overcast | night with practicals | golden hour}}. Sign face readable.</lighting>
<perspective>Sign at correct perspective from camera position.</perspective>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no watermark, no garbled text, no extra invented signs.</negative>
notes: Street sign convention: literal sign text + perspective-correct + readable under scene lighting is the rule.
GPT Image 2 and Nano Banana Pro handle this well.
antiPatterns: Do not skip perspective correction.
Do not introduce garbled text.

prompts.title: Screen or UI with text content
prompts.when: You want a screen or UI mockup with literal text rendered on the display.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a UI / device mockup designer.</role>
<task>Render a {{device}} displaying {{app_or_screen_concept}} with literal text content.</task>
<text_content>
Title: '{{title}}'.
Body: '{{body}}'.
Buttons: {{button_labels}}.
All text literal. No invented text.
</text_content>
<device>{{device: smartphone | laptop | tablet | smartwatch | TV | desktop monitor}}. Visible device frame, bezels, screen surface.</device>
<angle>3/4 angled device shot. Visible screen reflection and ambient lighting on glass.</angle>
<background>Setting: {{background: desk | hand-held | wall-mounted | in use}}. Real environment.</background>
<constraints>
- Render all text exactly as written.
- Typography legible.
- Real device, not abstract UI.
</constraints>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no watermark, no garbled text, no UI placeholders like 'lorem ipsum'.</negative>
notes: Screen mockup convention: literal text + real device + 3/4 angle + screen reflection is the rule.
GPT Image 2 and Nano Banana Pro handle screen UI well.
antiPatterns: Do not use placeholder text. Render the literal text.
Do not skip the device frame. Real device is the genre.

prompts.title: Neon sign with glow
prompts.when: You want a glowing neon sign with literal text rendered realistically.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a neon sign photographer capturing a glowing sign at night.</role>
<task>Render a realistic neon sign for {{business_or_message}}.</task>
<sign_text>The literal neon text: '{{sign_text}}'. Spell out the text using bent glass tubes.</sign_text>
<font>Tubing shape: {{font: cursive script | block capitals | geometric | retro display}}. The tubing must follow the letterforms.</font>
<color>Tube color: {{tube_color}}. Optional secondary color: {{accent_color}}.</color>
<glow>Visible bloom and glow around the tubes. Cast neon-colored light onto surrounding surfaces.</glow>
<mounting>Mounting: {{mounting: window | wall | freestanding | hanging | rooftop}}. Realistic installation.</mounting>
<scene>Scene around the sign: {{scene: city street at night | bar interior | storefront | alley}}. Dark environment to make the glow pop.</scene>
<constraints>
- Render neon text exactly as written.
- Visible tube thickness.
- Visible halo and bloom.
</constraints>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no watermark, no garbled text, no daytime.</negative>
notes: Neon sign convention: literal text + visible tube + bloom + cast glow is the rule.
GPT Image 2 and Nano Banana Pro handle neon text well.
antiPatterns: Do not skip the tube thickness.
Do not render the sign in daylight.

prompts.title: Rule of thirds composition
prompts.when: You want a subject placed on the rule-of-thirds intersections for a balanced classic composition.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a composition specialist applying the rule of thirds.</role>
<task>Compose a {{scene_type}} of {{subject_description}} on the rule of thirds.</task>
<grid>Divide the canvas into a 3x3 grid. Place the subject's primary focal point on one of the four intersections: top-left, top-right, bottom-left, or bottom-right.</grid>
<horizon>Place any horizon on the upper third (subject dominant) or lower third (sky dominant) line.</horizon>
<leading_lines>Use implied leading lines from corners to the subject.</leading_lines>
<negative_space>Leave generous negative space in the two-thirds of the frame not occupied by the subject.</negative_space>
<camera>Eye-level or slight high/low angle. Avoid dead-center subject placement.</camera>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no center-placed subject, no horizon in the middle, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Rule of thirds convention: explicit intersection placement + horizon on third line + leading lines is the rule.
Classic composition references and youmind photography patterns: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/photography.
antiPatterns: Do not center the subject. Intersections only.
Do not place the horizon in the middle.

prompts.title: Golden ratio composition
prompts.when: You want a composition following the golden ratio spiral or golden rectangle placement.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a composition specialist applying the golden ratio.</role>
<task>Compose {{scene_type}} of {{subject_description}} along the golden ratio.</task>
<spiral>Place the subject's focal point where the golden spiral terminates. The subject fills the tightest spiral curl.</spiral>
<divisions>Horizon or major line on a golden ratio division (1 to 1.618, not 1 to 2).</divisions>
<flow>The eye follows the spiral from open negative space into the tight curl around the subject.</flow>
<camera>Eye-level or angle chosen to emphasize the spiral.</camera>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no center placement, no thirds, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Golden ratio convention: explicit spiral placement + 1:1.618 divisions is the genre.
Classical art composition references.
antiPatterns: Do not default to rule of thirds. The golden ratio is a different proportion.
Do not center the subject.

prompts.title: Leading lines composition
prompts.when: You want a composition with strong leading lines that draw the eye to the subject.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a composition specialist using leading lines.</role>
<task>Compose {{scene_type}} of {{subject_description}} with strong leading lines.</task>
<lines>Identify or create converging lines: {{line_sources: road | railroad tracks | corridor | architectural lines | shadows | fence | river}}. Lines start from the corners or edges and converge toward the subject.</lines>
<vanishing_point>Lines converge on a single vanishing point at or near the subject.</vanishing_point>
<subject>The subject sits at the convergence point or along the strongest line.</subject>
<depth>Strong sense of depth from foreground lines to midground to background.</depth>
<camera>Eye-level or low angle for strongest line convergence.</camera>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no dead-center composition, no crossing lines, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Leading lines convention: explicit line sources + convergence on subject + depth is the rule.
Classic composition references and youmind photography patterns.
antiPatterns: Do not let lines cross or compete.
Do not center the subject without supporting lines.

prompts.title: Frame within frame composition
prompts.when: You want the subject framed by another element (window, arch, doorway, foliage) for depth and context.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a composition specialist using frame-within-frame technique.</role>
<task>Compose {{scene_type}} of {{subject_description}} framed by {{framing_element}}.</task>
<framing_element>{{framing_element: window | archway | doorway | foliage | tunnel | bridge | cave mouth | alley | fence opening}}. The framing element occupies the edges or one side of the frame.</framing_element>
<subject>The subject sits inside the opening created by the framing element. The opening is centered or rule-of-thirds.</subject>
<depth>Three planes: foreground (framing element), midground (subject), background (scene beyond).</depth>
<lighting>The framing element often creates a vignette of darker edges, focusing the eye inward.</lighting>
<camera>Eye-level. The camera may shoot through the framing element as if peeking.</camera>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no flat composition, no subject on the edge, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Frame-within-frame convention: explicit framing element + subject inside opening + depth planes is the rule.
Classic composition references.
antiPatterns: Do not let the framing element cover the subject.
Do not omit the depth planes.

prompts.title: Symmetrical composition
prompts.when: You want a bilaterally symmetric composition for a formal, balanced, or architectural feel.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a composition specialist designing a bilaterally symmetric image.</role>
<task>Compose a symmetric {{scene_type}} of {{subject_description}}.</task>
<axis>Mirror axis along the vertical center line of the canvas. Left and right must mirror each other.</axis>
<subject>Subject on the axis or mirrored on both sides.</subject>
<background>Symmetric environment: corridor, facade, reflection, paired objects.</background>
<variation>Optional subtle asymmetry: one small element on one side only, to break the perfect symmetry without breaking the form.</variation>
<camera>Frontal, perpendicular to the mirror axis. No tilt.</camera>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no asymmetric placement, no rule-of-thirds placement, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Symmetry convention: explicit mirror axis + frontal camera is the rule.
Wes Anderson and architectural symmetry references.
antiPatterns: Do not place the subject off-axis.
Do not introduce tilt or rotation.

prompts.title: Radial or circular composition
prompts.when: You want a radial composition where elements radiate from or converge to a center.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a composition specialist designing a radial image.</role>
<task>Compose a radial {{scene_type}} with elements radiating from or converging on a center.</task>
<center>Subject or focal point at the exact center of the canvas.</center>
<radiating>Lines, shapes, or elements radiate outward from the center. Or elements converge inward toward the center.</radiating>
<repetition>Repeating motifs at intervals around the center, like spokes.</repetition>
<palette>{{palette}}. Symmetric around the center.</palette>
<camera>Frontal, perpendicular to the canvas plane.</camera>
<output>Square 1:1 PNG.</output>
<negative>no off-center subject, no asymmetric placement, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Radial convention: explicit center + radiating spokes + repetition is the genre.
Mandala and rose-window composition references.
antiPatterns: Do not place the focal point off-center.
Do not break the radial symmetry.

prompts.title: Diagonal dynamic composition
prompts.when: You want a strong diagonal composition for energy, motion, or drama.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a composition specialist designing a diagonal image.</role>
<task>Compose {{scene_type}} of {{subject_description}} along a strong diagonal.</task>
<diagonal>Identify or create a strong diagonal: subject or major element runs from corner to corner (or near-corner to near-corner).</diagonal>
<energy>The diagonal implies energy, motion, or tension. Avoid static horizontal or vertical placement.</energy>
<camera>Tilt the camera, angle the subject, or use perspective to create the diagonal.</camera>
<background>Supporting elements follow or contrast the diagonal direction.</background>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no horizontal or vertical dominant, no static composition, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Diagonal convention: explicit corner-to-corner energy + tension is the genre.
Classic composition references and youmind photography patterns.
antiPatterns: Do not flatten the diagonal into a horizontal.
Do not introduce static symmetry.

prompts.title: Negative space minimal composition
prompts.when: You want a minimal composition with generous negative space and a single subject.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a composition specialist designing a minimal negative-space image.</role>
<task>Compose {{scene_type}} of {{subject_description}} with dominant negative space.</task>
<negative_space>70 to 90 percent of the canvas is empty or near-empty. Single small subject in the remaining area.</negative_space>
<subject_placement>Subject off-center, placed where the negative space reads as intentional.</subject_placement>
<breathing>Generous breathing room around the subject. No clutter.</breathing>
<background>Empty or near-uniform. The emptiness is part of the composition.</background>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no busy background, no centered subject, no clutter, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Negative space convention: 70 to 90 percent empty + small intentional subject is the genre.
Classic minimalism references and youmind minimal illustration patterns.
antiPatterns: Do not fill the negative space with detail.
Do not center the subject.

prompts.title: Layered depth composition
prompts.when: You want a composition with clear foreground, midground, and background depth layers.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a composition specialist designing a layered depth image.</role>
<task>Compose {{scene_type}} of {{subject_description}} with three clear depth layers.</task>
<foreground>One foreground element: {{foreground_element}}. Partially frames the scene or adds depth cue.</foreground>
<midground>The subject in the midground. Largest layer in the composition.</midground>
<background>Background environment: {{background}}. Visible but subordinate.</background>
<depth_cues>Use {{cues: atmospheric perspective | size gradient | overlapping forms | focus gradient | aerial perspective}} to create depth.</depth_cues>
<camera>Stop down (f/8 to f/11) for full scene sharpness, OR use depth of field to isolate the subject.</camera>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no flat composition, no single-layer scene, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Layered depth convention: explicit three planes + depth cues is the rule.
Classic landscape composition references.
antiPatterns: Do not flatten the depth into a single plane.
Do not omit the foreground element.

prompts.title: S-curve composition
prompts.when: You want an S-curve composition for landscape, road, river, or flowing subject.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a composition specialist using the S-curve.</role>
<task>Compose an S-curve {{scene_type}} of {{subject_description}}.</task>
<curve>Identify or create an S-shaped curve that runs through the canvas: enters from one edge, curves to the opposite, exits the other edge.</curve>
<subject>Subject placed along the curve. Eye follows the curve to the subject.</subject>
<flow>The curve creates natural flow and depth. Often used for rivers, roads, fences, paths.</flow>
<camera>High angle or eye level. The curve is most visible when the camera sees the full length.</camera>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no straight composition, no centered subject, no watermark.</negative>
notes: S-curve convention: explicit curve shape from edge to edge is the rule.
Classic landscape composition references.
antiPatterns: Do not flatten the S into a straight line.
Do not center the subject off the curve.

prompts.title: Golden hour atmosphere
prompts.when: You want a golden-hour warm atmosphere with backlight and long shadows.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a cinematographer setting up a golden hour scene.</role>
<task>Render a golden hour {{scene_type}} of {{subject_description}}.</task>
<sun_position>Sun within 10 degrees of the horizon, {{left_or_right}} of the frame.</sun_position>
<light>Warm 3200K color temperature. Long horizontal shadows. Backlight rim on subjects facing the sun.</light>
<flare>Lens flare if sun in frame, with halo and anamorphic streak if applicable.</flare>
<palette>Warm dominant: amber, peach, coral, gold. Cool accents in shadow.</palette>
<mood>{{mood: warm and nostalgic | romantic and dreamy | cinematic and dramatic | peaceful and quiet}}.</mood>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no midday harsh light, no cool blue tones, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Golden hour convention: low sun + warm dominant + rim backlight is the genre.
Youmind golden hour references: https://youmind.com/prompts/image/photography.
antiPatterns: Do not place the sun overhead.
Do not mix cool and warm dominants.

prompts.title: Blue hour atmosphere
prompts.when: You want a blue-hour twilight atmosphere with deep blue sky and city lights beginning.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a twilight cinematographer setting up blue hour.</role>
<task>Render a blue hour {{scene_type}} of {{subject_description}}.</task>
<time>Within 30 minutes after sunset or before sunrise. Sun below the horizon.</time>
<sky>Deep saturated blue gradient, brighter at the horizon, deeper overhead.</sky>
<lights>City lights, building windows, streetlights, signs beginning to glow. Practical lights visible.</lights>
<palette>Cool blue dominant. Warm accents from practical lights. High contrast between sky and lit windows.</palette>
<mood>{{mood: contemplative and quiet | cinematic and dramatic | magical and dreamlike | city and energetic}}.</mood>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no full daylight, no golden-hour warmth, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Blue hour convention: deep blue sky + emerging practical lights + warm/cool contrast is the genre.
Youmind cinematic / photography references.
antiPatterns: Do not introduce full daylight.
Do not over-light the scene. Blue hour is dark.

prompts.title: Fog and mist atmosphere
prompts.when: You want a foggy or misty scene with depth falloff and ethereal mood.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an atmospheric photographer working in fog or mist.</role>
<task>Render a foggy {{scene_type}} of {{subject_description}}.</task>
<fog_type>{{fog: ground fog | valley mist | coastal fog | morning mist | smoke | haze}}. Visible and intentional.</fog_type>
<density>{{density: light | medium | heavy}}. Heavy fog reduces visibility to a few meters.</density>
<depth_falloff>Distant objects fade into fog. Atmospheric perspective reduces contrast and saturation with distance.</depth_falloff>
<lighting>{{light: backlit fog glows with sun behind | flat overcast soft | moonlight through fog | warm streetlight glow}}.</light>
<palette>Desaturated. Muted. Cool dominant unless lit by a warm source.</palette>
<mood>{{mood: mysterious and eerie | peaceful and contemplative | cinematic and dramatic | ethereal and dreamlike}}.</mood>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no clear blue sky, no sharp distant detail, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Fog convention: depth falloff + visible fog particle + named light source through fog is the genre.
Youmind atmospheric / mood references.
antiPatterns: Do not skip the depth falloff. Fog does that.
Do not introduce a clear sky.

prompts.title: Rain and wet atmosphere
prompts.when: You want a rainy scene with visible rain streaks, wet surfaces, and reflective puddles.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a rainy-day photographer capturing wet atmosphere.</role>
<task>Render a rainy {{scene_type}} of {{subject_description}}.</task>
<rain>Visible rain streaks in the air, especially against darker backgrounds or backlit sources. Slanted or vertical depending on wind.</rain>
<wet_surfaces>Surfaces look wet: reflective puddles, sheen on pavement, water droplets on glass and metal, dark saturated colors.</wet_surfaces>
<reflections>Puddles reflect light sources, signs, silhouettes. Mirror-like in heavy rain.</reflections>
<lighting>{{light: overcast soft | neon city reflections | warm streetlight glow | backlit rain | umbrella-diffused light}}.</lighting>
<palette>Cool dominant. Saturated wet colors. Highlights from light sources reflecting on water.</palette>
<mood>{{mood: melancholy and reflective | cinematic noir | cozy indoors | urban electric | contemplative}}.</mood>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no dry sunny scene, no clear sky, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Rain convention: visible streaks + wet surfaces + puddle reflections is the genre.
Youmind cinematic / atmospheric references.
antiPatterns: Do not skip the puddle reflections.
Do not introduce dry surfaces.

prompts.title: Snow and winter atmosphere
prompts.when: You want a snowy scene with falling snow, snow accumulation, and cold winter mood.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a winter photographer working in the snow.</role>
<task>Render a snowy {{scene_type}} of {{subject_description}}.</task>
<snow_condition>{{snow: light flurries | heavy snowfall | fresh powder | packed snow | ice | freezing rain}}.</snow_condition>
<falling_snow>Visible flakes in the air, especially against dark backgrounds. Variable size, sense of motion.</falling_snow>
<accumulation>Snow accumulated on surfaces: ground, branches, rooftops, ledges, shoulders of subjects.</accumulation>
<breath>Visible breath from any people or animals in the cold.</breath>
<lighting>{{light: overcast winter soft | blue twilight snow | golden hour on fresh snow | streetlight on snow | backlit snowfall}}.</light>
<palette>Cool dominant: cool grays, blues, muted whites. Warm accent from any visible light source.</palette>
<mood>{{mood: peaceful and quiet | cinematic and dramatic | magical and pristine | harsh and survival | cozy and warm}}.</mood>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no green vegetation, no warm tropical feel, no clear sky, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Snow convention: falling flakes + accumulation + visible breath + cool palette is the genre.
Youmind winter / atmospheric references.
antiPatterns: Do not omit the visible breath. Cold is the genre.
Do not introduce warm tones.

prompts.title: Sunset dusk atmosphere
prompts.when: You want a sunset or dusk scene with warm-to-cool sky gradient and silhouettes.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a sunset photographer capturing the transition moment.</role>
<task>Render a sunset {{scene_type}} of {{subject_description}}.</task>
<sun_position>Sun on or just below the horizon. Halo or rim visible.</sun_position>
<sky_gradient>Vertical sky gradient: deep blue overhead, transitioning through purple, magenta, orange, gold near the horizon.</sky_gradient>
<silhouettes>Subjects often in silhouette against the bright sky. Visible profile, dark interior.</silhouettes>
<lighting>Mixed: warm from the setting sun, cool from the sky overhead. Long shadows. Soft rim light on subjects.</lighting>
<palette>Warm dominant at horizon, cool dominant overhead. Strong color contrast.</palette>
<mood>{{mood: romantic and golden | contemplative and quiet | cinematic and dramatic | nostalgic and wistful | hopeful}}.</mood>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no midday harsh light, no pure cool palette, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Sunset convention: warm-to-cool sky gradient + silhouettes + rim light is the genre.
Youmind cinematic / golden hour references.
antiPatterns: Do not place the sun overhead.
Do not mix midday light with sunset sky.

prompts.title: Aurora borealis atmosphere
prompts.when: You want an aurora borealis scene with visible northern lights over a dark landscape.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an astrophotographer capturing the aurora borealis.</role>
<task>Render an aurora {{scene_type}} of {{subject_description}}.</task>
<aurora>Visible aurora borealis: green dominant with possible pink, purple, or red at the edges. Curtain-like vertical structure. Movement implied.</aurora>
<sky>Dark night sky with visible stars. Aurora bright enough to cast subtle light on the landscape below.</sky>
<landscape>Dark silhouette landscape: {{landscape: tundra | forest | mountain | lake | fjord}}. Reflected aurora in water if present.</landscape>
<lighting>Aurora as primary light source. Subtle ambient light on landscape. Long exposure feel: sharp stars, aurora curtain slightly soft from motion.</lighting>
<palette>Green dominant aurora, dark blue-black sky, dark landscape. Optional magenta or pink aurora edges.</palette>
<mood>{{mood: magical and awe | quiet and meditative | cinematic and epic | spiritual and vast}}.</mood>
<output>Horizontal 16:9 or vertical 2:3 PNG.</output>
<negative>no daylight, no city lights, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Aurora convention: green curtain structure + dark sky + reflected light on landscape is the genre.
Youmind atmospheric / night-sky references.
antiPatterns: Do not introduce daylight.
Do not omit the reflected aurora on water.

prompts.title: Candlelit intimate atmosphere
prompts.when: You want a candlelit scene with warm intimate light and deep shadow.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a still-life or portrait photographer working in candlelight.</role>
<task>Render a candlelit {{scene_type}} of {{subject_description}}.</task>
<light_source>{{n_candles}} candle(s) as primary light source. Visible flame. Warm orange-yellow flicker.</light_source>
<lighting>Strong chiaroscuro: lit faces or surfaces on the candle side, deep shadow on the opposite side. Visible warm glow on nearby surfaces.</lighting>
<palette>Warm dominant: amber, gold, deep orange. Cool dark shadows in the unlit areas. Limited range, no bright color.</palette>
<mood>Intimate, contemplative, romantic, or solemn. Time seems to slow.</mood>
<background>Dark or near-black. Vignette of darkness around the subject.</background>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no daylight, no fluorescent, no bright color, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Candlelight convention: visible flame + chiaroscuro + warm narrow palette is the genre.
Youmind cinematic / atmospheric references.
antiPatterns: Do not introduce modern electric light.
Do not flatten the chiaroscuro.

prompts.title: Overcast soft atmosphere
prompts.when: You want an overcast or cloudy scene with soft diffused light and muted colors.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a soft-light photographer working on an overcast day.</role>
<task>Render an overcast {{scene_type}} of {{subject_description}}.</task>
<sky>Uniform overcast sky. Soft white or pale gray, no visible sun. Even illumination from above.</sky>
<lighting>Soft diffused light from the entire sky. No hard shadows. Wraps around subjects gently.</lighting>
<palette>Muted and slightly desaturated. Greens rich but not bright. Blues gray rather than saturated.</palette>
<contrast>Lower contrast than sunny conditions. Highlights and shadows compressed.</contrast>
<mood>{{mood: contemplative and quiet | cinematic and moody | peaceful and even | dramatic in stillness}}.</mood>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no harsh midday sun, no strong cast shadows, no bright saturated colors, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Overcast convention: uniform sky + diffused light + muted palette is the genre.
Youmind photography / atmospheric references.
antiPatterns: Do not introduce hard cast shadows.
Do not push saturation.

prompts.title: Stormy thunder atmosphere
prompts.when: You want a stormy scene with dramatic clouds, lightning, and electric atmosphere.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a storm photographer capturing dramatic atmospheric conditions.</role>
<task>Render a stormy {{scene_type}} of {{subject_description}}.</task>
<clouds>Dramatic cumulonimbus or storm clouds: heavy, dark, layered. Visible structure and movement. Light from within or behind.</clouds>
<lightning>Optional visible lightning bolt. Branched structure. Bright illumination of clouds and landscape at the strike.</lightning>
<rain>Visible heavy rain. Sheets of water. Reduced visibility.</rain>
<lighting>Mixed: dark sky, occasional lightning flash, possible lightning backlight on clouds. Dark overall with bright accents.</lighting>
<palette>Dark cool dominant. Bright accents from lightning or practical lights. High dynamic range.</palette>
<mood>{{mood: ominous and powerful | cinematic and dramatic | awe and fear | elemental and raw}}.</mood>
<output>{{dimensions}} PNG.</output>
<negative>no sunshine, no blue sky, no bright daylight, no watermark.</negative>
notes: Storm convention: dramatic clouds + optional lightning + heavy rain + dark dynamic palette is the genre.
Youmind atmospheric / cinematic references.
antiPatterns: Do not introduce sunshine or blue sky.
Do not flatten the dark dramatic mood.

prompts.title: Cinematic feature film opener
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 30 second theatrical-quality opener to brand a feature, reel, or pitch.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a virtual cinematographer directing a feature-quality opening title sequence.</role>
<scene>A wide establishing shot over a vast mountain range at dawn. Fog sits in the valleys. Sunlight rakes across granite peaks, lens flares softly through the mist.</scene>
<subject>No human subjects. Pure landscape with one slow-moving eagle silhouette that crosses the upper third of the frame.</subject>
<motion>Camera drifts forward on a dolly track, gradually descending into the valley while tilting up to catch the sun. The eagle glides left to right. Mist flows downhill. Foliage ripples.</motion>
<camera>Anamorphic 2.39:1 framing, 35mm equivalent, slow push-in over 12 seconds, then static hold for the final 6 seconds as the studio mark fades in.</camera>
<lighting>Natural golden hour key from upper right, cool blue ambient fill from the shadowed valley, warm rim light on ridge edges.</lighting>
<audio>Deep orchestral swell that builds slowly. Distant wind. One synth note holding underneath.</audio>
<output>Aspect 2.39:1, 24fps, 18 seconds, photorealistic, no stylized filters.</output>
<negative>no logos, no text, no people, no cartoon style, no over-saturated HDR, no sudden camera cuts, no handheld shake.</negative>
notes: Pattern adapted from Christopher Nolan / Roger Deakins feature opener conventions: anamorphic framing, controlled dolly, single hero light source.
Forcing a single slow camera move rather than multiple cuts gives the model a clear motion target and avoids morph artifacts.
antiPatterns: Do not stack multiple camera moves (push-in plus tilt plus pan). Pick one primary motion.
Do not ask for text overlays in the same prompt. Add titles in post.

prompts.title: Rain-soaked neon mood scene
prompts.when: You need a 10 to 15 second atmosphere shot for a mood reel, opening title, or B-roll.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a DP shooting a moody establishing shot for a neo-noir short.</role>
<scene>Empty side street in a dense Asian megacity at 2am. Wet asphalt. Vertical neon signs in Japanese and Korean reflect off the ground in pink, cyan, and amber.</scene>
<subject>No people. A single black umbrella leans against a closed metal shutter, abandoned. A puddle ripples from an unseen drip above.</subject>
<motion>Static wide shot. Only movement is rain falling in sheets, neon signs flickering subtly, and the puddle ripples expanding outward.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 24mm wide, deep focus from foreground puddle to distant signage. Shutter 1/60 to preserve slight rain motion blur.</camera>
<lighting>Practical neon sources only. No moonlight. Reflections are the entire lighting palette: hot pink on left, cool cyan on right, amber streetlamp center distance.</lighting>
<audio>Steady rain hiss on concrete. Distant muffled bass from a club. A single bicycle bell three streets away.</audio>
<output>Aspect 16:9, 24fps, 12 seconds, photorealistic, slight grain.</output>
<negative>no people, no text, no vehicles, no bright daylight, no over-saturated neon, no camera movement, no lens flare.</negative>
notes: Atmosphere shots without people are far more reliable on current video models because identity drift is removed from the equation.
Constraining the palette to neon-only is a Blade Runner 2049 lighting trick that hides AI artifacts in colorful specular reflections.
antiPatterns: Do not add a character walking through the scene. It will pull focus from the atmosphere and likely deform.
Do not request camera motion on a moody static shot. Movement kills the contemplative pacing.

prompts.title: Hero sunset reveal shot
prompts.when: You need a single grand reveal shot to punctuate the end of a cinematic short.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a DP designing the climactic hero shot of a feature trailer.</role>
<scene>A lone figure on horseback stands on a desert mesa, looking out over an endless red rock canyon system as the sun sets behind distant buttes.</scene>
<subject>One rider, silhouetted against the sky, cowboy hat, duster coat catching the wind. Horse stands still, tail flicking.</subject>
<motion>Camera holds perfectly still for the first 8 seconds while the sun drops two degrees. In the final 4 seconds, a slow push-in begins, closing from 50mm to 85mm equivalent, ending on the rider's profile.</motion>
<camera>35mm base, anamorphic flare at frame edges, deep focus, locked tripod for the static phase then engaged geared head for the slow push.</camera>
<lighting>Backlit hero. Sun is the key, rim-lighting the rider and horse in molten orange. Foreground falls into deep purple shadow. Sky bands from amber to violet to indigo at the zenith.</lighting>
<audio>Wind across open rock. One horse snort. Distant hawk cry. A low cello drone fades in over the final 3 seconds.</audio>
<output>Aspect 2.39:1, 24fps, 12 seconds, photorealistic, film grain.</output>
<negative>no zooming during the static phase, no other characters, no modern objects, no clouds covering the sun, no lens dust, no text.</negative>
notes: Inspired by Terrence Malick and John Ford horizon shots: a single silhouetted figure against a painted sky.
Forcing a 'still then push' timing pattern prevents the model from doing a generic push-in the entire shot.
antiPatterns: Do not animate the horse or rider heavily. A still silhouette reads more epic than a moving one.
Do not request clouds. They drift unpredictably and break the locked horizon composition.

prompts.title: Demo reel showcase shot
prompts.when: You need a 10 second signature shot that demonstrates your technical skill to a client or audience.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a DP and colorist producing a single hero shot for a director's reel opening.</role>
<scene>An ultra-wide, hyper-detailed macro shot of a mechanical watch movement, fully assembled, sitting on black velvet. A single shaft of hard light cuts across the frame from upper left.</scene>
<subject>Watch movement with jewels, gears, screws, balance wheel. No case, no strap, no dial. The mechanism is exposed in its full horological complexity.</subject>
<motion>Camera performs a slow, perfectly steady horizontal slide from left to right, ending on the balance wheel which oscillates back and forth. Subtle rack focus: front of movement soft, balance wheel sharp at the end.</motion>
<camera>Macro probe lens, 100mm equivalent, very shallow depth of field (f/2.8 effective), locked slider dolly, motion smoothing.</camera>
<lighting>Single hard key light from upper left, blue-white daylight balance. Black flag on right side for contrast. No fill. Background drops to pure black.</lighting>
<audio>Mechanical tick-tock at a steady 4Hz. A faint metallic resonance. Silence underneath.</audio>
<output>Aspect 16:9, 24fps, 10 seconds, hyperrealistic, no stylized filters.</output>
<negative>no hands, no movement of the watch itself except the balance wheel, no bright background, no text, no watermark, no over-saturated color.</negative>
notes: Demo reel pattern: pick a single hero subject and run one perfectly-executed camera move for the full duration.
Macro + mechanical subject is a perennial reel winner because depth of field hides AI artifacts on edges.
antiPatterns: Do not stack multiple watch angles in the same shot. Pick one movement and execute it.
Do not ask for slow motion. The watch tick-tock depends on real-time pacing.

prompts.title: Lightning storm hero shot
prompts.when: You need a 15 second weather-driven hero shot for an opener, transition, or dramatic beat.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a DP and storm chaser compositing a controlled storm shot for a feature trailer.</role>
<scene>A lone wooden farmhouse sits on a flat prairie at night. A massive supercell thunderstorm rolls in from the horizon, lit from within by continuous lightning.</scene>
<subject>One farmhouse, weathered grey wood, single warm light in an upstairs window. No people visible. A windmill spins rapidly in the foreground.</subject>
<motion>Camera holds a static wide for the first 6 seconds while lightning flickers. Then a slow push-in over the final 9 seconds toward the farmhouse window. The storm cloud rotates slowly on its own axis.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 24mm wide, deep focus, no stabilizer.</camera>
<lighting>Storm is the primary key: cold blue-white lightning illuminates cloud and ground in irregular pulses. The farmhouse window provides a tiny warm counter-key.</lighting>
<audio>Distant thunder rolling, wind building, the windmill creaking, faint glass rattle in the farmhouse window.</audio>
<output>Aspect 2.39:1, 24fps, 15 seconds, photorealistic, slight film grain.</output>
<negative>no people, no vehicles, no text, no oversaturated color, no nuclear green sky, no camera shake, no tornado.</negative>
notes: Pattern inspired by Twister and Into the Storm storm-cinematography: locked wide + natural lightning as the lighting engine.
Specifying the farmhouse window as warm counter-key is the storytelling beat. Without it, the shot is just weather footage.
antiPatterns: Do not add a tornado or extreme weather beyond what one wide shot can hold. Models collapse at multi-element disasters.
Do not ask for the lightning to strike a specific target. Continuous cloud-internal lightning is achievable; targeted bolts deform.

prompts.title: Underwater surface ascend shot
prompts.when: You need a 10 to 15 second dreamlike transition shot for a short film, music video, or reel.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an underwater DP and a topside DP shooting a coordinated dual-environment shot.</role>
<scene>Camera starts fully submerged in clear turquoise ocean water looking upward. As the shot progresses, the camera breaks the surface and continues rising into open sky.</scene>
<subject>No human subject. Refracted light caustics dance on the underwater portion. Bubbles drift upward. Once surfaced, the horizon reveals a single small wooden sailboat in the middle distance.</subject>
<motion>Continuous vertical ascend from 10 feet below the surface to 30 feet above it. Water level crosses the lens around the 5 second mark. The sailboat remains static in the frame as the camera rises.</motion>
<camera>Locked vertical lift on a stabilized gimbal. Wide 18mm equivalent to capture both environments. Slight forward drift on the boat.</camera>
<lighting>Underwater: refracted sunlight as the only source, blue-green color cast, dancing caustics. Above water: full daylight with sun high and slightly behind camera.</lighting>
<audio>Underwater silence for first 5 seconds (muffled, pressure-feel), then sudden break: surface splash, wind, distant gull calls.</audio>
<output>Aspect 16:9, 24fps, 12 seconds, photorealistic, slight haze at the surface transition.</output>
<negative>no people, no boats near camera, no text, no over-saturated aqua, no rapid splashes, no jellyfish.</negative>
notes: The 'breaking the surface' transition is a signature cinematic move (see Life of Pi, Avatar: Way of Water).
Splitting audio design across the two halves sells the transition more than visual tricks alone.
antiPatterns: Do not add a character swimming. Continuity of human form across the waterline is the hardest possible motion for current models.
Do not request slow motion. The transition timing depends on real speed.

prompts.title: Aerial city flyover opener
prompts.when: You need a 15 second aerial hero shot for a city-set feature, real estate reel, or news opener.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a drone operator and colorist designing the opening aerial shot for a city drama.</role>
<scene>A fictional mid-sized coastal city at the blue hour, just after sunset. The skyline mixes modern glass towers with older brick mid-rises. A river cuts through the center with two illuminated bridges.</scene>
<subject>No people visible. The city itself is the subject: lit windows, headlights on bridges, a single train crossing one bridge.</subject>
<motion>Camera starts at street level tilted up at the nearest tower. Drone rises smoothly over 12 seconds to 800 feet altitude while rotating 90 degrees to face downriver. Final 3 seconds hold on a top-down view of the river and bridges.</motion>
<camera>DJI Inspire equivalent virtual camera, smooth 3-axis gimbal, no abrupt direction changes, locked horizon.</camera>
<lighting>Blue hour ambient. Warm tungsten from building windows. Cool white LED streetlights on bridges. Reflection of city on calm river.</lighting>
<audio>Low ambient city hum. Distant traffic. One distant foghorn. No music.</audio>
<output>Aspect 16:9, 24fps, 15 seconds, photorealistic, slight atmospheric haze in the distance.</output>
<negative>no birds, no aircraft, no text, no visible camera drone shadow, no over-saturated neon, no people in close-up.</negative>
notes: Pattern modeled on real-world drone cinema opens: tilt up, ascend, rotate, top-down. Predictable progression helps the model commit to motion.
Blue hour is the easiest time-of-day for video models because ambient light is balanced and shadows are not extreme.
antiPatterns: Do not request the camera to perform a barrel roll or aggressive maneuver. Steady cinematic motion is what current models can hold.
Do not add specific landmarks by name unless they are world-famous. Generic skylines deform less.

prompts.title: Magic portal opening sequence
prompts.when: You need a 10 second VFX-heavy portal reveal for a fantasy short, game cinematic, or ad.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a VFX supervisor planning a single complex portal reveal in-camera.</role>
<scene>A circular stone arch in a mossy ruin at twilight. Runes carved into the arch begin to glow, build to a flash, then resolve into a stable portal showing a different world.</scene>
<subject>Stone arch with carved runes. No characters. The portal interior shows a glowing alien desert with two moons in a purple sky.</subject>
<motion>Static wide for 3 seconds, then a slow push-in to medium close-up on the arch. At the 6 second mark, runes ignite in sequence from bottom to top, peaking in a flash. The portal interior stabilizes and remains static for the final 4 seconds.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod with one slow push-in, 28mm to 50mm equivalent, shallow depth of field with focus on the runes.</camera>
<lighting>Twilight base. As runes ignite, they emit warm amber key light onto surrounding moss. The portal interior is the dominant key by the final 4 seconds, casting purple-blue fill onto the stone.</lighting>
<audio>Low wind, distant birds. As runes ignite: rising ethereal synth choir. At flash: a deep resonant boom. Final 4 seconds: subtle wind from the portal world.</audio>
<output>Aspect 16:9, 24fps, 10 seconds, photorealistic, balanced exposure.</output>
<negative>no characters, no other ruins, no stars in the twilight sky, no oversaturated glow, no camera shake on the flash.</negative>
notes: Pattern inspired by Doctor Strange and Stargate portal reveals: build, ignite, flash, settle.
Specifying the post-flash state (alien desert with two moons) prevents the model from inventing inconsistent portal interiors.
antiPatterns: Do not ask the model to show a character walking through the portal in the same shot. Multi-stage character continuity across a VFX event is not reliable.
Do not request multiple portals in the same scene.

prompts.title: Product hero in cathedral light
prompts.when: You need a 10 second luxury product hero shot that doubles as ad footage and reel B-roll.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a commercial DP and gaffer setting up a single-product hero shot with cathedral lighting.</role>
<scene>An empty marble hall with tall arched windows on one side. A single object of art sits on a black plinth in the exact center, lit by a single shaft of hard sunlight cutting through the nearest window.</scene>
<subject>The object on the plinth: {{product_description: a perfume bottle, a watch on a stand, a single sneaker}}. Choose one. Surface reflects the window and the sun shaft.</subject>
<motion>Camera performs a perfect 180-degree orbit around the plinth at a constant height and radius, completing one full revolution in 10 seconds. The sun shaft remains fixed in frame, the camera orbits through light and shadow.</motion>
<camera>100mm macro equivalent, shallow depth of field with focus locked on the product, geared slider on a circular track.</camera>
<lighting>Single hard sunlight key from upper left through the window, cool blue ambient from the marble hall, no fill. Hard shadow falls right of the product.</lighting>
<audio>Silence except a single faint echo of a footstep on marble at the 5 second mark, suggesting the camera operator.</audio>
<output>Aspect 16:9, 24fps, 10 seconds, hyperrealistic, slight film grain.</output>
<negative>no people, no other props, no text on the plinth, no background music, no second light source, no dust motes.</negative>
notes: Orbit-shot pattern adapted from luxury watch and fragrance commercials: a single subject on a single light.
The implicit footstep sound at the half-orbit is a Wes Anderson trick to remind the viewer a camera exists.
antiPatterns: Do not move the camera up or down during the orbit. Vertical drift breaks the symmetry.
Do not request multiple products in the same shot. One subject per orbit.

prompts.title: End credits rolling shot
prompts.when: You need a loopable 30 second ambient background plate to roll credits over.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a designer producing a subtle, looping ambient plate suitable as a credits background.</role>
<scene>An abstract gradient field, deep teal at the bottom rising to soft amber at the top. A single line of horizon at the lower third. Tiny particles drift slowly through the air.</scene>
<subject>No characters, no objects. The field, gradient, and particles are the subject.</subject>
<motion>Particles drift continuously from right to left at varying speeds. The gradient breathes very subtly: the amber brightens by 5 percent and dims back over 30 seconds, looping seamlessly.</motion>
<camera>Static locked frame. No motion. No cuts.</camera>
<lighting>Self-illuminated gradient. No directional source. Even, balanced exposure from top to bottom.</lighting>
<audio>Subtle synth pad that holds a single chord, with a slow attack every 8 seconds and a slow release. No percussion.</audio>
<output>Aspect 16:9, 24fps, 30 seconds, seamless loop, soft cinematic, no text.</output>
<negative>no credits text in the shot (added in post), no people, no logos, no recognizable objects, no harsh contrast, no quick cuts.</negative>
notes: Loop-friendly ambient plates are a perennial credit-sequence solution. The trick is asking for a 5 percent brightness breath, not a hard loop.
Specifying no text in the shot is critical; the prompt is for the background plate, not the titles.
antiPatterns: Do not request any visible logos or text. Render those on top in post for control over typography.
Do not add scene changes. The whole point is a single seamless ambient loop.

prompts.title: TikTok-style daily vlog opener
prompts.when: You need a 15 second vertical opener for a daily-life TikTok or Reel.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a casual vlogger filming yourself walking to a coffee shop on a Saturday morning.</role>
<scene>A young adult walking down a sunny urban sidewalk, holding the camera at chest height facing themselves, single-handed selfie grip.</scene>
<subject>The vlogger: {{appearance}}. Wearing casual weekend clothes. Energetic, smile-ready, mid-stride.</subject>
<motion>Handheld selfie camera bouncing slightly with each step. Vlogger glances around at storefronts, then back to camera. Hair sways with the gait. Background pedestrians move past in opposite direction.</motion>
<camera>Vertical 9:16 phone selfie framing, face in upper third, eye-line with lens. No stabilization. Realistic handheld shake.</camera>
<lighting>Natural morning sunlight from the side, slight squint-friendly exposure. No ring light. No filter.</lighting>
<audio>Natural sidewalk ambience: distant traffic, a bicycle bell, soft chatter. Vlogger voiceover: 'Okay, so today I am on a mission to find the best latte in this neighborhood.'</audio>
<output>9:16 vertical, 24fps, 15 seconds, iPhone selfie aesthetic, slight over-exposure on highlights.</output>
<negative>no cinematic grade, no studio lighting, no skin smoothing, no drone shots, no text overlays, no licensed music, no filters.</negative>
notes: Pattern adapted from the YouMind daily-vlog library: real phone aesthetic, no stabilizer, side sun. https://youmind.com/prompts/video/social-lifestyle.
Forbidding filters and stabilizers in the negative prompt is the difference between AI-looking footage and authentic TikTok feel.
antiPatterns: Do not ask for 'cinematic' or 'filmic' in this prompt. It directly contradicts the vlog look.
Do not add text overlays or trending audio. Keep it source footage for editing.

prompts.title: Behind-the-scenes creator studio
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second behind-the-scenes clip for a creator's social channel or brand page.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a content creator filming your own setup process in a home studio.</role>
<scene>A small but well-lit home creator studio. Desk with monitor, ring light on a stand, microphone on a boom arm, plants, books, a half-drunk coffee.</scene>
<subject>The creator, casual clothing, hair still slightly messy, mid-20s. Setting up a camera on a tripod and adjusting its angle.</subject>
<motion>Camera is a static tripod shot of the creator from across the desk. Creator walks in from the right, sets the camera on the tripod, climbs a step stool to adjust height, looks down into the lens with a smile, then steps out of frame.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 35mm equivalent, deep focus across the desk, eye-level with the creator when standing on the floor.</camera>
<lighting>Soft ring light key from upper right, warm practical lamp on the desk as fill, monitor glow on the creator's face.</lighting>
<audio>Room tone with a quiet hum of the AC. Footsteps. The click of the camera locking into the tripod mount. A soft laugh as creator looks into the lens.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 18 seconds, natural indoor color, no filter.</output>
<negative>no other people, no text, no logo, no cinematic grade, no zoom, no music.</negative>
notes: BTS content outperforms polished content on social because it lowers the perceived distance to the creator.
The detail of the coffee cup half-drunk is a realism anchor; the model uses it as a subtle lifecycle cue.
antiPatterns: Do not put the creator in a clean white studio. The lived-in clutter sells the authenticity.
Do not add text overlays or graphics. BTS is a pure observational genre.

prompts.title: Overhead cooking reel shot
prompts.when: You need a 15 second overhead cooking shot for a recipe Reel or TikTok.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a food content creator filming a recipe overhead with a phone clamped above a wooden cutting board.</role>
<scene>A clean wooden cutting board on a white marble counter. Ingredients arranged in small bowls around the edges. The cook's hands enter from the bottom of frame.</scene>
<subject>The cook's hands: {{appearance: gender-neutral, manicured, simple ring}}. Hands are chopping an onion with a sharp chef's knife. The motion is confident and rhythmic.</subject>
<motion>Static overhead phone. Hands enter, dice the onion in even strokes, scoop the diced pieces into a small white bowl, then reach for garlic next. Knife taps the board on each cut.</motion>
<camera>Locked overhead phone mount, 24mm equivalent, deep focus, natural phone perspective (slight barrel at edges).</camera>
<lighting>Soft diffused daylight from a window off-frame left, supplemented by a single soft overhead LED panel. No harsh shadows.</lighting>
<audio>Crisp knife on board. Soft chopping rhythm. A pinch of salt hitting the onion. No music.</audio>
<output>1:1 or 9:16 square, 24fps, 15 seconds, food-magazine realism, slight color saturation on ingredients.</output>
<negative>no face, no other people, no text, no logos, no narration, no music, no camera motion, no filter.</negative>
notes: Overhead food shots are the single highest-performing food format on Instagram Reels and TikTok.
Specifying the sound of knife on board is the audio signature of cooking content; models under-deliver when it is left generic.
antiPatterns: Do not put the cook's face in the frame. Overhead with hands only reads as professional recipe content.
Do not add narration. This is a pure ambient plate; voiceover goes in edit.

prompts.title: Get ready with me mirror
prompts.when: You need a 15 second get-ready-with-me mirror shot for a beauty or lifestyle Reel.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are filming a get-ready-with-me morning routine in a bedroom mirror with a phone propped on the dresser.</role>
<scene>A bright bedroom with a large rectangular mirror leaning against a white wall. A vanity table in front with brushes, lipstick, skincare bottles arranged neatly.</scene>
<subject>The creator, wearing a silk robe, hair loose and slightly messy, minimal makeup. Standing in front of the mirror applying lip balm.</subject>
<motion>Creator is centered in the mirror. She picks up a lip balm, applies it slowly to her lower and upper lip, smiles at her reflection, then turns her head to one side to check her hairline. Returns to face center.</motion>
<camera>Locked phone propped on dresser, vertical 9:16, eye-level with the mirror reflection. No movement.</camera>
<lighting>Soft daylight from a window off-frame right, supplemented by a warm ring light just out of frame to soften skin.</lighting>
<audio>Quiet bedroom: the click of the lip balm cap opening, a soft hum of a fan, distant birds.</audio>
<output>9:16, 24fps, 15 seconds, natural skin texture, slight color warmth.</output>
<negative>no other people, no text, no filters, no music, no over-saturated color, no dramatic lighting.</negative>
notes: GRWM is a top-three format on beauty TikTok. The mirror frame is the genre's signature composition.
Requiring natural skin texture in the negative block is how you get pores and freckles instead of AI-smoothed plastic.
antiPatterns: Do not add a face close-up or product showcase. Keep the mirror frame at chest-and-up.
Do not request a song. Audio goes in post.

prompts.title: Outfit of the day loop
prompts.when: You need a 10 to 15 second OOTD (outfit-of-the-day) reveal shot for a fashion Reel.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a fashion creator filming a fit reveal in a clean minimalist setting.</role>
<scene>A blank white wall or soft pastel backdrop with even, soft light. The creator stands center.</scene>
<subject>The creator, wearing {{outfit_description: a casual summer fit, a tailored streetwear look, a Y2K outfit}}. Shoes, accessories, hair, and makeup all coordinated.</subject>
<motion>Creator walks into frame from the left, stops at center mark, does a single confident 360-degree spin in place, then holds a final pose facing camera. Hair and clothes move with the spin physics.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 50mm equivalent, full-body framing with two feet of headroom and floor space, deep focus.</camera>
<lighting>Soft, even, high-key. No shadows. Suggests a ring light or a softbox at camera height.</lighting>
<audio>Footsteps on a hard floor, fabric swish on the spin, the soft click of the final heel-set.</audio>
<output>9:16 or 4:5, 24fps, 12 seconds, clean fashion-editorial color.</output>
<negative>no other people, no props, no text, no logos visible, no music, no filter, no vignette.</negative>
notes: OOTD format has not changed in ten years: walk in, spin, pose. Prompts that over-complicate it under-perform.
Specifying one 360 spin rather than multiple poses prevents the model from generating inconsistent outfits across angles.
antiPatterns: Do not request a fast motion blur spin. The audience needs to read the outfit on the way around.
Do not add text captions in the prompt. Add them in editing.

prompts.title: Morning run POV clip
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second first-person running POV for a fitness vlog or Strava edit.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are running with a chest-mounted or wrist-mounted camera on a quiet morning route.</role>
<scene>A paved riverside path at sunrise. Trees on the left, water on the right, dew still on the path, occasional jogger or dog-walker passing in the opposite direction.</scene>
<subject>No face. The POV is the camera wearer's eyes. Only their arms swing into frame periodically. Their shadow leads on the path.</subject>
<motion>Continuous forward running pace at 5:30 min/km cadence. Natural head bob from the run. Arms swing rhythmically. The path curves gently left then right.</motion>
<camera>Chest-mount action camera, 120-degree wide, slight rolling shutter wobble, locked forward orientation.</camera>
<lighting>Low warm sunrise side-light. Long shadows from trees. No harsh midday contrast.</lighting>
<audio>Footsteps on paved path in even rhythm. Breath rhythm. Distant birds. Soft wind.</audio>
<output>16:9, 30fps, 18 seconds, action-camera color, slight saturation boost.</output>
<negative>no face, no text, no music, no other people in close-up, no dashboard mounts, no helmet selfie.</negative>
notes: Chest-mount POV is the gold-standard for fitness content; wrist mount is too shaky and head mount breaks the first-person contract.
Specifying the cadence (5:30/km) gives the model a clear motion rhythm rather than guessing.
antiPatterns: Do not show the runner's face in a selfie cut. POV means no cuts to a face shot.
Do not request slow motion. The whole point is the pace.

prompts.title: Creator studio tour talking head
prompts.when: You need a 20 to 30 second creator studio tour that doubles as a vlog intro.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a content creator giving a quick tour of your workspace in a single take.</role>
<scene>The creator's home studio, a converted spare room with a desk against the window, a clothing rack with curated pieces, plants, an acoustic foam panel on one wall, a ring light, a camera on a tripod.</scene>
<subject>The creator, casual clothing, expressive hands, walking through the space while talking to camera held selfie-style.</subject>
<motion>Handheld selfie. Creator enters from the door in the background, walks past the desk, gestures to the rack, gestures to the ring light, sits down at the desk, points at the camera, leans in. The whole sequence is one unbroken handheld shot.</motion>
<camera>Vertical 9:16 selfie handheld, 24mm equivalent, slight handheld shake, focus locked on the creator's face.</camera>
<lighting>Window daylight from behind (creates a rim light) plus a soft ring light front-fill from the desk.</lighting>
<audio>Voiceover: 'Welcome to my little studio. This is where the magic happens...' with natural pauses. Footsteps on rug. The clink of a coffee mug being set down.</audio>
<output>9:16, 24fps, 25 seconds, natural indoor color.</output>
<negative>no other people, no text, no music, no professional stabilization, no cuts, no filter.</negative>
notes: Studio tours work because they reveal process, not product. The single-take uncut requirement is what makes it feel real.
Voiceover-in-prompt is acceptable here because the camera is showing the speaker; lip-sync is not required.
antiPatterns: Do not request multiple cuts or b-roll inserts. The single-take feel is the format.
Do not add bouncy music. Keep raw audio.

prompts.title: Travel walking tour street
prompts.when: You need a 20 second walking-tour travel clip for a city guide or vacation Reel.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a travel creator walking through a famous city street, filming in selfie mode.</role>
<scene>A famous or generic city street: {{city_type: cobblestone European alley, neon-lit Asian district, sunlit Latin plaza}}. Architecture and street life are the visual subject.</scene>
<subject>The creator in the foreground, half-body framing, gesturing to the surroundings, walking backwards slowly so the street stays in frame behind them.</subject>
<motion>Handheld walking backwards. Camera faces the creator who occasionally turns to point behind them, then turns back. Pedestrians pass behind on the right and left. Trams or cars cross at intersections.</motion>
<camera>Handheld selfie, 9:16 vertical, 24mm equivalent, focus hunting slightly between creator and background, realistic handheld shake.</camera>
<lighting>Available daylight. Time of day: {{time_of_day: morning golden hour, harsh midday, late afternoon}}. No fill light.</lighting>
<audio>Creator voiceover narrating what they see. Distant city ambience. A car horn. A street musician's violin.</audio>
<output>9:16, 24fps, 20 seconds, natural travel vlog color.</output>
<negative>no studio lighting, no filters, no music, no text, no professional stabilization, no tripod feel.</negative>
notes: Walking-backwards selfie is the standard travel vlog framing; it keeps the creator present while showing the place.
Pattern inspired by YouMind travel vlog prompt for Tokyo: https://youmind.com/prompts/video/social-lifestyle.
antiPatterns: Do not show a tripod. The handheld authenticity is the format.
Do not add slow motion or cinematic grade.

prompts.title: Quick fitness check-in reel
prompts.when: You need a 10 to 15 second fitness check-in shot for a daily accountability Reel.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are filming a quick before-workout mirror check-in in your home gym corner.</role>
<scene>A home gym corner: a wall-mounted mirror, a yoga mat on the floor, a kettlebell, a water bottle, a smartwatch on a small bench. Bright daylight from a side window.</scene>
<subject>The creator in workout clothes, holding the camera at face height in one hand, breathing slightly heavy from a warm-up.</subject>
<motion>Handheld selfie in front of the mirror. Creator smiles at their reflection, holds up two fingers in a peace sign, gives a thumbs-up, then turns the camera off-screen to reveal the mat and kettlebell behind them. Whole sequence in one handheld shot.</motion>
<camera>Vertical 9:16, 24mm equivalent, handheld, slight shake, mirror reflection visible in background.</camera>
<lighting>Bright natural daylight from side window. No ring light. Real shadows.</lighting>
<audio>Creator breathing audibly. A kettlebell set down. A water bottle open. Voiceover: 'Day one. Let's go.'</audio>
<output>9:16, 24fps, 12 seconds, natural indoor color, no filter.</output>
<negative>no other people, no music, no filters, no text, no professional lighting, no over-saturated color.</negative>
notes: Accountability content works because the low-polish look implies real effort. Adding filters breaks the contract.
The breath audio is the format's audio signature; without it the shot reads as posed.
antiPatterns: Do not request slow motion or dramatic lighting.
Do not add a workout montage. This is one short check-in.

prompts.title: Clean with me satisfying loop
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second satisfying cleaning loop for an ASMR-style Reel.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are filming a satisfying cleaning transformation in a real kitchen or bathroom.</role>
<scene>A real residential kitchen counter, initially cluttered with dishes, crumbs, and used utensils. Bright overhead light, white surfaces.</scene>
<subject>The cleaner's hands and arms, wearing a yellow rubber glove on one hand, holding a sponge and a microfiber cloth.</subject>
<motion>Overhead angle. The hands spray cleaner on the counter, wipe in smooth circular motions, lift each item, wipe underneath, return it. By the end of the shot, the counter is empty and gleaming.</motion>
<camera>Locked overhead phone or small tripod, vertical 9:16 or 1:1, deep focus, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Bright overhead LED panel, soft and even. No shadows.</lighting>
<audio>Spray hiss, sponge on counter, cloth squeak on wet surface, the soft clink of dishes being lifted. No music, no voice.</audio>
<output>9:16 or 1:1, 24fps, 18 seconds, clean bright color, slight saturation.</output>
<negative>no face, no other people, no text, no music, no cuts, no before/after split.</negative>
notes: Cleaning content has a built-in transformation arc: messy to clean. This satisfies viewer expectations and gives the model a clear before/after.
Pattern is a YouMind favorite for satisfying Reels: https://youmind.com/prompts/video/social-lifestyle.
antiPatterns: Do not show the cleaner's face. Hands-only overhead reads as more professional.
Do not add music. The audio of cleaning itself is the product.

prompts.title: Letterbox short film narrative
prompts.when: You need a 30 to 60 second festival-style short narrative arc in a single shot.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an indie director planning a single-take 45-second short film about a moment of decision.</role>
<scene>An empty city bus stop at night. Bench, route map, single streetlamp. Across the street, a lit apartment window on the third floor.</scene>
<subject>One character: a person in their 30s, simple coat, holding a single suitcase. They stand at the bus stop, looking across at the window.</subject>
<motion>Static wide for the first 20 seconds. The character looks up at the window. A silhouette appears in the window. They lock eyes. The character takes one step toward the street, then stops. They set the suitcase down. They walk away from the stop, toward the camera, and exit frame at the 40 second mark.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide, 35mm equivalent, deep focus from the bus stop to the distant window. Letterboxed 2.39:1.</camera>
<lighting>Single streetlamp as warm key on the character. Cool blue moonlight as fill on the building. Window glow is warm interior yellow.</lighting>
<audio>City ambience: distant traffic, one siren, a dog bark. A long sustained synth note. Footsteps on pavement become prominent in the final 10 seconds.</audio>
<output>2.39:1 letterbox, 24fps, 45 seconds, photorealistic, natural film grain.</output>
<negative>no dialogue, no other characters, no music, no text, no cuts, no camera movement, no slow motion.</negative>
notes: Single-take narrative shorts are a Sundance and Vimeo Staff Pick staple. The constraint forces clarity of story.
Forbidding cuts and music forces the model to use acting, eye-line, and audio to carry the narrative.
antiPatterns: Do not include dialogue. Subtitles or voiceover cannot be reliably synced in current models.
Do not break the single-take rule. Multiple shots become incoherent across generations.

prompts.title: Festival 30-second arc
prompts.when: You need a 30 second complete narrative arc with setup, beat, and resolution for a short film festival submission.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a writer-director compressing a complete story arc into a single 30-second shot.</role>
<scene>A child's bedroom at night, lit by a single nightlight. Toys on the floor. A small bed with rumpled sheets. Window showing a full moon.</scene>
<subject>A child of about 6, in pajamas, sitting up in bed, clearly just woken from a nightmare. Eyes wide.</subject>
<motion>Setup (0 to 8s): Child looks around the dark room, breathing shallowly, hugging a stuffed animal. Beat (8 to 18s): A shadow moves across the window. The child's eyes track it. The shadow stops. Resolution (18 to 30s): The door creaks open. A parent silhouette appears in the doorway, holding a glass of water. The child exhales, climbs out of bed, walks to the parent. They hug. Camera holds on the empty bed.</motion>
<camera>Locked medium-wide, eye-level with the bed. Deep focus from the child to the window to the door.</camera>
<lighting>Practical nightlight (warm amber). Cool blue moonlight from window. No overhead light.</lighting>
<audio>Shallow breathing. The creak of floorboards outside. A clock tick. The soft pad of parent footsteps. The exhale of relief. No music.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 30 seconds, photorealistic, warm but desaturated.</output>
<negative>no dialogue, no monster reveal, no text, no music, no cuts, no camera movement, no bright lighting.</negative>
notes: The 30-second arc with three named beats (setup, beat, resolution) gives the model a storyboard-like scaffold.
Ending on the empty bed (rather than the hug) is the lesson of every indie short film festival winner: leave the audience holding the feeling.
antiPatterns: Do not reveal the nightmare source. Imagination is more powerful than a monster on screen.
Do not add dialogue. Kids in single-take shorts with speech always feel uncanny.

prompts.title: Story within a single shot
prompts.when: You need a 20 to 30 second experimental short where the entire story plays out in one continuous frame with no cuts.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an experimental filmmaker telling a complete emotional story in one locked shot.</role>
<scene>A small theatre stage. Empty red velvet seats face a single wooden chair under a spotlight. A proscenium arch frames the shot.</scene>
<subject>One performer: an elderly woman in a simple black dress. She enters from the wings, sits, performs an invisible monologue with full emotion, then exits.</subject>
<motion>Locked shot. The spotlight is on. Empty stage for 3 seconds. The woman enters from stage right, walks to the chair at center, sits. She looks out at the empty seats as if performing for a full house. She begins an invisible monologue: her face shifts through joy, sorrow, anger, peace. After 18 seconds, she stands, bows to the empty seats, walks stage left and exits. Spotlight holds for 3 more seconds on the empty chair.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide, proscenium framing, 35mm equivalent, deep focus, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Single warm spotlight key on the chair. House is in darkness. Proscenium edges just barely visible.</lighting>
<audio>Silence except the soft footfalls on the wooden stage. The creak of the chair as she sits. A single barely-audible breath at the emotional peak.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 28 seconds, photorealistic, theatrical warmth.</output>
<negative>no dialogue, no audience visible, no music, no text, no other performers, no close-ups, no cuts.</negative>
notes: The 'story in a single shot' is a Cannes and Venice short-program favorite. Constraint drives creativity.
Empty stage framing mirrors the emotional theme of performing for an absent audience.
antiPatterns: Do not include audience reactions. The empty seats are the point.
Do not request close-ups. The locked wide is the format.

prompts.title: Time loop grocery store
prompts.when: You need a 30 second short film showing a character trapped in a repeating loop in a single location.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a director of magical realism visualizing a time-loop scene in a single setting.</role>
<scene>A neighborhood grocery store aisle, slightly dated. Shelves of canned goods, a checkout counter with a bored clerk, fluorescent overhead lighting.</scene>
<subject>One character: a person in their 30s, simple clothes, a half-full shopping basket.</subject>
<motion>The character walks down the aisle, picks up a can, examines it, puts it back. They walk to the counter, the clerk rings them up. The moment the customer takes the receipt, the scene loops: same walk, same can, same clerk, same receipt. Repeat 3 times. On the 4th loop, the character freezes, looks directly at camera, smiles knowingly, and drops the can. The can clatters.</motion>
<camera>Locked medium-wide from the end of the aisle, deep focus from the character to the clerk, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Flat fluorescent overhead. Slight flicker on the second loop. No shadows.</lighting>
<audio>The hum of the cooler. The beep of the register. The can landing on the floor at the end. No music.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 30 seconds, photorealistic, slightly desaturated.</output>
<negative>no dialogue, no text, no music, no cuts, no close-ups, no other customers, no visible camera movement.</negative>
notes: Time-loop shorts are a known festival-winning formula because the structure is clear and the model can repeat a learned motion.
The 'breaking the fourth wall' on the final loop is the visual punchline; without it, the prompt is just a scene.
antiPatterns: Do not try to show the loop reset visually. The audience should feel it from the repetition, not from a VFX cue.
Do not add text or numbers like 'Day 1, Day 2'. Trust the repetition.

prompts.title: Window-light monologue short
prompts.when: You need a 20 to 30 second dramatic short built around an actor in window light.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are directing a single-actor monologue short with no cuts.</role>
<scene>A small apartment living room. A single armchair by a tall window with sheer white curtains. A wooden floor, a small side table with a coffee cup, no other furniture.</scene>
<subject>One actor, 40s, simple grey sweater, sitting in the armchair. Reading a handwritten letter.</subject>
<motion>The actor sits in the chair, holds a folded letter. They unfold it. Their eyes scan the page. Their expression shifts from neutral to a slight smile, then to tears forming, then to a small laugh through the tears, then to quiet composure. They fold the letter, set it on the side table, look out the window, take one slow breath.</motion>
<camera>Locked medium shot, eye-level, 50mm equivalent, shallow depth of field with the curtains slightly soft behind the actor.</camera>
<lighting>Pure window light from camera-left. No fill. The right side of the actor's face falls into soft shadow. Curtains diffuse the light naturally.</lighting>
<audio>Silence except the rustle of paper being unfolded. The actor's controlled breathing. The barely-audible swallow. No music.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 25 seconds, photorealistic, warm but restrained color.</output>
<negative>no dialogue, no other characters, no cuts, no camera movement, no text on screen, no music.</negative>
notes: Window-light monologue is a film school classic. Single source, single actor, full performance arc.
Asking for the emotional sequence (neutral to smile to tears to laugh to composure) gives the model a clear performance brief.
antiPatterns: Do not request dialogue. Subtle emotion reads better than speech in current models.
