Do not ask for close-ups. The medium shot holds the window-light framing.

prompts.title: Handheld walk-and-confess
prompts.when: You need a 30 second dramatic short built around a walking-and-talking handheld take.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a director of an indie drama shooting a confessional walk-and-talk in a single handheld take.</role>
<scene>A long straight urban sidewalk at dusk. Brownstones on one side, parked cars on the other. Lampposts beginning to glow.</scene>
<subject>Two characters: Character A in a dark coat, walking; Character B in a denim jacket, walking alongside, slightly behind.</subject>
<motion>Handheld camera follows the two as they walk towards camera (which is moving backwards at walking pace). Character A speaks throughout, gesturing with one hand. Character B listens, occasionally nodding, occasionally looking away. By the end of the shot, Character B stops, says one line, and Character A stops too. Both stand still. Camera continues to move slowly backward away from them.</motion>
<camera>Handheld, 35mm equivalent, slight handheld shake, eye-level with the characters' chests (waist-up framing), no cuts.</camera>
<lighting>Dusk ambient. Warm sodium streetlamp top-light from above as they pass each one. Cool blue fill from the sky.</lighting>
<audio>Footsteps on concrete. Distant traffic. Subtle dialogue (transcript in caption for clarity). A moment of silence at the end.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 30 seconds, photorealistic, slightly desaturated dusk color.</output>
<negative>no music, no cuts, no text on screen, no other pedestrians, no professional stabilization, no over-stylized color.</negative>
notes: The walk-and-talk is a Sundance staple. The handheld behind-walk format is the easiest variation for video models.
Specifying the camera moves backward at walking pace removes any decision the model has to make about framing drift.
antiPatterns: Do not request multiple takes or angles. Single handheld is the format.
Do not request that the dialogue be lip-synced. Audio post goes in editing.

prompts.title: Short ending on still life
prompts.when: You need a 20 second contemplative short ending on a held still-life shot.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a director of contemplative cinema ending a short film on a held still life.</role>
<scene>A wooden kitchen table. On it: a half-drunk cup of coffee, a pair of reading glasses, an unopened letter, a single key. Window light from upper left.</scene>
<subject>No characters. The objects are the subject. Their arrangement tells the story.</subject>
<motion>Static locked frame for the full duration. The only movement: the steam from the coffee slowly rising and dissipating. A single beam of light drifts slightly across the table as a cloud passes outside.</motion>
<camera>Locked overhead angle at 45 degrees from the table corner, 50mm equivalent, deep focus on all objects, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Soft window light key from upper left, slow drift of a cloud shadow. No fill.</lighting>
<audio>Room tone. A clock ticking. Distant wind outside. The very soft creak of the house settling. No music.</audio>
<output>1:1 or 2.39:1, 24fps, 20 seconds, photorealistic, warm but desaturated.</output>
<negative>no characters, no text, no music, no cuts, no camera movement, no visible brand on any object.</negative>
notes: Still-life endings are how thoughtful shorts avoid the awkward 'final scene' problem. The objects hold the meaning.
Pattern inspired by Hirokazu Kore-eda and Kelly Reichardt endings.
antiPatterns: Do not animate any of the objects. The still life is the point.
Do not add a character hand entering the frame to take something. That ruins the held frame.

prompts.title: Vintage reel love letter
prompts.when: You need a 20 second experimental short combining vintage film look with a present-day subject.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a director of an experimental short about a memory that blurs past and present.</role>
<scene>A modern kitchen in soft daylight. A wooden chair, a counter with a kettle, a window with a small potted herb on the sill.</scene>
<subject>One character, female, 60s, wearing a plain apron. She is making tea.</subject>
<motion>Wide static shot of the kitchen. The woman pours water from the kettle into a cup. As she does, a soft white flash transitions the image to vintage 8mm film look, slightly desaturated, with rounded vignette and soft gate weave. She sits in the wooden chair, holds the cup with both hands. She looks directly at camera (the present viewer) and smiles. A second soft flash returns the image to clean modern digital. She is still sitting, still smiling. The kettle whistles once.</motion>
<camera>Locked medium-wide, 35mm equivalent, deep focus, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Natural daylight from the window, soft and even.</lighting>
<audio>Kettle whistle. The pour of water. A clock tick. The whistle returns at the end. No music.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 20 seconds, photorealistic with a mid-shot film texture change.</output>
<negative>no dialogue, no text, no other characters, no music, no cuts (only the flash transitions), no camera movement.</negative>
notes: Memory-flash transitions are a structural device that lets a single shot feel like two eras.
Specifying the flash as the only transition prevents the model from cutting away.
antiPatterns: Do not add dialogue. The smile is the dialogue.
Do not request multiple characters. The single subject is the bridge between past and present.

prompts.title: Waiting room suspense short
prompts.when: You need a 30 second suspense short built entirely in a waiting-room setting.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a director of contained thriller shorts with a single locked camera.</role>
<scene>A drab government waiting room. Plastic chairs along two walls, a vending machine humming in the corner, a reception desk with a sliding glass window, fluorescent lights, no windows.</scene>
<subject>One character, late 20s, simple business attire, sits in one of the chairs holding a numbered ticket. Eyes flicking to the reception window each time it slides open.</subject>
<motion>Locked wide for the duration. The character sits, watches the window. The window slides open. A clerk appears, calls 'Number 47'. The character looks at their own ticket. They stand. They walk to the window. As they reach it, the window slides shut. The character stares. The window slides open again. The clerk is gone. The character sits back down. They look directly at camera.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide from the corner of the room, deep focus across the room to the reception window, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Flat fluorescent overhead. No shadows. Slight flicker at the end.</lighting>
<audio>Humming fluorescent. The vending machine hum. The sliding window click. The clerk's voice through a speaker. A clock tick. No music.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 30 seconds, photorealistic, slightly green-tinted fluorescent color.</output>
<negative>no dialogue from main character, no text, no music, no other people, no cuts, no camera movement.</negative>
notes: Contained-thriller is a known short-film festival format. The constraint of a single location forces tension from performance.
The fourth-wall look at camera at the end implicates the viewer; the empty clerk window is the genre's classic reveal.
antiPatterns: Do not add background music. The fluorescent hum is the score.
Do not reveal what happened to the clerk. Mystery is the genre.

prompts.title: Magical realism dinner table
prompts.when: You need a 25 second magical realism short where an ordinary scene slowly turns impossible.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a director of magical realism where the impossible happens so quietly the audience questions their own eyes.</role>
<scene>A modest dining table set for one. A bowl of soup, a spoon, a glass of water, a folded napkin. Window light from the left.</scene>
<subject>One hand and arm, gender-neutral, rolled-up sleeve, enters from the right of frame. The hand picks up the spoon.</subject>
<motion>Locked static frame. The hand enters, picks up the spoon, dips it into the soup, lifts it. As the spoon rises, a second identical spoon slowly rises from the bowl behind it. The hand brings the first spoon to where a mouth would be (off-frame). The second spoon hovers. A third spoon rises. The hand lowers the spoon back to the bowl. All three spoons settle slowly back into the soup as if nothing happened. The hand withdraws from frame.</motion>
<camera>Locked overhead at 45 degrees, 35mm equivalent, deep focus across the table, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Window light from camera-left, soft and even, no fill.</lighting>
<audio>A spoon clinking on a ceramic bowl. The soft sound of liquid. A single bird outside. Silence between actions. No music.</audio>
<output>1:1 or 2.39:1, 24fps, 25 seconds, photorealistic, soft natural color.</output>
<negative>no dialogue, no other characters, no text, no music, no cuts, no visible face, no rapid magical effects.</negative>
notes: Magical realism in 25 seconds requires impossible actions that occur at the speed of normal life.
Three spoons, one hand, one frame is the entire budget of the impossible; no more, no less.
antiPatterns: Do not show the source of the magic. The realism comes from absence of explanation.
Do not request overt VFX. The illusion depends on ordinary motion.

prompts.title: 15 second product launch ad
prompts.when: You need a 15 second hero ad spot for a product launch across social and pre-roll.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a creative director for a 15 second product launch spot designed for social and pre-roll.</role>
<scene>A clean product stage: white seamless backdrop, soft gradient from pure white at top to very subtle grey at floor. No other props, no people.</scene>
<subject>The product: {{product_description: a single sneaker, a perfume bottle, a wireless earbud case}}. Centered in frame.</subject>
<motion>The product sits still for 3 seconds. Then a slow rotation begins: 360 degrees over 9 seconds. The rotation pauses at the hero angle (logo facing camera) for the final 3 seconds. The lighting does not change. The product does not move except the rotation.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod at product-level, 85mm equivalent, shallow depth of field, focus locked on the product.</camera>
<lighting>Single soft top-front key (large softbox), subtle warm rim from the back-right, no fill. Reflections visible on the product surface.</lighting>
<audio>Silence for the first 8 seconds. At the 8 second mark, a single synth chord swells and sustains through the end. Brand sting at the very end.</audio>
<output>1:1, 24fps, 15 seconds, hyperreal product still-life, no people.</output>
<negative>no people, no text on product, no background music before 8 seconds, no cuts, no second product, no visible price tag, no watermark.</negative>
notes: The 15 second product launch pattern is a creative agency standard: 3s hold, 9s reveal, 3s hero.
Silence at the start is a known attention tactic for mobile feeds where audio auto-plays muted.
antiPatterns: Do not include people holding the product. The product alone sells better in this format.
Do not request rapid cuts. The single rotation holds attention.

prompts.title: 30 second brand story arc
prompts.when: You need a 30 second brand story ad that builds an emotional connection to a product or service.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a creative director crafting a 30 second brand-story arc for a service business.</role>
<scene>A real small business at work: {{business_type: a neighborhood bakery, an independent bookshop, a family-run garage}}. Real setting, real equipment, real details.</scene>
<subject>The owner of the business, 40s to 60s, hands and face. No other people featured prominently. Authentic working environment.</subject>
<motion>Setup (0 to 8s): Hands of the owner working: kneading dough, shelving a book, tightening a bolt. Beat (8 to 18s): Pull back to medium shot showing the owner's face as they look up at the result. Resolution (18 to 30s): Final close-up on the finished product. Hold for the last 3 seconds.</motion>
<camera>Macro on the hands (0-8s), medium close-up on the face (8-18s), close-up on the product (18-30s). Three cuts, all locked or hand-held intimate.</camera>
<lighting>Available real-world light. Window light if indoor. Slight color warmth. No studio lights.</lighting>
<audio>Authentic sound of the work: dough being kneaded, book pages, wrench on metal. A soft piano begins in the resolution and sustains.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 30 seconds, natural brand-film color, slightly warm.</output>
<negative>no voiceover, no logo on screen (added in post), no other employees, no stock footage feel, no rapid cuts, no text.</negative>
notes: Brand-story arcs are the highest-converting ad format for small businesses because they build trust through authenticity.
The three-beat structure (work, person, product) is borrowed from documentary brand films for Patagonia, Levi's, and Apple.
antiPatterns: Do not add voiceover in the prompt. The voiceover and text go in edit.
Do not stack more than three shots in 30 seconds. Each beat needs 8 to 10 seconds to land.

prompts.title: App feature demo reel
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second app UI demo for a SaaS landing page or feature release.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a product designer creating an app UI demo loop for a landing page.</role>
<scene>A clean modern phone screen on a soft pastel background. The phone is centered, slightly tilted. No hands, no other objects.</scene>
<subject>The phone showing a fictional app called {{app_name}}. The UI has a top bar, a card with content, and a primary call-to-action button at the bottom.</subject>
<motion>The phone screen animates through a feature flow: a card slides in from the bottom, a check appears, a button pulses, the screen transitions to a success state, then resets. The loop seamlessly restarts at the end.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, vertical 9:16 or 16:9 framing, 50mm equivalent, focus locked on the phone screen, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Soft, even, top-down softbox light. The pastel background has a slight gradient.</lighting>
<audio>A subtle UI sound per action: a soft tap, a chime for success, a whoosh for the transition. No music.</audio>
<output>9:16 or 16:9, 30fps, 20 seconds, seamless loop, clean UI demo.</output>
<negative>no hands holding the phone, no other people, no text outside the UI, no music, no voiceover, no logos from real brands, no cuts.</negative>
notes: App demo loops are the standard landing-page hero. They loop seamlessly without obvious reset points.
Pattern inspired by Linear, Notion, and Arc app demo reels: clean UI, minimal environment, no human distraction.
antiPatterns: Do not show a person holding the phone. The phone alone is the subject.
Do not use real brand logos in the UI. Mock brands only.

prompts.title: Food sizzle hero shot
prompts.when: You need a 10 to 15 second food sizzle hero shot for a restaurant ad or menu feature.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a food DP and gaffer setting up a single hero shot of a plated dish being finished at the pass.</role>
<scene>A chef's pass in a real restaurant kitchen. Stainless steel counter, low ambient kitchen light from above, warm from the heat lamps.</scene>
<subject>The plated dish: {{dish_description}}. The chef's hands enter with a small copper pan and pour a hot sauce over the dish. The sauce hits and creates a visible steam plume.</subject>
<motion>Locked static frame. The dish sits on the pass. The chef's hands enter with a small pan and pour a sauce. Visible steam rises. The chef's hands withdraw. Hold on the dish as the steam continues.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod at table height, 50mm equivalent, shallow depth of field, focus on the dish, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Single hard key light from upper-left to make the steam visible. Warm rim from the heat lamp at right. Slight under-fill from the counter reflection.</lighting>
<audio>The hiss of the hot sauce hitting the dish. The sizzle. The kitchen ambience of distant pans. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 12 seconds, food-magazine color, slight saturation boost.</output>
<negative>no face, no text, no other chefs, no background music, no cuts, no visible kitchen signage.</negative>
notes: The sizzle-pour is the signature restaurant ad shot. The steam is the visual hook.
Specifying a hard key from upper-left is the lighting trick that makes steam read on camera.
antiPatterns: Do not include the chef's face. Hands-only reads as more premium.
Do not request background music. The sizzle is the audio hook.

prompts.title: Fashion runway walk ad
prompts.when: You need a 15 second fashion runway walk shot for a brand campaign or show opener.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a fashion show DP setting up a single hero runway shot for a brand campaign.</role>
<scene>An empty fashion runway at a minimalist show. White reflective floor, a single straight catwalk extending into the distance, soft front-row silhouettes barely visible on each side.</scene>
<subject>One model walks toward camera wearing {{outfit_description}}. Outfit is the subject. Hair and makeup are minimalist.</subject>
<motion>Locked static frame at the end of the runway. The model walks from the far end directly toward the camera for the full 15 seconds. Walks with editorial pace: confident, slow, eyes forward, one foot in front of the other. Final 3 seconds: model walks past camera and out of frame.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod at runway end, 85mm equivalent, shallow depth of field with soft front-row silhouettes blurred, focus tracking the model from far to near.</camera>
<lighting>Single hard front key from upper-mid (show lighting standard). White reflective floor bounce. No colored gels.</lighting>
<audio>The soft rhythm of heels on the runway. The muffled ambient sound of the show. A single sustained bass note underneath. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 15 seconds, high-fashion editorial color, slight desaturation.</output>
<negative>no audience faces visible, no other models, no text on the runway, no music with lyrics, no cuts, no camera movement.</negative>
notes: Pattern inspired by Dior, Chanel, and Saint Laurent single-shot runway opens.
The 85mm focal length is the fashion show standard; wide lenses distort the figure.
antiPatterns: Do not show the audience in focus. The blurred silhouettes are the genre's signature.
Do not cut to a back-of-model shot. The walk toward camera is the format.

prompts.title: Car reveal spin shot
prompts.when: You need a 12 second cinematic car reveal spin shot for an automotive ad or dealership reel.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an automotive DP setting up a single hero car reveal in a controlled environment.</role>
<scene>A black-box studio. The car sits on a circular turntable in the center. The floor is a dark glossy surface that reflects the car and lights. The backdrop is a seamless black void.</scene>
<subject>The car: {{car_description: a luxury sedan, a sports coupe, an electric SUV}}. Clean, polished, showroom condition.</subject>
<motion>The car sits on a slowly rotating turntable, completing one full 360 degree rotation in 12 seconds. The camera stays locked. The reflection rotates with the car.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 50mm equivalent, eye-level with the car's centerline, deep focus across the car and its reflection.</camera>
<lighting>Three-point car studio lighting: a strong soft key from upper-left, a sharp white rim from the back-right, a subtle warm underglow from the floor to define the wheel arches. No background light.</lighting>
<audio>A low rumble of an engine starting at the 2 second mark. A sustained synth pad. The very subtle mechanical sound of the turntable.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 12 seconds, hyperreal automotive color, deep blacks.</output>
<negative>no people, no visible turntable mechanism, no text on the car, no music with lyrics, no background detail, no white floor.</negative>
notes: The single-shot car reveal is an automotive advertising standard. The turntable does the work; the camera stays out of the way.
Specifying the floor reflection is the trick that doubles the visual presence of the car without showing the turntable.
antiPatterns: Do not move the camera. The rotating subject is the only motion.
Do not include people admiring the car. The car alone is the hero.

prompts.title: Tech product 360 orbit
prompts.when: You need a 15 second 360 orbit of a small tech gadget for a launch reel or product page.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a tech-product DP setting up a 360 orbit on a turntable for a gadget launch.</role>
<scene>A clean light-grey studio. The gadget sits on a small pedestal. Softbox overhead, white sweep background curving from floor to wall.</scene>
<subject>The gadget: {{gadget_description: a smartphone, a smartwatch, a wireless speaker, a camera}}. Centered, slightly elevated on the pedestal.</subject>
<motion>The camera performs a perfect 360 degree orbit around the pedestal at constant height and constant distance, completing one full orbit in 15 seconds. The gadget stays centered. Reflections track smoothly.</motion>
<camera>Geared slider on a circular track, 100mm equivalent macro lens, locked tripod height, no vibration.</camera>
<lighting>Single large softbox above and slightly in front. Subtle white card fill on the side opposite the softbox. The white sweep is the background.</lighting>
<audio>A clean soft synth pad that builds slowly across the orbit. A single chime at the 8 second mark when the logo faces camera.</audio>
<output>1:1 or 16:9, 24fps, 15 seconds, hyperreal product color, balanced exposure.</output>
<negative>no people, no text, no visible pedestal structure, no visible camera equipment, no music with lyrics, no cuts.</negative>
notes: The 360 orbit is the standard tech-product reveal for Apple, Samsung, and Sony launches.
Specifying the softbox above and slightly in front prevents the model from generating flat front-lighting that hides the gadget's shape.
antiPatterns: Do not change camera height during the orbit. Constant height is the format.
Do not include any other props or context. The gadget alone.

prompts.title: Travel destination tease ad
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second travel destination tease ad for tourism boards or travel brands.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a tourism-board creative director designing a 20 second destination tease.</role>
<scene>A single iconic or beautiful location: {{destination_type: an alpine lake at dawn, a tropical beach at golden hour, a historic European plaza at twilight}}. Real-world place. Sweeping landscape.</scene>
<subject>No human subject or only a single distant silhouette walking along the shore or the path. The landscape is the subject.</subject>
<motion>Sequence of three locked frames, each held for 6 to 7 seconds. Frame 1: Wide establishing shot. Frame 2: Medium-wide of the foreground detail (water lapping, birds taking flight). Frame 3: Wide of the destination again with the sky's color now shifted to peak color.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod for all three frames. Wide 24mm equivalent for the establishing, slightly tighter 35mm for the foreground, back to 24mm for the final frame.</camera>
<lighting>Natural light only. Time progression from early light to peak color across the 20 seconds.</lighting>
<audio>Natural ambience: water, birds, wind. A single soft piano note that begins at the 5 second mark and sustains through to the end.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 20 seconds, travel-magazine color, slightly saturated.</output>
<negative>no text on screen, no other people, no music with lyrics, no voiceover, no drone camera, no close-ups of food or drinks.</negative>
notes: Destination teases sell the feeling of a place, not the activities. Activities go in longer cuts.
Pattern inspired by Visit Norway, Tourism New Zealand, and Travel Alaska ad openers.
antiPatterns: Do not include people in close-up or activity. Distant silhouette only.
Do not request aerial drone footage in the same shot. Keep it ground-level and intimate.

prompts.title: UGC talking head testimonial
prompts.when: You need a 20 to 30 second UGC-style talking head testimonial ad for a product page or paid social.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a creator filming a talking head testimonial in your own home, phone propped on a stack of books.</role>
<scene>A real home environment: a kitchen counter background, a living room couch, or a home office. Visible everyday mess (a coffee cup, a plant, a stack of mail).</scene>
<subject>The creator, casual clothing, no makeup or light makeup, genuine smile. Looking at the phone camera as if speaking to a friend.</subject>
<motion>Locked phone selfie. The creator speaks naturally to camera with hand gestures, head tilts, and natural pauses. They pick up the product, show it to camera, set it back down, and finish their pitch. The whole sequence is one continuous shot.</motion>
<camera>Vertical 9:16 phone selfie propped at eye-level, 24mm equivalent, slight barrel distortion at edges.</camera>
<lighting>Available indoor light, possibly supplemented by a window. No studio ring light. Slightly under-exposed to feel candid.</lighting>
<audio>Voiceover of the creator speaking naturally about why they love the product. Soft room tone. No music.</audio>
<output>9:16, 24fps, 25 seconds, natural indoor color, no filter.</output>
<negative>no other people, no professional lighting, no music, no text overlays, no cuts, no visible studio setup, no tripod visible.</negative>
notes: UGC ads convert 2 to 3 times better than polished brand ads for direct-to-consumer products.
The visible mess (mail stack, coffee cup) is intentional; it signals authenticity and lowers perceived sales intent.
antiPatterns: Do not ask for a clean white background. It reads as an ad and reduces trust.
Do not request cuts or B-roll. UGC is single-take by definition.

prompts.title: Explainer motion graphic ad
prompts.when: You need a 20 second explainer motion graphic for a service business landing page.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a motion designer creating a 20 second explainer ad for a service business.</role>
<scene>A flat-color 2D animated world. Soft pastel background. Simple geometric shapes representing {{service_concept: insurance coverage, file backup, team chat}}.</scene>
<subject>No human characters. Iconographic representations: a shield with a checkmark, a cloud with an arrow, a chat bubble with three dots.</subject>
<motion>Setup (0 to 5s): A problem appears as a broken icon. Beat (5 to 12s): A simple animated solution appears: the shield slides in and repairs the broken icon. Resolution (12 to 20s): The repaired icon animates into a happy state and a clean word mark appears at the bottom.</motion>
<camera>Locked frame, 2D composition, no movement except the 2D elements.</camera>
<lighting>Flat color. No realistic lighting. Soft drop shadows under each element.</lighting>
<audio>A clean modern music bed at low volume. A subtle pop sound when each new element appears. A final chime at the word mark reveal.</audio>
<output>16:9, 30fps, 20 seconds, motion graphic, pastel palette.</output>
<negative>no live action footage, no human faces, no photorealistic rendering, no gradients in backgrounds, no more than 5 animated elements at once.</negative>
notes: Motion graphic explainers are the standard for B2B SaaS and fintech landing pages.
The problem-solution-happy state pattern is the standard 2D explainer arc.
antiPatterns: Do not add live action footage. The whole point is motion graphics.
Do not crowd the frame with more than five elements at once.

prompts.title: Anamorphic widescreen cinematic
prompts.when: You need the signature anamorphic widescreen look: oval bokeh, horizontal lens flares, and a 2.39:1 frame.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a DP selecting an anamorphic prime lens to shoot a wide cinematic scene.</role>
<scene>A city boulevard at dusk. Neon signs reflect off wet streets. Tall buildings frame both sides. A lone pedestrian walks along the sidewalk.</scene>
<subject>The pedestrian, mid-30s, dark coat, hands in pockets. The buildings and reflections are co-subjects.</subject>
<motion>Slow dolly forward on a slider, eye-level, 18 seconds. The pedestrian walks diagonally toward camera.</motion>
<camera>Virtual anamorphic prime lens (Panavision C-series equivalent), 2.39:1 aspect ratio, oval bokeh on background lights, characteristic horizontal blue lens flare when bright light enters frame.</camera>
<lighting>Practical neon sources only. Warm orange sodium streetlamps. Cool blue ambient from the sky. Wet reflections double the lighting palette.</lighting>
<audio>Footsteps on wet pavement. Distant city hum. A single soft synth note underneath.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 18 seconds, photorealistic, anamorphic lens character.</output>
<negative>no people close-up, no text, no logos, no spherical lens look, no narrow depth of field on subject, no music with lyrics.</negative>
notes: Anamorphic look is identified by oval bokeh and horizontal flares, not by aspect ratio alone. Specifying both is critical.
Pattern adapted from youmind cinematic scene prompts: https://youmind.com/prompts/video/cinematic-realistic.
antiPatterns: Do not request spherical lens flares. The horizontal streak is what defines anamorphic.
Do not crop to 16:9. The 2.39:1 letterbox is the format.

prompts.title: 35mm film grain look
prompts.when: You need a digital-clean scene rendered with classic 35mm film grain and gentle Kodak-style color.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are emulating the look of Kodak Vision3 500T shot on a Panavision Panaflex for an indie feature.</role>
<scene>A small diner in a small town at night. A counter with stools, a coffee machine, a pie display case. One customer at the far end of the counter. The cook behind the counter.</scene>
<subject>Two characters: the cook (50s, apron, tired eyes) and the customer (30s, trucker hat, holding a coffee mug). Their faces are the subject.</subject>
<motion>Static medium-wide framing both characters. The cook wipes the counter with a rag. The customer lifts the mug to drink. Slow, gentle pacing.</motion>
<camera>35mm spherical prime (Panaflex equivalent), 1.85:1 aspect, deep focus, slight handheld breathing but mostly locked.</camera>
<lighting>Practical diner lighting: warm overhead fluorescents, hot light from the pie case, cool moonlight from the front window. Mixed color temperature is the signature.</lighting>
<audio>The cook's rag on the counter. The coffee machine gurgle. A truck passing on the road outside. No music.</audio>
<output>1.85:1, 24fps, 15 seconds, photorealistic, 35mm film grain, slight color warmth, gentle halation on highlights.</output>
<negative>no digital cleanliness, no oversaturated color, no modern LED lighting, no text, no music, no cuts.</negative>
notes: 35mm grain emulation is the easiest film-look to fake in prompts because the model associates grain keywords with film rendering.
Mixed color temperature (warm practical + cool window) is the genre's signature lighting trick.
antiPatterns: Do not request a clean digital look. Grain is the whole point.
Do not stack modern LED sources. Stick to practical lighting.

prompts.title: Super 8 home movie look
prompts.when: You need a vintage 1970s home-movie aesthetic with the soft gate weave, warm color shift, and slight flicker.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are emulating a Super 8 home movie shot by a parent on a summer holiday.</role>
<scene>A family backyard on a bright summer afternoon. A trampoline, a garden hose, a BBQ grill with smoke, a dog lying in the shade.</scene>
<subject>No single character. Two or three children playing, blurred in motion, partially out of frame. The dog resting. The yard itself.</subject>
<motion>Loose handheld, wandering aimlessly. Pan left to the trampoline, pan right to the grill, tilt up to a tree. Gate weave throughout.</motion>
<camera>Super 8 frame (slightly soft at edges), 4:3 aspect, real Super 8 characteristics: gentle gate weave, soft focus, slight light leaks at frame corners.</camera>
<lighting>Bright daylight with high sun. Colors shift warm orange and slightly faded. Highlights bloom.</lighting>
<audio>Children laughing and shouting. A dog bark. The hiss of the BBQ. Distant lawnmower. Sound feels mono and slightly compressed.</audio>
<output>4:3, 18fps with gentle motion blur, 15 seconds, vintage Super 8 look.</output>
<negative>no digital clarity, no modern clothing, no smartphone era objects, no text, no music, no professional camera work.</negative>
notes: Super 8 prompts succeed when they specify the soft gate weave and color shift; without them, models default to clean digital.
Pattern inspired by Stranger Things and Bo Burnham: Eighth Grade home-movie nostalgia sequences.
antiPatterns: Do not request 24fps cinematic motion. Super 8 is 18fps with visible frame stutter.
Do not ask for steady camera. The hand-held is the whole point.

prompts.title: Found footage horror
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 30 second found-footage horror sequence with handheld camera and VHS-style degradation.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a found-footage horror director simulating a missing person's last video recording.</role>
<scene>An abandoned rural house, interior. Dusty furniture covered in sheets. A long hallway with closed doors. Single flashlight beam.</scene>
<subject>The person holding the camera: only their breathing and occasional voice heard. Their flashlight beam is the only light source, sweeping the room.</subject>
<motion>Handheld, shaky. The camera pans nervously across the room, then slowly down the hallway. The flashlight beam trembles. At the 12 second mark, the beam catches something at the end of the hall, the camera jerks away, and the footage cuts to black.</motion>
<camera>Handheld consumer camcorder, 16:9, slight rolling shutter wobble, real flashlight exposure (overexposed close, underexposed far).</camera>
<lighting>Single flashlight beam. Deep shadows everywhere else. No other sources.</lighting>
<audio>Shallow breathing. The voice says 'Hello?' once, weakly. Distant floorboard creak. The flashlight click as it shuts off at the end.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 15 seconds, slight VHS grain, low-light noise, photorealistic.</output>
<negative>no monster visible, no other characters, no music, no text, no cuts, no steady shot, no wide daylight.</negative>
notes: Found-footage horror works because the camera's fear becomes the viewer's fear. Specifying the flashlight beam is the lighting engine.
The genre's rule: never show the monster. Implication is scarier.
antiPatterns: Do not reveal the source of the fear. The unseen is the horror.
Do not use music. The breathing is the score.

prompts.title: Neo-noir detective scene
prompts.when: You need a 20 to 30 second neo-noir detective scene with venetian-blind shadows and a femme fatale silhouette.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a neo-noir director shooting a single locked detective office scene.</role>
<scene>A private detective's office, 1940s styling. A wooden desk with a green-shaded banker's lamp, a typewriter, a bottle of whiskey, venetian blinds on the window casting striped shadows across the room.</scene>
<subject>Two characters: the detective behind the desk, fedora on the desk beside him, cigarette in his mouth. A woman in a dark dress stands in the doorway, backlit so her face is in shadow.</subject>
<motion>Locked static wide for the full duration. The detective leans back, exhales smoke. The woman walks slowly from the doorway to the chair opposite the desk, sits, crosses her legs. They look at each other. The detective takes a drag. The woman lights her own cigarette.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide, 35mm, 1.85:1, deep focus, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Single banker's lamp key from desk. Venetian blind shadows from window as fill. The woman is backlit so only her silhouette is sharp. Smoke catches the light in beams.</lighting>
<audio>Typewriter in the next room, muffled. A saxophone playing somewhere in the building. The flick of a lighter. No dialogue. No music on the soundtrack directly.</audio>
<output>1.85:1, 24fps, 25 seconds, photorealistic, deep contrast, cigarette smoke visible.</output>
<negative>no dialogue, no bright lighting, no text, no other characters, no music with lyrics, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Neo-noir is defined by striped shadows and silhouetted figures. Both are easy for video models to render.
Pattern inspired by Chinatown, The Big Sleep, L.A. Confidential. Single locked wide with two figures is the genre's signature.
antiPatterns: Do not add dialogue. Noir is visual storytelling.
Do not light the woman's face. The silhouette is the mystery.

prompts.title: Handheld documentary realism
prompts.when: You need an observational documentary-style scene with realistic handheld, available light, and unposed subjects.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a documentary DP shooting an observational scene with a small handheld camera.</role>
<scene>A small independent bookstore in a working-class neighborhood. Real shelves, real customers browsing, the owner restocking at the back.</scene>
<subject>Three or four real-looking customers browsing, plus the owner in the background. No posed performance; subjects are mid-action.</subject>
<motion>Loose handheld camera that drifts from the front window, follows a customer to the poetry section, pans to the owner restocking, then settles on a window where a couple argues softly outside.</motion>
<camera>Handheld, 24mm equivalent, slight shake, real autofocus hunting, exposure breathing.</camera>
<lighting>Available light only: front window daylight, warm overhead bulb, cool fluorescent at the back. Real mixed color temperature.</lighting>
<audio>Real bookstore ambience: pages turning, the bell on the door, the owner coughing, the muffled argument outside.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 30 seconds, photorealistic, observational documentary color.</output>
<negative>no professional lighting, no posed acting, no music, no text overlays, no cuts, no tripod.</negative>
notes: Observational documentary works because the camera appears uninterested in its subjects. Specifying mid-action subjects prevents posed performances.
Mixed color temperature is the signature of available-light documentary shooting.
antiPatterns: Do not ask the subjects to pose or look at the camera. Documentary is observational.
Do not add music. Real ambient sound is the score.

prompts.title: Cinema verite kitchen scene
prompts.when: You need a tense cinema verite scene in a kitchen that feels like a true-crime documentary.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a documentary DP capturing a tense family conversation in a kitchen with restrained handheld movement.</role>
<scene>A modest residential kitchen. A table with the remains of a meal. Dishes, a pitcher of water, a single overhead bulb. Late evening.</scene>
<subject>Three family members at the table: a father, a mother, a teenage daughter. All three have expressionless faces. The daughter is the focus.</subject>
<motion>The camera starts on the daughter's hands folded on the table. It slowly tilts up to her face. She says nothing. The parents look at each other, then at her. The camera holds on her face for the rest of the shot. The overhead bulb flickers once.</motion>
<camera>Handheld, 35mm equivalent, very slight movement, focus on the daughter's face. Slow tilt-up at the start.</camera>
<lighting>Single overhead bulb as the only source. Hard shadows under the eyes of all three subjects. No fill.</lighting>
<audio>The scrape of a fork on a plate (early). Silence for the rest of the shot except the bulb's electrical buzz.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 25 seconds, photorealistic, hard overhead lighting, slightly desaturated.</output>
<negative>no dialogue from the daughter, no text, no music, no other characters, no soft lighting, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Cinema verite scenes work because the camera's restraint makes the silence louder.
Hard overhead lighting with shadows under the eyes is the genre's signature visual signal of emotional weight.
antiPatterns: Do not add dialogue. The silence is the dialogue.
Do not soften the lighting. The hard shadows are the point.

prompts.title: Vintage TV broadcast look
prompts.when: You need footage that looks like it was recorded off a vintage CRT television from the 1970s or 1980s.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a video artist simulating a vintage television broadcast from the late 1970s.</role>
<scene>A small-town local news desk set. Wood paneling, a blue screen behind the anchor, a state seal mounted on the wall, an American flag in the corner. Two anchors at the desk.</scene>
<subject>Two anchors in 1970s formal wear. Hair and makeup of the era. Reading from paper scripts, looking into the camera lens as if at a viewer.</subject>
<motion>Static wide of the news desk. The anchors speak (no audio speech needed). One anchor gestures to a graphic on the blue screen behind them. The other smiles and nods.</motion>
<camera>Studio camera, locked medium-wide, 4:3 aspect ratio (vintage TV), simulated CRT scanlines and slight color bleed.</camera>
<lighting>Bright even studio lighting, hot key lights from upper angles. Slight color shift toward warm yellow (vintage phosphor).</lighting>
<audio>News theme music under. The anchors' voices (generic news audio, no specific words). A paper shuffle.</audio>
<output>4:3, 24fps with simulated scanlines, 15 seconds, vintage broadcast look.</output>
<negative>no modern HD clarity, no widescreen aspect, no contemporary clothing, no text other than simulated graphics, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Vintage TV look requires the CRT scanlines and 4:3 aspect. Without both, the model defaults to modern broadcast.
Pattern inspired by David Fincher's Zodiac and Mindhunter period television inserts.
antiPatterns: Do not request widescreen. 4:3 is the format.
Do not request modern broadcast graphics.

prompts.title: 2000s DV camcorder footage
prompts.when: You need footage that looks like it was shot on a 2000s-era MiniDV camcorder for indie film or found-footage nostalgia.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are emulating a MiniDV camcorder recording from the early 2000s for a mumblecore indie film.</role>
<scene>A suburban backyard on a summer afternoon. A plastic pool, a sprinkler, a picnic table. Two friends sitting in folding chairs talking.</scene>
<subject>Two people in their early 20s, casual 2000s clothing, talking and laughing. Unposed, mid-conversation.</subject>
<motion>Loose handheld, walking toward the two friends from across the yard, then standing still and recording them. The two friends continue talking without paying attention to the camera.</motion>
<camera>MiniDV camcorder, 4:3 or 16:9 (depending on era), real autofocus hunting, real exposure pumping in the sun, the slight digital noise of consumer DV.</camera>
<lighting>Bright daylight with high sun. Slight overexposure on highlights. The DV look is slightly washed out with low contrast.</lighting>
<audio>Real backyard ambience: sprinkler hiss, distant lawnmower, kids shouting, the two friends' laughter.</audio>
<output>4:3 or 16:9, 24fps with DV-style compression, 15 seconds, photorealistic DV look.</output>
<negative>no HD clarity, no professional stabilization, no modern clothing, no text, no music, no tripod.</negative>
notes: DV camcorder look is identifiable by the slight washed-out color and visible digital noise. Specifying both prevents clean digital output.
Pattern adapted from YouMind 2000s DV Korean vlog: https://youmind.com/prompts/video/social-lifestyle.
antiPatterns: Do not request tripod stability. The handheld is the format.
Do not ask for modern color grading. The DV look is intentionally mediocre.

prompts.title: IMAX documentary look
prompts.when: You need an IMAX-scale nature or science documentary look with extreme resolution and color fidelity.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a documentary DP capturing a hero IMAX-scale nature shot for a feature documentary.</role>
<scene>An extreme wide of a mountain range in the Himalayas at sunrise. Snow peaks, deep glacier valleys, a single eagle soaring in the foreground.</scene>
<subject>The landscape is the subject. The eagle is a scale reference.</subject>
<motion>Static locked wide for 8 seconds. The eagle soars across the frame from left to right. Distant cloud movement on the peaks. Final 4 seconds: a slow, subtle pan following the eagle out of frame.</motion>
<camera>IMAX virtual camera (65mm equivalent), extreme sharpness from foreground to background, locked tripod then smooth geared pan.</camera>
<lighting>Pure natural sunrise. Warm side-light on the peaks. Cool blue ambient in the shadow valleys. No fill.</lighting>
<audio>Wind across the peaks. A single distant eagle cry. A deep orchestral note underneath in the final 4 seconds.</audio>
<output>1.43:1 IMAX aspect, 24fps, 12 seconds, hyperreal, deep depth of field.</output>
<negative>no people, no text, no music with lyrics, no foreground subjects dominating frame, no cuts.</negative>
notes: IMAX look is defined by extreme sharpness and depth. The 1.43:1 aspect (slightly taller than 16:9) signals the format.
Pattern inspired by nature docs by BBC Earth, National Geographic, and Disneynature.
antiPatterns: Do not stack multiple wildlife subjects. One scale reference is enough.
Do not request rapid camera moves. IMAX is steady and slow.

prompts.title: Fairy tale enchanted forest
prompts.when: You need a 15 second storybook enchanted-forest scene with bioluminescent flora and gentle magical light.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a fantasy DP shooting a storybook enchanted-forest establishing shot.</role>
<scene>A deep forest at twilight. Towering old trees with hanging moss. Ferns and toadstools on the forest floor. A small brook running through. Bioluminescent flowers and mushrooms glow softly in blues, greens, and pale pinks.</scene>
<subject>A single small fox with faintly glowing paw prints walking along the brook toward camera. The fox is curious, unhurried.</subject>
<motion>Slow dolly forward at the fox's walking pace, eye-level with the fox, deep focus. Magical particles drift slowly through the air like dust. The fox pauses once to drink, then continues.</motion>
<camera>35mm equivalent, deep focus, slight handheld breathing. Slow forward dolly.</camera>
<lighting>Twilight ambient as base. Bioluminescent flora as accent lights scattered across the scene. A single shaft of moonlight from upper right catches the brook.</lighting>
<audio>A soft wind. The trickle of the brook. A single magical chime every 4 seconds. No music with lyrics.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 15 seconds, storybook painterly realism, gentle saturation.</output>
<negative>no human characters, no text, no violence, no bright daylight, no modern objects, no harsh contrast.</negative>
notes: Storybook prompts succeed when they specify the bioluminescent palette. Without accent colors, models default to dark and murky.
Pattern inspired by Princess Mononoke and the Pan's Labyrinth forest sequences.
antiPatterns: Do not add human characters. The forest and fox carry the magic.
Do not request violent or threatening imagery. Fairy tale is gentle.

prompts.title: Dark fantasy brooding hero
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second dark fantasy hero shot with brooding atmosphere and stylized mood.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a dark fantasy DP capturing a brooding hero establishing shot for a feature.</role>
<scene>A fog-shrouded moor at night. Craggy rocks, dead trees, distant ruins. Mist flows low across the ground.</scene>
<subject>One hero in dark armor, tattered cape, helmet obscuring the face. Standing on a rock outcrop, looking toward the distant ruins. Sword pointed downward in one hand.</subject>
<motion>Static wide for the first 8 seconds. The hero stands still, cape and mist moving. The final 7 seconds: a slow push-in toward the hero's helmet. The camera ends on the visor slit.</motion>
<camera>35mm equivalent, deep focus, slight low angle to make the hero feel imposing.</camera>
<lighting>Single moon backlight rim from upper left. Cool blue ambient. Distant orange torchlight from the ruins. No fill.</lighting>
<audio>Wind across the moor. The creak of armor. Distant ravens. A low synth drone building through the shot.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 15 seconds, photorealistic with stylized dark color, heavy atmosphere.</output>
<negative>no other characters, no bright colors, no music with lyrics, no text, no fast motion, no modern objects.</negative>
notes: Dark fantasy hero shots rely on silhouette and fog. Both hide AI artifacts and create mood.
Pattern inspired by Dark Souls cinematics, Game of Thrones wide shots, and Witcher key art.
antiPatterns: Do not show the hero's face in detail. The obscured helmet is the mystery.
Do not add bright colors. The palette is monochromatic blue-black with a single warm accent.

prompts.title: Wizardry magical duel
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second wizard duel with energy blasts and shield spells, suitable as a fantasy action scene.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a VFX-heavy fantasy director shooting a duel between two robed mages in a ruined temple.</role>
<scene>A ruined stone temple at twilight. Crumbling pillars, shattered stained glass, magical glyphs glowing on the floor. Mist and dust fill the air.</scene>
<subject>Two mages: Mage A in red robes, Mage B in blue robes. Both hoods up, faces obscured. They face each other across the temple.</subject>
<motion>Locked wide. Both mages extend their hands. Mage A fires a red energy blast. Mage B deflects with a blue shield. The energy blast ricochets and explodes against a pillar. Mage B counters with a blue energy lance that Mage A dodges. Both raise their hands and the final clash explodes in a burst of white light. Hold on the empty temple.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide, 35mm equivalent, deep focus across the temple, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Ambient twilight base. Red and blue magical light dominates by subject. The final white burst is the climax.</lighting>
<audio>Wind. The hiss of magical energy. The boom of the final explosion. A deep synth swell. No dialogue.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 18 seconds, photorealistic with stylized magical VFX.</output>
<negative>no visible faces, no dialogue, no other characters, no text, no modern objects, no clean white lighting, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Wizard duel prompts succeed when they constrain the action to two opposing colors (red vs blue). It gives the VFX a clear identity.
Pattern inspired by Doctor Strange, Harry Potter duels, and the Wheel of Time trailer.
antiPatterns: Do not show the mages' faces in detail. The hooded silhouettes are the genre's signature.
Do not request specific incantations. The visual effects carry the magic.

prompts.title: Dream sequence surreal float
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second dream-sequence shot where physics bends in a controlled, painterly way.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a dream-sequence DP shooting a slow-motion float through a surreal interior.</role>
<scene>An impossibly large Victorian library. Bookshelves the size of buildings. Floating books drift slowly through the air. A chandelier hangs in the distance. A grand staircase spirals upward into fog.</scene>
<subject>One figure: a young woman in a simple white nightgown, eyes closed, floating in the center of the library as if pulled by gravity toward the ceiling. Hair drifts upward around her.</subject>
<motion>Slow-motion float. The figure rises gently. Books drift past. A feather falls upward (against gravity). The camera holds a static wide.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide, 35mm equivalent, deep focus. No movement.</camera>
<lighting>Warm candlelight from a hundred invisible candles. A single shaft of cool moonlight from a high window. Volumetric atmosphere.</lighting>
<audio>Silence except a single sustained celesta melody that plays very slowly. The sound of pages rustling in still air.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 18 seconds, painterly photorealism, soft focus edges, slow motion.</output>
<negative>no other characters, no text, no music with lyrics, no dialogue, no rapid motion, no modern objects, no bright daylight.</negative>
notes: Dream sequence prompts work by specifying impossible physics within a real architecture. The contrast is the dream.
Pattern inspired by Inception, The Fall, Tarsem Singh's The Cell.
antiPatterns: Do not add violent imagery. Dreams should feel gentle and strange.
Do not specify modern clothing. The timeless gown reads as dream-appropriate.

prompts.title: Mythological Greek scene
prompts.when: You need a 20 second mythological Greek establishing shot for a feature or game cinematic.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a DP capturing a mythological Greek hero shot for an epic fantasy feature.</role>
<scene>A marble cliff on the coast of ancient Greece. The Aegean Sea spreads below, glittering blue. Olive trees on the cliff. A marble temple partially visible in the distance.</scene>
<subject>One hero: a warrior in bronze armor with a red cape, holding a bronze sword pointed down. Standing on the cliff edge, looking toward the distant temple.</subject>
<motion>Static wide for 8 seconds. The hero raises the sword slowly to point toward the temple. Final 8 seconds: a slow push-in toward the hero from behind, ending on the sword tip pointing at the temple.</motion>
<camera>35mm equivalent, deep focus from hero to distant temple, slight low angle on the hero.</camera>
<lighting>Bright midday Mediterranean sun. Hard shadows on the marble. The sea reflects bright blue sky. No clouds.</lighting>
<audio>Wind from the sea. Distant seabirds. A single sustained lyre note. No dialogue.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 18 seconds, photorealistic ancient setting, warm Mediterranean color.</output>
<negative>no other characters, no text, no modern objects, no music with lyrics, no rain, no fantasy creatures.</negative>
notes: Mythological prompts work by specifying real architecture (marble, bronze) and real geography (Aegean, olive trees). The realism sells the mythic.
Pattern inspired by Troy, 300, and the original Clash of the Titans.
antiPatterns: Do not add fantasy creatures in the same shot. The hero alone against the landscape is enough.
Do not request overcast weather. Mediterranean sun is the genre's signature.

prompts.title: Elemental storm mage
prompts.when: You need a 15 second shot of a single mage summoning a localized storm with visible elemental VFX.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a fantasy VFX supervisor capturing a single elemental mage summoning a storm.</role>
<scene>A rocky plateau at twilight. Clouds gather above, dark and fast-moving. The mage stands on the plateau edge.</scene>
<subject>One mage in dark robes, hood up, one arm raised toward the sky. Lightning begins to arc between their hand and the clouds.</subject>
<motion>Static wide for the first 5 seconds. As the mage raises their arm, lightning begins to crack. By the 8 second mark, multiple lightning bolts arc. By the 12 second mark, a full localized storm forms above. Final 3 seconds: a single huge bolt strikes the plateau.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide, 24mm equivalent, deep focus from mage to clouds, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Mage is backlit by their own lightning. Ambient daylight dies. The lightning is the only key light by the end.</lighting>
<audio>Wind building. Thunder rolling. The crack of lightning. A deep synth drone. No dialogue.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 15 seconds, photorealistic with stylized storm VFX.</output>
<negative>no other characters, no text, no music with lyrics, no modern objects, no dialogue, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Elemental VFX is the most demanding fantasy category. Constraining to a single element (lightning) and a single character keeps the prompt achievable.
Pattern inspired by the Stormlight Archive covers, Final Fantasy XIV summoner cinematics, and Doctor Strange's lightning.
antiPatterns: Do not combine multiple elements (fire plus ice plus lightning) in one shot. Pick one.
Do not show the mage's face. The hood is the signature.

prompts.title: Celtic fairy ring
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second Celtic fairy-ring shot for a folklore-themed fantasy short.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a folklore DP capturing a mystical fairy ring in an ancient Celtic forest at twilight.</role>
<scene>A circular clearing in an old oak forest. A ring of pale mushrooms grows at the perimeter. The grass inside the ring is a slightly different shade, faintly glowing blue.</scene>
<subject>Small luminous orbs (fairy lights) drift slowly above the ring, hovering at varying heights. No human figures.</subject>
<motion>Static locked wide. The fairy lights drift slowly within and above the ring, occasionally darting and then resuming drift. A faint blue mist rises from the grass inside the ring.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide, 35mm equivalent, deep focus, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Twilight blue ambient. The fairy lights and glowing grass are the only key lights. Cool, gentle, mystical.</lighting>
<audio>Wind in oak leaves. A single sustained harp note. Distant birds settling. No music with lyrics.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 18 seconds, photorealistic with magical accents, deep blues and pale greens.</output>
<negative>no human characters, no text, no modern objects, no music with lyrics, no cuts, no bright daylight.</negative>
notes: Celtic folklore prompts work because the visuals (mushroom ring, fairy lights) are well-documented in art history.
Pattern inspired by Brothers Grimm illustrations and Pan's Labyrinth forest sequences.
antiPatterns: Do not add human characters interacting with the ring. The empty forest is the magic.
Do not request fast movement. The slow drift is the spell.

prompts.title: Arcane ritual circle activation
prompts.when: You need a 15 second arcane ritual activation shot for a horror-fantasy short or game cinematic.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a fantasy horror DP capturing a ritual activation in a candlelit stone chamber.</role>
<scene>A stone chamber beneath a ruined abbey. A chalk ritual circle on the floor. Black candles at the cardinal points. Old books and bones on a side table.</scene>
<subject>One hooded figure in dark robes, kneeling at the circle's edge, hands raised. The chalk symbols begin to glow red as the ritual progresses.</subject>
<motion>Locked overhead angle. The kneeling figure raises both hands. Symbols ignite one by one in sequence around the circle. By the 10 second mark, the entire circle is glowing. At 12 seconds, a column of red light erupts from the center. The figure is thrown back. Hold on the empty glowing circle.</motion>
<camera>Locked overhead, 24mm equivalent, deep focus across the circle, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Candlelight base until the activation. Then red magical light dominates. No other sources.</lighting>
<audio>Wind in the chamber. A low chant (no specific words). The ignition hiss of each symbol. The final column's rumble. No music with lyrics.</audio>
<output>1:1, 24fps, 15 seconds, photorealistic with magical VFX, deep red and shadow.</output>
<negative>no visible face, no dialogue, no text, no music with lyrics, no cuts, no bright daylight.</negative>
notes: Ritual activation works as a 15 second arc because the visual progression (one by one, then full circle, then column) is clear.
Pattern inspired by Evil Dead, Hereditary, and traditional occult art.
antiPatterns: Do not show the ritualist's face. The hood is the horror.
Do not reveal what was summoned. The empty circle is scarier than any creature.

prompts.title: Underwater kingdom fantasy
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second shot of a fantasy underwater kingdom for a feature or game cinematic.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a fantasy DP capturing an underwater kingdom from a single locked wide.</role>
<scene>The ruins of a coral-covered kingdom on the ocean floor. Marble columns toppled, statues half-buried in sand. Schools of fish drift through. Shafts of sunlight pierce the water from above.</scene>
<subject>No human characters. A single mermaid silhouette swims slowly across the frame in the background, behind the columns. Fish schools move with the current.</subject>
<motion>Static wide underwater shot. Light shafts move as the water surface moves above. Fish drift across. The mermaid silhouette passes slowly from right to left, then disappears behind a column.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide, 18mm equivalent, deep focus from foreground coral to background columns.</camera>
<lighting>Sunlight from above as the key. Cool blue ambient. Bioluminescent accents on coral and the mermaid's tail.</lighting>
<audio>Muffled underwater ambience. The slow whoosh of large fish. A single distant whale call. No music.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 18 seconds, photorealistic underwater, blue-green palette with shaft lighting.</output>
<negative>no bubbles from humans, no text, no modern objects, no bright daylight surface, no cuts, no camera movement.</negative>
notes: Underwater fantasy prompts work when the mermaid is a silhouette, not a face. Distance is the magic.
Pattern inspired by Aquaman, The Shape of Water, and the Atlantis Disney film.
antiPatterns: Do not show the mermaid's face in detail. The silhouette is the genre.
Do not request bright surface daylight. The depth is the world.

prompts.title: Glowing mystical portal
prompts.when: You need a 15 second glowing-portal shot for a fantasy transition or world-building sequence.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a fantasy VFX DP capturing a single static portal reveal in a hidden glade.</role>
<scene>A hidden glade surrounded by ancient trees with hanging moss. At the center, a stone arch carved with ancient symbols. The glade is dim and cool.</scene>
<subject>No characters. The portal itself is the subject: a vertical disc of swirling blue-white energy hovering within the stone arch.</subject>
<motion>Static wide. The arch stands empty for 3 seconds. A pinpoint of light appears at the center and expands into the full portal disc by the 8 second mark. The disc swirls gently for the remaining 7 seconds.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide, 35mm equivalent, deep focus from arch to surrounding trees, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Cool blue ambient base. The portal disc becomes the dominant light source by the end, casting blue-white light on the surrounding stones and tree trunks.</lighting>
<audio>Wind in the trees. A slow-building synth pad. The hum of the portal's energy. No music with lyrics.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 15 seconds, photorealistic with stylized portal VFX, blue-white palette.</output>
<negative>no characters, no text, no music with lyrics, no modern objects, no cuts, no rapid magical effects.</negative>
notes: Portal reveals work as transitions when the camera stays static and the portal does the work. The static frame makes the magic feel earned.
Pattern inspired by Doctor Strange, Stargate, and the fantasy portal trope.
antiPatterns: Do not show a character walking through. One-shot reveal is the format.
Do not request multiple portals.

prompts.title: Cyberpunk Tokyo night street
prompts.when: You need a 15 second cyberpunk Tokyo night street establishing shot for an anime or game cinematic.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a cyberpunk DP shooting an establishing shot of a futuristic Tokyo side street at night.</role>
<scene>A narrow side street in futuristic Shibuya. Tall buildings on both sides covered in holographic signage in Japanese and English. Wet asphalt reflecting every neon color. A noodle stall with red lanterns.</scene>
<subject>A lone figure in a long coat and dark visor walks away from camera down the middle of the street. No other characters clearly visible.</subject>
<motion>Static wide. The figure walks away into the distance. Holograms flicker. Rain falls in sheets. Neon reflections shift on the wet ground.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide, 24mm equivalent, deep focus, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Practical neon only: hot pink on left, cyan on right, amber noodle stall center. No moonlight. Deep shadows under awnings.</lighting>
<audio>Rain on metal. Distant city hum. The hiss of a noodle stall. A passing drone whirr. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 15 seconds, photorealistic cyberpunk, neon-saturated reflections.</output>
<negative>no other characters in close-up, no text on signs, no daylight, no cartoon style, no modern anachronisms.</negative>
notes: Cyberpunk Tokyo prompts work when the rain and reflections are specified. Without them, the neon reads as flat.
Pattern adapted from YouMind Tokyo neon motorcycle chase: https://youmind.com/prompts/video/cyberpunk-scifi.
antiPatterns: Do not show close-ups of the protagonist's face. The visor silhouette is the genre.
Do not add visible text on signs. Real signage deforms; abstract glyphs read better.

prompts.title: Futuristic Tokyo rooftop
prompts.when: You need a 15 second futuristic Tokyo rooftop vista for an anime-influenced cyberpunk short.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a cyberpunk DP shooting a Tokyo rooftop vista at night with a holographic city spread below.</role>
<scene>A flat concrete rooftop with antennae and a small ventilation structure. The Tokyo skyline extends in every direction, packed with neon signs, holographic billboards, and flying vehicle lights.</scene>
<subject>One figure in a sleek dark jacket and short hair stands at the rooftop edge, back to camera, looking out over the city.</subject>
<motion>Static wide. The figure stands still. The city pulses with light. Flying vehicles cross at varying heights. A giant holographic face briefly appears on a tower billboard in the distance.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide, 24mm equivalent, deep focus from rooftop to far skyline, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>City light pollution as the entire palette. Neon pink, cyan, amber, and white. No direct moon.</lighting>
<audio>Wind at altitude. Distant city hum. The faint whirr of passing flying vehicles. A single sustained synth note.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 15 seconds, photorealistic cyberpunk, deep contrast neon.</output>
<negative>no other characters on rooftop, no visible text on billboards, no daylight, no music with lyrics, no cartoon style.</negative>
notes: Rooftop vista is the cyberpunk equivalent of the western's cliffside hero shot.
Pattern inspired by Ghost in the Shell, Akira, and Blade Runner 2049.
antiPatterns: Do not show the protagonist's face. Back-to-camera is the genre.
Do not over-stack flying vehicles. Three or four are enough to imply a busy sky.

prompts.title: Dystopian megacity panorama
prompts.when: You need a 20 second dystopian megacity panorama for a feature or game cinematic establishing shot.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a dystopian sci-fi DP capturing a single vast establishing shot of a megacity under perpetual smog.</role>
<scene>A massive vertical megacity. Towers stacked on towers. Cable cars strung between buildings. Billboards. Flying cars. Smog sits between the layers. The ground level is barely visible through the haze.</scene>
<subject>No individual characters. The city is the subject. Tiny silhouettes of cable cars and distant flying vehicles give scale.</subject>
<motion>Slow vertical pan from street level up to the highest tower over 20 seconds. The camera never stops moving. At the end, the highest tower is visible above the smog.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod with a slow motorized vertical pan, 18mm equivalent, deep focus throughout.</camera>
<lighting>Diffused smog light. Neon accents at lower levels. A pale sun barely visible through the haze at the top.</lighting>
<audio>City ambience below: traffic, voices, machinery. Wind above. The hum of cable car cables. No music.</audio>
<output>9:16 vertical, 24fps, 20 seconds, photorealistic dystopian, smoggy yellow-grey palette with neon accents.</output>
<negative>no characters in close-up, no clear text on billboards, no blue sky, no music with lyrics, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Vertical pan from street to sky is the dystopian megacity signature. It communicates vertical scale better than any wide.
Pattern inspired by Blade Runner, Dredd, Elysium, and Alita: Battle Angel.
antiPatterns: Do not show clear blue sky. Smog is the genre.
Do not include close-ups of characters. Scale is the message.

prompts.title: Space station interior
prompts.when: You need a 15 second space station interior with zero-gravity hints and realistic NASA-style design.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a sci-fi DP shooting the interior of a working space station corridor.</role>
<scene>A cylindrical corridor inside a space station. Equipment lockers, handrails along the walls, soft blue-white LED lighting, a viewport at the far end showing Earth.</scene>
<subject>One astronaut in a standard EMU suit without a helmet, tethered by a single thin cable, drifting slowly down the corridor as if in zero gravity. Their hair floats.</subject>
<motion>Static wide. The astronaut drifts slowly from the far end toward camera. They use one hand to pull themselves along a handrail. By the end, they pass camera and out of frame. The viewport shows Earth rotating slowly.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide, 24mm equivalent, deep focus, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Soft blue-white LED strips along the ceiling. The Earth in the viewport provides a soft fill. No other sources.</lighting>
<audio>The hum of the life support system. A faint air handler. The soft tap of the handrail. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 15 seconds, photorealistic space station interior, cool blue-white palette.</output>
<negative>no aliens, no explosions, no music with lyrics, no bright daylight, no other characters, no rapid motion.</negative>
notes: Real space station interiors are visually documented (ISS, Tiangong). Prompting with realistic equipment beats generic sci-fi design.
Pattern inspired by Gravity, Interstellar, and The Martian space scenes.
antiPatterns: Do not include aliens or creatures. The realism is the genre.
Do not request explosions or action. Working interiors are quiet.

prompts.title: Alien planet surface vista
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second alien planet surface establishing shot for a sci-fi feature.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a sci-fi DP capturing a wide vista of an alien planet's surface from a stationary landing craft.</role>
<scene>A rocky alien plain under a violet sky with two moons. Strange crystalline rock formations rise from the ground. A thin orange atmosphere creates deep contrast on every surface.</scene>
<subject>One astronaut in a full pressure suit and helmet stands on a ridge, looking out over the alien plain. A small landing craft sits behind them.</subject>
<motion>Static wide for 10 seconds. The astronaut looks around. Final 5 seconds: a slow push-in on the astronaut's helmet visor, which reflects the alien landscape.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide, 24mm equivalent, deep focus from astronaut to far plain, slight push-in at the end.</camera>
<lighting>Two moons as cool blue-white sources from above. The thin orange atmosphere as ambient. No sun visible.</lighting>
<audio>Wind on the planet's surface (low and unfamiliar). The astronaut's breathing inside the suit. A single sustained synth note. No music with lyrics.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 18 seconds, photorealistic alien vista, violet sky with two moons.</output>
<negative>no aliens in close-up, no text, no bright daylight, no Earth-like sky, no music with lyrics, no rapid motion.</negative>
notes: Specifying the two moons and violet sky is what makes an alien planet read as alien without resort to creatures.
Pattern inspired by Interstellar's Mann's planet, The Martian, and Avatar's Pandora surface shots.
antiPatterns: Do not show alien creatures in the same shot as the astronaut. Mystery is better.
Do not request an Earth-like blue sky. The violet is the signature.

prompts.title: Neon hologram alley
prompts.when: You need a 15 second neon hologram alley shot for a cyberpunk short or ad.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a cyberpunk DP capturing a narrow neon-lit alley with floating holographic advertisements.</role>
<scene>A narrow alley between two cyberpunk buildings. Pipes and cables line the walls. Floating holographic signs in Japanese, Korean, and English drift at head height. Puddles on the ground reflect everything.</scene>
<subject>No characters. The alley and its holographic signage are the subject.</subject>
<motion>Static wide. Holograms flicker and rotate slowly. One sign glitches out and back. Puddles ripple from unseen drips. A faint mist drifts through the alley.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide, 24mm equivalent, deep focus from foreground puddle to background alley exit, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>All sources are neon and hologram: hot pink, cyan, lime green. No moonlight. The puddles double the palette.</lighting>
<audio>City hum from beyond the alley. The buzz of holographic projectors. Distant muffled music from a club. A single drip echo.</audio>
<output>9:16 or 16:9, 24fps, 15 seconds, photorealistic cyberpunk, neon-saturated reflections.</output>
<negative>no characters, no readable text on holograms, no daylight, no music with lyrics, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Empty cyberpunk alleys without characters are more achievable and atmospheric than character-heavy ones.
Pattern inspired by Ghost in the Shell and Blade Runner alley scenes.
antiPatterns: Do not add characters. The alley is the subject.
Do not include readable text on holograms; abstract glyphs read better at this scale.

prompts.title: Spaceship cockpit launch
prompts.when: You need a 20 second spaceship cockpit launch sequence for a hard science fiction short.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a sci-fi DP shooting from inside a realistic spaceship cockpit during launch.</role>
<scene>A spacecraft cockpit, two seats, large forward viewport, control panels with many small buttons and screens. The viewport shows a launch pad and sky.</scene>
<subject>Two pilots in flight suits. Both focused on panels and instruments. Pilot A in the left seat grips the throttle. Pilot B in the right seat calls out checklists.</subject>
<motion>Locked camera from behind both seats, looking past them toward the viewport. The shuttle launches. Through the viewport, the ground drops away, the sky darkens, and the curvature of Earth becomes visible. Final 5 seconds: the spacecraft is in orbit, Earth below.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide from behind the seats, 24mm equivalent, deep focus on both pilots and the viewport.</camera>
<lighting>Initially daylight through the viewport. As launch progresses, the light darkens. Cockpit instrument lights become the dominant fill by the end.</lighting>
<audio>Engine ignition rumble. The roar of ascent. The hiss of the atmosphere thinning. The sudden silence of vacuum as orbit is achieved. The pilot's voice calling out altitude.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 20 seconds, photorealistic sci-fi, documentary realism.</output>
<negative>no aliens, no fantasy elements, no music with lyrics, no text on screen, no other characters, no rapid cuts.</negative>
notes: Hard sci-fi works when the cockpit is realistic (seats, panels, viewport) rather than abstract. Realism sells the fiction.
Pattern inspired by Apollo 13, Gravity, and The Martian launches.
antiPatterns: Do not add fantasy elements (laser swords, glowing runes). The realism is the genre.
Do not request a soundtrack. The rocket sound is the score.

prompts.title: Mech suit hangar reveal
prompts.when: You need a 15 second mech suit hangar reveal shot for a mecha anime or Pacific Rim-style short.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a sci-fi DP shooting the reveal of a giant mech suit in its hangar.</role>
<scene>A massive military hangar. Catwalks at multiple levels. Cables hanging from the ceiling. Steam rising from vents. The mech stands in the center on a maintenance platform.</scene>
<subject>One giant humanoid mech, 15 meters tall, matte dark grey armor with subtle blue accent lights. A cockpit opens at the chest. No pilots visible yet.</subject>
<motion>Static wide from one end of the hangar for 8 seconds. The mech is dark. At the 4 second mark, overhead lights snap on one by one, illuminating the mech top to bottom. Final 7 seconds: a slow push-in from full wide to medium on the cockpit.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide, 18mm equivalent to capture the full hangar and mech. Smooth geared push-in for the final 7 seconds.</camera>
<lighting>Initially dark hangar with only emergency lights. As the overhead lights snap on, the mech is fully revealed in cool industrial light. Steam catches the light.</lighting>
<audio>Hangar ambience. The hiss of steam. The mechanical click of overhead lights engaging. A deep brass note swell. No music with lyrics.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 15 seconds, photorealistic mecha, industrial cool palette.</output>
<negative>no pilots, no explosions, no other mechs, no music with lyrics, no text, no cartoon style.</negative>
notes: Mech reveals work when the light reveal pattern (dark to lit) is specified. Models can lock the mech pose while lights come up.
Pattern inspired by Pacific Rim, Patlabor, and Gundam hangar scenes.
antiPatterns: Do not show the mech in action. The reveal is the format.
Do not include other mechs. One hero per shot.

prompts.title: Cyberpunk vendor stall
prompts.when: You need a 15 second cyberpunk street vendor stall shot for a night-market scene.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a cyberpunk DP capturing a single busy street vendor stall at night.</role>
<scene>A small vendor stall on a wet cyberpunk sidewalk. The stall has a corrugated metal roof, hanging lights, and a glass display case of unusual food items. A vendor in an apron stands behind.</scene>
<subject>The vendor, mid-30s, cyberpunk styling (subtle facial tech, dyed hair, layered jacket). They prepare an order with quick hand movements.</subject>
<motion>Locked medium-wide of the stall. The vendor moves behind the counter, prepares food with quick gestures, hands it to a customer off-screen right. Steam rises from the cooking surface.</motion>
<camera>Locked medium-wide, 35mm equivalent, deep focus across the stall and vendor, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Single hanging bulb over the stall as warm key. Cool neon signs in the background as fill. The cooking surface emits its own warm glow.</lighting>
<audio>The sizzle of the cooking surface. The vendor's hands tapping the counter. Distant city noise. A soft synth note.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 15 seconds, photorealistic cyberpunk market, warm key on cool neon palette.</output>
<negative>no other characters in close-up, no readable text, no daylight, no music with lyrics, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Vendor stalls are the most achievable cyberpunk scenes because the action is contained to a small area.
Pattern adapted from YouMind underground market courier: https://youmind.com/prompts/video/cyberpunk-scifi.
antiPatterns: Do not add multiple vendors in the same shot. One stall, one vendor.
Do not include readable text on the stall signage.

prompts.title: AI server room infinite
prompts.when: You need a 15 second AI server room shot for a tech-noir short or a cyberpunk feature.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a cyberpunk DP capturing a vast AI server room from a single locked angle.</role>
