<scene>A massive server room. Server racks extend into the distance with no visible end. Blue LED status lights on every rack. Cables run along the ceiling. A polished floor reflects the lights.</scene>
<subject>No human characters. The server room is the subject. A single floating holographic AI avatar (a slowly rotating wireframe face) hovers in the foreground at the 8 second mark.</subject>
<motion>Locked wide. The room hums. Status lights blink in slow waves. At the 8 second mark, the holographic face fades in at center frame and slowly rotates. By the 12 second mark, the face speaks (no audio needed) and fades out.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide, 24mm equivalent, deep focus from foreground avatar to distant server racks, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Blue LED status lights as the dominant palette. Cool white from overhead. No daylight.</lighting>
<audio>Server hum. Cooling fans. A single faint pulse when the avatar appears. The synthetic voice of the AI (no specific words).</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 15 seconds, photorealistic cyberpunk tech, deep blue palette.</output>
<negative>no human characters, no music with lyrics, no text, no rapid cuts, no cartoon style, no warm lighting.</negative>
notes: AI server rooms are visually iconic (CMatrix's Operator room, Ex Machina server room, Tron). The repetition of racks hides AI artifacts.
Pattern inspired by Tron: Legacy, Ex Machina, and The Matrix.
antiPatterns: Do not add humans interacting with the servers. The empty room is the message.
Do not request warm lighting. Cool blue is the genre.

prompts.title: Anime 90s shounen action
prompts.when: You need a 15 second anime shounen action scene with the classic 90s cel-shaded look.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an anime director working in classic 90s cel-shaded style for a shounen action episode.</role>
<scene>A ruined city street at sunset. Collapsed buildings, smoke, dramatic clouds. Two fighters face each other across a fifty-meter gap.</scene>
<subject>Two anime fighters: Fighter A with spiky hair and a tattered dark coat, Fighter B in light armor with a long sword. Both locked in aggressive stances.</subject>
<motion>Locked wide. Both fighters charge simultaneously. Mid-air clash with their weapons. A shockwave ripples outward, blowing debris. Both rebound and leap apart. Final pose: both fighters crouched, ready for the next exchange.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide, anime aspect ratio, slight low angle, deep focus. The motion is exaggerated: stretched frames, impact frames, motion blur trails.</camera>
<lighting>Sunset orange as key. Dramatic clouds. Cool blue ambient shadow. The clash point emits a bright white flash.</lighting>
<audio>Anime action audio: whooshes, metallic clashes, a deep bass impact on the clash. A short drum flourish at the impact. No dialogue.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 15 seconds, hand-drawn 2D anime, cel-shaded, sharp outlines.</output>
<negative>no photorealism, no 3D shading, no text, no dialogue, no Western animation style, no music with lyrics.</negative>
notes: 90s anime prompts succeed when 'cel-shaded' and 'sharp outlines' are explicit. Without these, models default to 3D anime.
Pattern inspired by Dragon Ball Z, Naruto, and Yu Yu Hakusho fight scenes.
antiPatterns: Do not request 3D rendering. 2D cel-shaded is the genre.
Do not add background music with lyrics. Anime uses instrumental tracks.

prompts.title: Anime Ghibli watercolor
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second Studio Ghibli-style watercolor anime scene for a gentle feature or short.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a Ghibli-style anime director shooting a gentle watercolor establishing shot.</role>
<scene>A small rural train station at sunset. A wooden platform, a single track, wildflowers swaying along the rails, lanterns beginning to glow. Distant green hills. A small steam train pulling in.</scene>
<subject>One child, 10 years old, simple clothes, satchel over shoulder, standing on the platform watching the train arrive. Their hair moves gently in the breeze.</subject>
<motion>Static wide. The train pulls in slowly, steam drifting back. The child's face shows quiet wonder. Wildflowers sway. Lantern light grows as the sky darkens by one shade.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide, anime aspect, 35mm equivalent, deep focus, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Warm sunset key from low and to the right. Soft pastel watercolor sky. Cool blue ambient shadow. Lanterns add warm pinpoints.</lighting>
<audio>Train whistle (distant, then closer). Steam hiss. Wind in grass. A single sustained piano note. No music with lyrics.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 18 seconds, hand-drawn watercolor anime, soft palette.</output>
<negative>no 3D rendering, no sharp digital outlines, no text, no dialogue, no photorealism, no music with lyrics.</negative>
notes: Ghibli prompts succeed when watercolor and pastel are specified. Default anime style is too sharp.
Pattern adapted from YouMind Ghibli train station: https://youmind.com/prompts/video/styles.
antiPatterns: Do not request 3D shading. Watercolor is flat.
Do not add violent or threatening elements. Ghibli is gentle.

prompts.title: Flat motion graphics explainer
prompts.when: You need a 15 second flat 2D motion graphics explainer for a SaaS or service landing page.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a motion graphics designer creating a flat 2D explainer sequence.</role>
<scene>A solid pastel background (light mint or pale coral). Flat geometric shapes: a circle, a square, a triangle. No gradients.</scene>
<subject>No human characters. The shapes are the subject, animated through a simple process.</subject>
<motion>The circle slides in from the left, the square from the right. They meet at center, combine into a hexagon. The hexagon pulses once. The triangle drops in from above and combines into a stylized icon. The icon holds for the final 3 seconds.</motion>
<camera>Locked frame. No camera movement.</camera>
<lighting>None. Flat colors only. Drop shadows under each shape.</lighting>
<audio>A short musical sting on each element's appearance. A chime on the final icon. Soft synth bed underneath.</audio>
<output>16:9, 30fps, 15 seconds, flat 2D motion graphics, pastel palette.</output>
<negative>no 3D rendering, no gradients, no live action footage, no human faces, no text beyond optional minimal labels.</negative>
notes: Flat motion graphics prompts succeed when 'flat' and 'no gradients' are explicit. Models otherwise add subtle 3D shading.
Pattern inspired by Google Material Design explainers and Headspace animations.
antiPatterns: Do not request gradients. Flat is the genre.
Do not request 3D. 2D only.

prompts.title: Painterly 2D animation
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second painterly 2D animated scene for an indie animated short or art film.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an indie animation director working in painterly 2D style inspired by European art films.</role>
<scene>A small cafe at dusk. Interior with a few tables, a counter, a window looking out at a rainy street. Warm interior light, cool blue street light.</scene>
<subject>One figure sitting alone at a table by the window, hands wrapped around a coffee cup, looking out at the rain.</subject>
<motion>Static wide. The figure sits still. Rain falls outside the window in painterly strokes. A single car passes on the street. The figure lifts the cup, drinks, sets it down, returns to looking out.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide, painterly aspect ratio, deep focus, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Warm tungsten from the cafe interior. Cool blue from the street outside. The window glass catches reflections. Painterly soft edges.</lighting>
<audio>The murmur of the cafe. Rain on glass. A single piano note sustained. No music with lyrics.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 18 seconds, painterly 2D animation, soft brush strokes, warm-cool palette.</output>
<negative>no 3D rendering, no sharp outlines, no photorealism, no text, no dialogue, no music with lyrics.</negative>
notes: Painterly 2D prompts work when 'soft brush strokes' and 'painterly soft edges' are specified. Without them, models default to clean anime.
Pattern inspired by The Breadwinner, Song of the Sea, and Studio Ponoc shorts.
antiPatterns: Do not request 3D shading. Painterly 2D only.
Do not request sharp outlines. Soft edges are the genre.

prompts.title: Stop motion claymation
prompts.when: You need a 15 second stop motion claymation sequence for a craft short or art film.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a stop motion animator directing a claymation sequence for an indie short.</role>
<scene>A miniature kitchen set, built from clay and craft materials. A small figure made of clay stands at a tiny counter.</scene>
<subject>One clay figure, roughly humanoid, made from a single color of clay with simple features (dots for eyes, a small mouth). Holding a tiny clay cup.</subject>
<motion>The clay figure raises the cup, looks at it, sets it back down. It walks two tiny steps to the right, picks up a small clay object, examines it, walks two steps back, sets it down. The classic stop motion frame-by-frame feel with tiny pose shifts.</motion>
<camera>Locked medium close-up of the clay figure, 35mm equivalent, deep focus across the kitchen set.</camera>
<lighting>Single soft warm practical light from upper left. Visible clay texture under the light. Shadows are deep but not harsh.</lighting>
<audio>Soft music box melody. The very subtle sound of clay being placed on a table (frame-by-frame quiet clicks). No dialogue.</audio>
<output>16:9, 15fps to feel stop-motion, 15 seconds, claymation aesthetic, soft craft lighting.</output>
<negative>no real human characters, no 3D animation, no photorealism, no text, no music with lyrics, no rapid motion.</negative>
notes: Stop motion prompts work when the 15fps feel is specified. Default 24fps reads as smooth animation.
Pattern inspired by Aardman (Wallace and Gromit), Laika (Coraline), and Rankin/Bass specials.
antiPatterns: Do not request 24fps. The 15fps stutter is the genre.
Do not request 3D rendering. Stop motion is the look.

prompts.title: Nature documentary creature hero
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second nature documentary hero shot of a single wild animal in its habitat.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a wildlife documentary DP following the National Geographic / BBC Earth format.</role>
<scene>A vast African savanna at golden hour. Tall golden grass, a single acacia tree, distant herd of wildebeest on the horizon.</scene>
<subject>One male lion, mature mane, walking slowly through the grass toward camera. Photorealistic anatomy.</subject>
<motion>Static locked wide for 10 seconds. The lion walks slowly toward camera through the grass. Final 5 seconds: a slow push-in from full wide to a medium on the lion's face.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 400mm telephoto equivalent for the wide, slight zoom in for the push-in, shallow depth of field with the grass soft around the lion.</camera>
<lighting>Golden hour side-light from the left. Warm key on the lion's mane. Cool blue shadow on the right.</lighting>
<audio>Savanna ambience: wind in grass, distant wildebeest calls, the soft breath of the lion, an ambient orchestral note underneath. No music with lyrics.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 18 seconds, photorealistic nature documentary, warm golden palette.</output>
<negative>no humans, no text, no voiceover in the prompt (added in post), no music with lyrics, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Nature doc prompts work when the telephoto compression and golden hour are specified. Both are BBC Earth signatures.
Pattern inspired by Planet Earth, Hostile Planet, and Dynasties.
antiPatterns: Do not request voiceover in the prompt. Voiceover goes in editing.
Do not add multiple animals in close-up. One hero per shot.

prompts.title: Behind the scenes craft documentary
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second behind-the-scenes craft documentary shot for a maker or artist reel.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a documentary DP capturing the process of a craftsperson at work.</role>
<scene>A small leather workshop. Wooden bench, tools hanging on the wall, scraps of leather, a window providing daylight.</scene>
<subject>One craftsperson, 40s, leather apron, hands scarred from years of work. Working on a leather wallet at the bench.</subject>
<motion>Handheld close-up that drifts between the hands working, the tools being picked up, and the craftsperson's face as they concentrate. The camera is observational, not staged. The craftsperson does not look at camera.</motion>
<camera>Handheld, 35mm to 85mm equivalent, shallow depth of field with focus on the hands, natural handheld shake.</camera>
<lighting>Window daylight as key from upper left. Warm tungsten practical bulb from the right. No fill.</lighting>
<audio>Workshop ambience: leather being cut, the tap of a hammer, the creak of the chair, distant birds outside.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 18 seconds, observational documentary, warm natural color.</output>
<negative>no other people, no voiceover (added in post), no text, no music, no staged looking at camera, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Craft documentary works because process footage is universally compelling. The maker's concentration carries the shot.
Pattern inspired by the Abstract Netflix series and short craft documentaries.
antiPatterns: Do not have the craftsperson look at camera. The observational ethic is the genre.
Do not add music. Real workshop sound is the score.

prompts.title: Talking head interview documentary
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second talking head interview shot for a documentary or testimonial piece.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a documentary DP shooting a sit-down interview in a real environment.</role>
<scene>The subject sits in their own real environment (their office, their workshop, their kitchen). Background tells us about them.</scene>
<subject>One subject, {{role_description: a doctor, a teacher, an artist}}, sitting and speaking directly to camera as if answering a question. Natural expressions and gestures.</subject>
<motion>Static locked frame. The subject speaks naturally. Hand gestures. Head tilts. Eye contact with camera. Slight shifts in posture. No other movement.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 50mm equivalent, eye-level with the subject, shallow depth of field with the background softly blurred.</camera>
<lighting>Single soft key from upper left (window or softbox). No fill. Background naturally falls slightly into shadow.</lighting>
<audio>The subject's voice (no specific words in the prompt). Room tone. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 20 seconds, natural interview color, slight warmth.</output>
<negative>no other characters, no text, no music, no professional studio backdrop, no harsh shadows.</negative>
notes: Documentary interview is the most reliable video format because the constraints are simple: one subject, one setting, one locked frame.
Pattern inspired by the New York Times Op-Docs and Vox interviews.
antiPatterns: Do not request a clean studio backdrop. The real environment is the genre.
Do not add music. The subject's voice is the audio.

prompts.title: Observational street life doc
prompts.when: You need a 20 second observational street life documentary shot for a city portrait or social piece.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an observational documentary DP shooting a long take of street life in a busy urban setting.</role>
<scene>A busy city intersection. Pedestrians crossing in multiple directions. Street vendors. Traffic. A small public square with a fountain.</scene>
<subject>No single subject. The street is the subject. Dozens of pedestrians going about their lives without awareness of the camera.</subject>
<motion>Static locked wide from a high vantage point (second-floor window equivalent). Pedestrians cross, vendors work, traffic passes, the fountain runs. A 20 second slice of city life.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide, 24mm equivalent, deep focus across the entire intersection, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Available daylight. Midday sun if outdoor, with shadows short and overhead.</lighting>
<audio>The full soundscape of the intersection: car horns, footsteps, conversations in fragments, vendor calls, the fountain.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 20 seconds, observational documentary, natural color.</output>
<negative>no staged action, no other cameras, no music, no text, no voiceover, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Observational street prompts work when subjects are explicitly unaware of the camera. Posed street life reads as advertising.
Pattern inspired by Frederick Wiseman's High School and the works of Jia Zhangke.
antiPatterns: Do not have subjects acknowledge the camera.
Do not add music. Real street sound is the audio.

prompts.title: Night shift workers documentary
prompts.when: You need a 15 second documentary shot of night shift workers for a labor or service piece.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a documentary DP capturing night shift workers at a real workplace.</role>
<scene>A 24-hour diner at 3am. A long counter, a few booths, the cook visible in the open kitchen. Three late-night customers scattered.</scene>
<subject>The cook, 40s, white apron stained with the shift, focused on the grill. Two waitstaff moving between counter and kitchen. The customers reading or staring into coffee.</subject>
<motion>Static wide from one corner of the diner. The cook works the grill. A waitress takes an order. A customer pays and leaves. Time passes quietly.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide, 24mm equivalent, deep focus, available light only.</camera>
<lighting>Practical diner lighting: warm overhead fluorescents, hot light from the grill, cool moonlight from the front window. Mixed color temperature.</lighting>
<audio>The sizzle of the grill. Coffee being poured. A coffee cup set on a counter. A distant jukebox playing softly. The clock on the wall ticking.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 15 seconds, observational documentary, warm practical lighting.</output>
<negative>no other characters, no staged looking at camera, no music, no text, no voiceover, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Night shift documentary is emotionally loaded by default. The quiet and labor carry the weight.
Pattern inspired by the Harvard Forest night-shift piece and short labor documentaries.
antiPatterns: Do not have workers acknowledge the camera.
Do not add music. The diner soundscape is the audio.

prompts.title: Dancer studio reel shot
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second dancer studio reel shot for an audition tape or social reel.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a dance videographer shooting a single studio reel shot for a dancer's audition tape.</role>
<scene>A clean dance studio with a wooden floor, a wall of mirrors on one side, soft overhead light. No other dancers.</scene>
<subject>One dancer, {{dance_style: contemporary, hip-hop, ballet, jazz}}, mid-20s, athletic build, wearing fitted dance clothes.</subject>
<motion>Locked medium-wide framing the full body. The dancer performs a continuous choreography of {{choreography_brief: turns, floor work, a leap, a controlled fall, recovery}}. The whole sequence is one continuous take.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod at the corner of the studio, 35mm equivalent, deep focus across the floor, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Even overhead soft panels. Slight warm fill from one side. The mirrors reflect the dancer and the lights.</lighting>
<audio>The dancer's footsteps on the wooden floor. The rhythm of their breath. A single sustained piano note if a music bed is desired. No music with lyrics.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 18 seconds, photorealistic, dance studio color.</output>
<negative>no other dancers, no text, no mirror reflections of off-camera people, no music with lyrics, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Dancer reel shots succeed when the choreography is described in motion verbs (turn, leap, fall, recover) rather than style labels.
Pattern inspired by YouMind hip-hop dance choreography: https://youmind.com/prompts/video/person-character.
antiPatterns: Do not request multiple dancers. The single subject is the audition format.
Do not request slow motion. Dance reels need real-time pacing.

prompts.title: Athlete training reel shot
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second athlete training shot for a fitness brand reel or social media.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a sports DP shooting a single athlete training shot for a fitness brand reel.</role>
<scene>An outdoor track on a clear morning. Red rubberized surface, lane lines, distant grandstand, fresh morning light.</scene>
<subject>One athlete, mid-20s, athletic build, wearing brand training gear, in the blocks for a sprint start.</subject>
<motion>Static medium-wide from low and slightly to the side (just behind the starting blocks). The athlete rises into the set position, holds, then explodes out of the blocks at full sprint. They run out of frame at the 6 second mark. The rest of the shot holds on the empty track with the athlete's footprint marks.</motion>
<camera>Locked low tripod, 35mm equivalent, deep focus from blocks to the 100-meter mark, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Clear morning sunlight from the side. Long shadows on the track. Bright blue sky.</lighting>
<audio>The shuffle in the blocks. The starter's pistol (off-screen). The explosive footfalls on the rubber. The hush after they pass. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 60fps, 18 seconds, sports-photography realism, slight motion blur on the sprint.</output>
<negative>no other athletes, no text, no slow motion, no music, no other sport equipment, no coaches visible.</negative>
notes: Sprint start is the most achievable athletic motion. Models can hold the set position and run the explosive exit.
60fps is specified to capture motion blur realistically for a sports look.
antiPatterns: Do not request slow motion. Real-time sprinting is the format.
Do not include other athletes. The single subject is the brand reel.

prompts.title: Chef finishing dish hero
prompts.when: You need a 10 to 15 second chef finishing a dish hero shot for a restaurant brand or food doc.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a food DP and gaffer capturing the final plating of a dish in a working kitchen.</role>
<scene>A pass in a busy professional kitchen. Stainless steel counter, hanging pans, warm service lights, the chaos of the kitchen blurred in the background.</scene>
<subject>The chef's hands (white chef's coat sleeves visible), plating the final touches of {{dish_description}}. Tweezers placing microgreens, a sauce quenelle, a final dusting of seasoning.</subject>
<motion>Locked close-up on the plate and the chef's hands. The hands work with precision and rhythm: place a garnish, drizzle a sauce, dust a powder, wipe the rim. Final 2 seconds: hands withdraw, leaving the finished dish center frame.</motion>
<camera>Locked overhead at 45 degrees, 50mm equivalent, shallow depth of field with focus on the plate, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Single hard key from upper left to highlight the dish's surface. Warm rim from the heat lamps. Soft fill from the kitchen's ambient light.</lighting>
<audio>The kitchen ambience: distant sizzle, the chef's breathing, the soft tap of tweezers on the plate rim. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 12 seconds, food-magazine color, slight saturation boost.</output>
<negative>no face visible, no other characters in focus, no text, no music, no cuts, no visible kitchen signage.</negative>
notes: Hands-only plating is more achievable and more cinematic than face-on chef shots. Models hold hand consistency better.
Pattern adapted from YouMind cooking reels and restaurant brand work.
antiPatterns: Do not show the chef's face. Hands only.
Do not add background music. The kitchen sound is the audio.

prompts.title: Musician instrument performance
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second musician performance shot for a music video insert or artist reel.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a music DP shooting a single musician performance shot for a music video insert.</role>
<scene>A small intimate venue, dim warm light. A single stool on a small stage. An audience silhouette barely visible in the deep background.</scene>
<subject>One musician playing {{instrument: acoustic guitar, piano, violin, cello}}. Mid-30s, simple dark clothing, hair natural, expression focused.</subject>
<motion>Static medium framing the musician and their instrument. The musician performs one continuous piece from beginning to end. Fingers move on the strings or keys. Body sways with the music. Eyes closed in concentration at the emotional peak.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 50mm equivalent, shallow depth of field with the background audience soft and dark.</camera>
<lighting>Single warm key from upper right. Cool blue rim from the back. The instrument catches the key light.</lighting>
<audio>The musician's performance of the actual instrument (no music bed needed). The room tone. The very soft breath at the phrase ends.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 18 seconds, intimate music video color, warm with deep contrast.</output>
<negative>no other musicians, no audience in focus, no text, no music bed (the performance IS the audio), no cuts.</negative>
notes: Single-musician prompts work when the performance is treated as the audio source. Models do not need a music bed.
Pattern inspired by Tiny Desk Concerts, NPR, and indie folk music videos.
antiPatterns: Do not add a backing track. The performance is the audio.
Do not show the audience clearly. The silhouettes are enough.

prompts.title: Fantasy character hero portrait
prompts.when: You need a 10 to 15 second fantasy character hero portrait shot for a feature or game cinematic.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a fantasy DP capturing a hero character in their signature environment.</role>
<scene>A {{fantasy_setting: a snowy mountain peak, a misty forest clearing, a desert oasis}}. The environment establishes the character's world.</scene>
<subject>One fantasy character: {{character_description: an elven archer in green leather, a dwarven warrior in bronze armor, a sorceress in dark robes}}. Heroic posture, confident presence.</subject>
<motion>Static wide for 5 seconds, then a slow push-in to medium close-up over the next 8 seconds. The character stands still, cape or hair moves in the wind, eyes locked on the camera (and thus the viewer).</motion>
<camera>Locked wide transitioning to medium close via smooth geared push, 50mm equivalent, shallow depth of field as the push-in progresses.</camera>
<lighting>Practical environment lighting: the snow reflects cold blue, the forest has dappled warm green, the desert has hot sun. A single warm rim from the side.</lighting>
<audio>Wind in the environment. A single sustained orchestral note underneath. No music with lyrics, no dialogue.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 13 seconds, photorealistic fantasy, slightly desaturated environment with character as the color.</output>
<negative>no other characters, no text, no music with lyrics, no dialogue, no modern objects, no bright daylight.</negative>
notes: Fantasy character prompts work when the environment is grounded (real mountain, real forest, real desert) rather than abstract fantasy locations.
Pattern inspired by Witcher key art, Lord of the Rings character introductions, and Skyrim character screens.
antiPatterns: Do not stack multiple costume elements (helmets plus capes plus armor). Pick one signature piece.
Do not show the character in action. The portrait is the format.

prompts.title: Person on camera talking head
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 30 second on-camera person shot for a YouTube intro, presentation, or talking-head segment.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a YouTube DP setting up a clean talking head shot of a presenter.</role>
<scene>A real room, not a studio: a home office, a living room, a small library. The presenter sits or stands in front of a wall or shelf that hints at their topic.</scene>
<subject>One presenter, {{presenter_appearance}}, wearing clean casual or business casual clothing, looking directly at camera as if speaking to the viewer.</subject>
<motion>Static locked frame. The presenter speaks naturally. Head tilts, hand gestures enter frame occasionally, slight shifts in posture. The shot is held for the full duration.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 35mm equivalent, eye-level with the presenter, slight background blur.</camera>
<lighting>Single soft key from upper left (window or ring light). A small warm practical lamp in the background as fill and visual interest.</lighting>
<audio>The presenter's voice (no specific words in the prompt). Room tone. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 25 seconds, natural YouTube color, slight warmth.</output>
<negative>no other people, no text, no music, no studio backdrop, no green screen, no visible studio equipment.</negative>
notes: YouTube talking head is the most common video format in the world. The key constraint is one subject, one setting, one locked frame.
Pattern inspired by MKBHD, Linus Tech Tips, and Vox presenter shots.
antiPatterns: Do not request a green screen or virtual background. Real environment is the genre.
Do not add cuts or b-roll. Single locked take is the YouTube talking head format.

prompts.title: Elderly portrait cinematic
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second cinematic portrait of an elderly person for a documentary or heritage piece.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a portrait DP capturing a cinematic portrait of an elderly person in their home.</role>
<scene>A real home filled with decades of objects. A favorite chair by a window, family photos on shelves, a small plant, reading glasses on a side table.</scene>
<subject>One elderly person, {{gender}}, {{ethnicity}}, late 70s or 80s, expressive lined face, comfortable clothing, hands visible doing a familiar activity (folding a napkin, polishing a frame, holding a cup).</subject>
<motion>Static medium close-up of the person in the chair. They perform their small activity. Their face is the subject: a slow smile, a glance out the window, a moment of thought. The whole sequence is unhurried.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 50mm equivalent, shallow depth of field with the home environment soft behind them.</camera>
<lighting>Window light from upper left as key. No fill. The lined face catches the light, casting natural shadows.</lighting>
<audio>Their breathing. The small activity sounds (fabric, glass). Distant clock tick. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 18 seconds, photorealistic, warm but desaturated.</output>
<negative>no other characters, no text, no music, no voiceover (added in post), no cuts.</negative>
notes: Elderly portrait prompts work because slow, small actions are reliable and meaningful.
Pattern inspired by Faces Places by Agnes Varda and the Up documentary series.
antiPatterns: Do not ask the subject to perform dramatic emotion. The quiet is the story.
Do not add a music bed. The room tone is the audio.

prompts.title: Child wonder close-up
prompts.when: You need a 10 to 15 second close-up of a child's face showing wonder or discovery for an emotional short.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a portrait DP capturing a single moment of wonder on a child's face.</role>
<scene>A real environment: a garden, a kitchen, a museum, a forest. The child is looking at something off-screen that we never see.</scene>
<subject>One child, 5 to 8 years old, simple clothing, hair natural, face unposed. Looking up and slightly to one side at something off-screen with an expression of wonder.</subject>
<motion>Static close-up of the child's face. The expression evolves: starts curious, then wide-eyed wonder, then a smile, then a small laugh. The whole sequence is held without cuts.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 85mm equivalent, very shallow depth of field with the background creamy soft.</camera>
<lighting>Soft natural light from the side of the child's face. Catch light in the eyes is essential. No fill.</lighting>
<audio>The child's breathing. A small gasp at the moment of wonder. The laugh at the end. Distant ambient sound. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 12 seconds, photorealistic, warm natural color.</output>
<negative>no other children, no text, no music, no modern objects unless relevant, no adult voiceover, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Child wonder prompts work because the off-screen subject keeps the viewer imagining. We never need to generate what they're seeing.
Pattern inspired by short documentary emotional portraits.
antiPatterns: Do not show what the child is looking at. Imagination is the format.
Do not add music. The child's sounds are the audio.

prompts.title: Couple walking holding hands
prompts.when: You need a 15 second couple walking shot for a wedding video, romance short, or lifestyle brand.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a romantic DP shooting a couple walking together for a wedding video or lifestyle brand.</role>
<scene>A real outdoor location: a beach at golden hour, a tree-lined avenue, a quiet city street at dusk.</scene>
<subject>One couple, holding hands. Simple coordinated clothing (no logos). The woman has loose hair, the man has short hair. Both mid-20s to 30s. Smiling, relaxed, in love.</subject>
<motion>Locked wide from the front. The couple walks toward camera at a relaxed pace, holding hands. They talk quietly to each other, glance at each other, smile. They walk out of frame at the end. Hand-held and intertwined.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 50mm equivalent, deep focus across the scene, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Golden hour side-light from the right. Warm key. Cool blue ambient fill.</lighting>
<audio>Their footsteps. Distant birds. Soft waves if on a beach. Their muffled laughter. No music.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 15 seconds, romantic warm color, slight halation.</output>
<negative>no other couples, no text, no music with lyrics, no modern logos, no rings in close-up, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Couple walking is a wedding-video staple. The key is consistent clothing and synchronized pace.
Pattern adapted from classic wedding videography and romance short films.
antiPatterns: Do not include dialogue. The couple's quiet laughter is the dialogue.
Do not request close-ups of rings or faces. The wide walk is the format.

prompts.title: Coworker office collaboration
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second B2B lifestyle shot of coworkers collaborating for a brand or HR piece.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a corporate lifestyle DP shooting a single collaboration moment for a brand piece.</role>
<scene>A modern open-plan office. A large shared desk, monitors, plants, sticky notes on a whiteboard, coffee cups.</scene>
<subject>Three coworkers gathered around a laptop. One points at the screen, one nods and takes notes, one leans in to look. All in smart casual clothing.</subject>
<motion>Static medium-wide from across the room. The three coworkers discuss, point at the screen, smile, one laughs at a comment. The whole sequence is one continuous observation.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 35mm equivalent, deep focus across the workspace, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Natural daylight from a window off-frame left, supplemented by soft overhead office panels. No harsh shadows.</lighting>
<audio>Office ambience: distant keyboard typing, a phone ringing softly, the muffled conversation of the three coworkers, laughter. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 18 seconds, natural office color, slight warmth.</output>
<negative>no other employees in focus, no text, no music, no face close-ups, no staged looking at camera, no cuts.</negative>
notes: B2B lifestyle prompts work when the coworkers look natural and unposed. Posed collaboration reads as stock footage.
Pattern inspired by Slack, Notion, and Asana brand case study videos.
antiPatterns: Do not have subjects look at camera. Observational is the genre.
Do not add music. Real office sound is the audio.

prompts.title: Extreme snowboard jump
prompts.when: You need a 15 second extreme sports snowboard jump shot for a winter brand or action reel.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an action sports DP capturing a single snowboard jump for a winter sports brand reel.</role>
<scene>A mountain terrain park. A large kicker jump, fresh snow, bluebird sky, distant peaks.</scene>
<subject>One snowboarder, mid-20s, neon winter gear, helmet, goggles.</subject>
<motion>Static wide from a low angle near the landing. The snowboarder enters from the left, hits the kicker, launches into the air, performs a single grab (method or indy), lands cleanly, rides out of frame. The whole sequence is one continuous action.</motion>
<camera>Locked low tripod, 24mm equivalent, deep focus from takeoff to landing, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Bright midday sun. Hard shadows on the snow. Vivid blue sky. The snow reflects pure white.</lighting>
<audio>The carve on the approach. The pop off the lip. The wind during the airtime. The crisp landing impact. The carve out. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 60fps, 15 seconds, action sports color, slight saturation boost on the snow.</output>
<negative>no other riders, no text, no slow motion (real-time is the genre), no music, no cuts, no other jumps.</negative>
notes: Single snowboard jump is achievable when the trajectory is clear: approach, pop, air, land, ride out. Avoid trick combos.
60fps is specified for realistic motion blur on the action.
antiPatterns: Do not request slow motion. Real-time is the action sports format.
Do not stack multiple tricks in one jump. One grab is enough.

prompts.title: Martial arts kata form
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second martial arts kata form shot for a martial arts brand or short film.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a martial arts DP capturing a single kata form for a dojo promotional video.</role>
<scene>A traditional dojo. Wooden floor, mirrored wall, white and red training gear hung on the wall, soft daylight from a high window.</scene>
<subject>One martial artist in a white gi and colored belt, mid-30s, focused expression.</subject>
<motion>Static wide from the side. The martial artist performs a single continuous kata (sequence of blocks, strikes, kicks, stances) from beginning to end. Each move is precise and deliberate. The form ends with a stillness and a bow.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 35mm equivalent, deep focus across the dojo floor, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Soft daylight from upper windows. Even, no harsh shadows. The gi catches the light cleanly.</lighting>
<audio>The gi's fabric movement. The breath of the martial artist (kiai at the strong strikes). The bow at the end. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 18 seconds, martial arts documentary color, clean and warm.</output>
<negative>no other martial artists, no text, no music, no opponents, no modern equipment, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Kata form is achievable because the motion vocabulary is well-defined. Each move has a clear pose the model can hold briefly.
Pattern inspired by martial arts tournament videos and dojo promotional reels.
antiPatterns: Do not request combat with an opponent. Kata is solo.
Do not add music. The kiai is the audio beat.

prompts.title: Race track finish sprint
prompts.when: You need a 15 second race finish sprint shot for a sports highlight or brand piece.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a sports DP capturing a single race finish moment for an athletics brand.</role>
<scene>A 400-meter outdoor athletics track. The home straight with lane lines. The finish line in clear view. Empty stadium seats in the background.</scene>
<subject>One runner, lean and athletic, in racing kit, approaching the finish line in a final sprint.</subject>
<motion>Static medium-wide from the side of the track, just before the finish line. The runner enters from the left, fully sprinting, crosses the finish line with arms slightly back in the lunge, decelerates, and slows to a jog out of frame.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 85mm equivalent, shallow depth of field with the stadium seats soft behind, sharp focus on the runner.</camera>
<lighting>Late afternoon golden hour sun from the side. Long shadows on the track. Warm and dramatic.</lighting>
<audio>The runner's footfalls. Their breath. The crowd roar as they cross the line (off-screen crowd). No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 60fps, 15 seconds, sports broadcast color, slight saturation.</output>
<negative>no other runners in the same shot, no text on screen, no slow motion, no music, no announcer voice, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Single runner crossing the line is more achievable than a full pack. The trajectory is unambiguous.
60fps for realistic motion blur on the sprint.
antiPatterns: Do not include multiple runners. The single hero is the brand reel.
Do not request slow motion. Real-time sprinting is the format.

prompts.title: Fight choreography dojo
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second choreographed fight scene for a martial arts short or action demo.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a fight choreographer and DP planning a single continuous fight exchange.</role>
<scene>An empty warehouse dojo. Wooden floor, hanging work lights, scattered gym equipment, dramatic side-light.</scene>
<subject>Two fighters: Fighter A in white gi, Fighter B in black gi. Both mid-30s, athletic build, controlled stances.</subject>
<motion>Locked medium-wide from one end. The two fighters face off. They circle briefly, then exchange a precise three-strike combination: Fighter A throws a jab-cross-hook, Fighter B ducks and counters with a body kick. Both reset, then Fighter A throws a spinning back kick that Fighter B dodges. They separate, bow, and walk out of frame on opposite sides.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 35mm equivalent, deep focus across the warehouse, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Hard side-key from upper left (a single work light). Cool blue ambient from the right. Hard shadows on the floor.</lighting>
<audio>The gi fabric on impact. The breath. The thump of the body kick. The bow at the end. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 18 seconds, action film color, slightly desaturated.</output>
<negative>no other characters, no blood, no real violence, no text, no music with lyrics, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Choreographed fights work when the sequence is short (3 to 4 moves) and ends with a reset. Models can hold the choreography if it's contained.
Pattern inspired by martial arts film behind-the-scenes fight choreography reels.
antiPatterns: Do not request blood or injury. Martial arts choreography is clean.
Do not stack more than four moves in one exchange. Models break down beyond that.

prompts.title: Parkour rooftop leap
prompts.when: You need a 15 second parkour rooftop leap for an extreme sports reel or action short.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an action sports DP capturing a single parkour leap across rooftops.</role>
<scene>Two adjacent urban rooftops at sunset. The gap between them is about 3 meters. HVAC units, antennas, and gravel on the roofs. City skyline in the background.</scene>
<subject>One parkour athlete, athletic build, fitted athletic wear, hair tied back.</subject>
<motion>Static wide from a rooftop angle that captures both rooftops and the gap. The athlete runs across the first rooftop, hits the edge, and leaps across the gap. They land in a roll on the second rooftop, recover, and run out of frame. The whole sequence is one continuous take.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod on a stable elevated position, 24mm equivalent, deep focus across the gap, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Sunset side-key. Long shadows on the gravel. Warm orange sky, cool blue city shadow.</lighting>
<audio>The footsteps on the gravel rooftop. The wind during the airtime. The thump of the landing roll. The recovery steps. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 60fps, 15 seconds, action sports color, slight saturation on the sunset.</output>
<negative>no other athletes, no text, no slow motion, no music, no other rooftops in close-up, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Single parkour leap is achievable when the trajectory is clear: run, jump, land. Avoid combo moves in one shot.
Pattern inspired by parkour brand reels and the opening chase of Casino Royale.
antiPatterns: Do not chain multiple leaps in one shot. One is the limit.
Do not add slow motion. Real-time parkour is the format.

prompts.title: Surfer wave ride
prompts.when: You need a 15 second surfer riding a wave for a surf brand reel or travel content.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a surf cinematographer capturing a single wave ride from a water-level angle.</role>
<scene>An open ocean wave breaking from right to left. Glassy water with the morning sun behind the surfer. Distant headland.</scene>
<subject>One surfer, wetsuit, shortboard, riding the wave face.</subject>
<motion>Static water-level shot from a position in front of the wave's breaking point. The surfer drops in from the right, carves along the face, hits the lip for a small off-the-top turn, continues down the line, and rides out of frame as the wave closes out. The whole sequence is one continuous ride.</motion>
<camera>Locked housing-mounted camera in the water, 24mm equivalent, deep focus, the wave breaking in foreground.</camera>
<lighting>Morning sun from behind. The wave glows translucent. The surfer is mostly in silhouette with rim light on the shoulders.</lighting>
<audio>The wave breaking. The surfer's breathing. The board on the water. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 60fps, 15 seconds, surf film color, deep blue water.</output>
<negative>no other surfers, no text, no slow motion (real-time is the genre), no music, no beach visible, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Single wave ride is achievable when the wave size is moderate (head-high, not massive). Bigger waves deform.
Pattern inspired by Moments Featuring Andy Irons and modern surf cinematographers.
antiPatterns: Do not include other surfers in the same wave. One hero per ride.
Do not request slow motion unless explicitly desired. Real-time surf is the genre.

prompts.title: Cyclist mountain climb
prompts.when: You need a 15 second cyclist mountain climb shot for a cycling brand or endurance sports content.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a sports DP capturing a single cyclist grinding up a steep mountain climb.</role>
<scene>A winding mountain road climbing through alpine forest. Switchbacks visible above. Distant snow-capped peak. Fresh cool air haze.</scene>
<subject>One cyclist in racing kit and helmet, on a road bike, low and aero on the drops.</subject>
<motion>Static medium-wide from the side of the road. The cyclist enters from the right, grinding up the climb at threshold pace. Their face shows effort. They pass camera and continue up the road. The camera holds on the empty road for the final 3 seconds.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod at the side of the road, 85mm equivalent, shallow depth of field with the forest soft behind.</camera>
<lighting>Filtered sun through the forest canopy. Soft warm key on the cyclist. Cool blue ambient in the forest shadows.</lighting>
<audio>The granny gear cadence. The chain. The cyclist's breath. Distant birds. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 60fps, 15 seconds, cycling film color, slight saturation on the green.</output>
<negative>no other cyclists, no text, no slow motion, no music, no cars, no announcer voice, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Single cyclist climb is achievable and emotionally loaded. The effort is the brand reel.
Pattern inspired by GCN (Global Cycling Network) and Tour de France cinematic moments.
antiPatterns: Do not include other cyclists in the same shot. The solo effort is the message.
Do not request slow motion. Real-time grinding is the format.

prompts.title: Climber crack climb
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second rock climber ascending a crack for an outdoor brand reel.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an outdoor adventure DP capturing a single rock climber ascending a crack.</role>
<scene>A tall granite cliff face with a single vertical crack system. Mountain backdrop, blue sky, distant valley.</scene>
<subject>One climber in a chalk bag, climbing shoes, helmet, using traditional crack technique (hand jams, foot jams, liebacking).</subject>
<motion>Static wide from the base looking up. The climber ascends the crack using a continuous sequence of jams and placements. They move up about 10 meters over the duration. Climbing rope trails below.</motion>
<camera>Locked wide from the base, 24mm equivalent, deep focus from the climber to the top of the cliff.</camera>
<lighting>Bright midday sun. Hard shadows in the crack. The climber's chalk catches the light.</lighting>
<audio>The climber's breathing. The scrape of shoes on granite. The click of protection being placed. Distant wind. No music.</audio>
<output>16:39, 24fps, 18 seconds, outdoor adventure color, slight saturation on the granite.</output>
<negative>no other climbers, no text, no music, no slow motion, no wide shot, no cuts, no talking.</negative>
notes: Single crack climb is achievable because crack climbing has a defined motion vocabulary (jam, place, step up).
Pattern inspired by Reel Rock and the Banff Mountain Film Festival.
antiPatterns: Do not include a belayer. The climber alone is the shot.
Do not request speed climbing. The slow burn is the brand reel.

prompts.title: Boxer heavy bag combo
prompts.when: You need a 15 second boxer working a heavy bag combo for a boxing brand or fitness content.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a boxing DP capturing a single combination on the heavy bag for a boxing brand reel.</role>
<scene>A real boxing gym. A heavy bag hanging from the ceiling in the center. Wraps hanging on the wall, a speed bag in the corner, mirrors, fluorescent overhead light.</scene>
<subject>One boxer, hand wraps and gloves, shirtless or in a tank top, focused expression. Athletic build.</subject>
<motion>Static medium-wide from the side. The boxer works a continuous combination on the bag: jab-jab-cross-hook-uppercut. The bag swings with each impact. The boxer moves around it, sets, repeats the combination. Final 3 seconds: a final hard cross that snaps the bag sideways, the boxer holds the pose.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 35mm equivalent, deep focus across the gym, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Single fluorescent overhead. Hard shadows under the eyes. Sweat catches the light.</lighting>
<audio>The impacts on the bag. The chain creaking. The boxer's breath and grunt. The final snap. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 60fps, 15 seconds, boxing gym color, slightly desaturated and hard.</output>
<negative>no other boxers, no text, no slow motion (real-time is the format), no music, no coach visible, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Single boxer on a bag is one of the most achievable sports motion shots. The trajectory is bounded.
60fps for realistic motion blur on the bag snap.
antiPatterns: Do not include a coach or trainer. The boxer alone is the brand reel.
Do not request slow motion. Real-time boxing is the format.

prompts.title: Diver plunge into pool
prompts.when: You need a 15 second diver plunging into a pool for an athletic brand reel or summer sports content.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a sports DP capturing a single dive from the platform for a diving brand reel.</role>
<scene>An outdoor swimming pool on a sunny day. A 10-meter platform at one end, blue water, lane lines, empty bleachers.</scene>
<subject>One diver, swim brief, athletic build, mid-20s.</subject>
<motion>Static wide from the side, framing the platform at top and the water entry at bottom. The diver stands at the edge of the platform, takes one breath, executes a single backward dive with one somersault and a clean entry (no splash). The camera holds on the entry ripple for the final 3 seconds.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod at deck level, 35mm equivalent, deep focus from platform to water, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Bright midday sun. Hard shadows. The water reflects pure blue.</lighting>
<audio>The single intake of breath. The platform spring. The wind during the fall. The clean entry sound. The underwater turbulence. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 60fps, 15 seconds, sports broadcast color, slightly saturated.</output>
<negative>no other divers, no text, no slow motion (real-time is the format), no music, no announcer, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Single dive is achievable when the dive is one move (one rotation, not three). Models hold trajectories well.
60fps for the water entry detail.
antiPatterns: Do not stack multiple rotations. One somersault is the limit.
Do not include a splash. Clean entry is the brand.

prompts.title: Ocean wave breaking
prompts.when: You need a 10 to 15 second ocean wave breaking shot for a travel reel or relaxation content.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a coastal DP capturing a single breaking wave from the beach.</role>
<scene>A clean ocean wave approaching a sandy beach. The wave forms a clean barrel before breaking. Golden sand in the foreground.</scene>
<subject>The wave is the subject. No people, no boats.</subject>
<motion>Static wide from the beach, low angle. The wave approaches, forms a barrel briefly, then breaks with white water rolling toward camera. The water reaches the wet sand and recedes. One wave, full cycle.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod low on the sand, 35mm equivalent, deep focus from foreground sand to background horizon.</camera>
<lighting>Late afternoon side-key from the left. The wave glows translucent. The white water catches the sun.</lighting>
<audio>The build of the swell. The crash. The sizzle of the white water on sand. The recede. No music.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 12 seconds, coastal photography color, slight saturation on the blue.</output>
<negative>no people, no boats, no text, no music, no filter, no rapid cuts.</negative>
notes: Single wave breaking is a perennial B-roll shot. Models can render water physics well at moderate scale.
Pattern inspired by coastline reels and travel content creators.
antiPatterns: Do not include surfers. The wave alone is the subject.
Do not request multiple waves. One full cycle is the format.

prompts.title: Mountain clouds rolling over
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second mountain clouds rolling over shot for a nature reel or opening sequence.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a nature DP capturing clouds rolling over a mountain ridge in a single continuous take.</role>
<scene>A sharp mountain ridge at sunrise. The peak catches the first sun. A thick cloud bank sits on the far side of the ridge, slowly flowing over.</scene>
<subject>The cloud flow is the subject. The mountain is the static reference.</subject>
<motion>Static wide from a distant viewpoint. The cloud bank slowly flows over the ridge, cascading down the far side like a slow waterfall. The flow continues throughout the shot. The peak remains visible above the cloud.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 50mm equivalent, deep focus across the ridge, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Sunrise warm key from the right. The cloud catches warm orange as it flows. Cool blue shadow on the mountain face away from the sun.</lighting>
<audio>Wind across the ridge. The muffled sound of cloud movement (a soft hiss). A distant bird. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 18 seconds, nature documentary color, slight warmth.</output>
<negative>no people, no text, no music, no rapid time-lapse, no other mountains in close-up, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Cloud-over-ridge shots are achievable because the cloud motion is smooth and continuous.
Pattern inspired by Timelapse Film Festival entries and nature documentaries.
antiPatterns: Do not request rapid time-lapse. The slow flow is the format.
Do not include people. The mountain is the subject.

prompts.title: Forest cathedral light shaft
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second forest light shaft shot for a nature mood reel or atmospheric B-roll.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a nature DP capturing a single shaft of light in an old-growth forest.</role>
<scene>A cathedral-like old-growth forest. Tall straight trunks, ferns on the floor, mist in the air. A single shaft of sunlight cuts diagonally through the canopy.</scene>
<subject>The light shaft and the mist within it are the subject. The forest is the container.</subject>
<motion>Static wide from the forest floor looking up at a slight angle. The light shaft holds steady. Mist drifts slowly through the shaft, glowing as it catches the light. Dust motes float in the beam.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 24mm equivalent, deep focus from foreground ferns to distant trunks.</camera>
<lighting>Single hard shaft of sunlight from upper right. Soft ambient blue from the rest of the canopy. The contrast is the entire mood.</lighting>
<audio>Forest ambience: distant birds, the soft hiss of wind in the canopy, the rustle of small animals in the undergrowth. No music.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 18 seconds, nature documentary color, deep greens and warm gold.</output>
<negative>no people, no text, no music, no other light sources, no rapid time-lapse, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Forest light shaft prompts work when the canopy is specified as old-growth (tall, straight, sparse understory). Modern forests lack the cathedral quality.
Pattern inspired by Planet Earth and Tree of Life.
antiPatterns: Do not include hikers. The empty forest is the message.
Do not request multiple light shafts. One is the genre.

prompts.title: Desert dune wind sweep
prompts.when: You need a 15 second desert dune wind sweep shot for a travel reel or atmospheric B-roll.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a desert DP capturing a wind sweep across a sand dune.</role>
<scene>A vast Saharan-style sand dune. The windward face shows rippled sand patterns. The leeward face drops into shadow.</scene>
<subject>The wind and the sand it carries are the subject. No people, no vehicles.</subject>
<motion>Static wide from the crest of an adjacent dune. Wind sweeps sand across the dune face in sheets. The rippled pattern shifts slowly as sand is added and removed. The leeward shadow edge stays sharp.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 35mm equivalent, deep focus across the dune face, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Late afternoon sun from the right. Warm key on the windward face. Cool blue shadow on the leeward face. The sand particles catch the backlight as a glowing stream.</lighting>
<audio>Wind across sand. The hiss of moving sand grains. Distant, empty. No music.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 15 seconds, warm desert color, deep contrast.</output>
<negative>no people, no vehicles, no text, no music, no other dunes in close-up, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Desert wind sweep is achievable as a single continuous motion. The visible sand grain particles give the shot scale.
Pattern inspired by Planet Earth deserts and Dune (2021) establishing shots.
antiPatterns: Do not include a single traveler. The empty desert is the message.
Do not request rapid wind. The slow sweep is the genre.

prompts.title: Aurora over arctic night
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second aurora over arctic night shot for a nature reel or travel brand.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an arctic DP capturing the aurora borealis in a single continuous shot.</role>
<scene>A flat arctic tundra at night. Snow-covered ground. A single small cabin with a warm light in the window in the mid-ground. Distant mountain silhouette on the horizon.</scene>
<subject>The aurora is the subject. The cabin and the mountains are the static references.</subject>
<motion>Static wide looking north. The aurora ribbons drift slowly across the sky from right to left, glowing green with hints of pink at the edges. The cabin window stays lit. Stars are visible.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 24mm equivalent, deep focus from foreground snow to distant mountains.</camera>
<lighting>Starlight and aurora as the sky sources. The cabin window as a single warm anchor point. Snow reflects the aurora's green faintly.</lighting>
<audio>Arctic silence. A faint wind. A distant dog bark. The very low crackle of cold air. No music.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 18 seconds, cold arctic color, deep greens and blues.</output>
<negative>no people, no text, no music, no other lights, no rapid time-lapse, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Aurora prompts succeed when the cabin or other human-scale anchor is included. Scale sells the sky.
Pattern inspired by northern Norway aurora tourism reels and BBC Frozen Planet.
antiPatterns: Do not include people looking at the aurora. The cabin window is enough.
Do not request rapid time-lapse. The slow drift is the genre.

prompts.title: Wildlife elephant herd drinking
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second wildlife shot of an elephant herd at a watering hole for a nature doc.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a wildlife DP capturing a small elephant herd at a watering hole at golden hour.</role>
<scene>An African savanna at golden hour. A watering hole in the foreground. Acacia trees on the horizon. The light is warm and low.</scene>
<subject>A small herd of five elephants: two adults, one juvenile, two calves. Drinking at the watering hole.</subject>
<motion>Static wide from across the watering hole. The elephants drink with their trunks. One calf plays in the shallow water. An adult gently touches the calf with its trunk. The herd continues drinking throughout the shot.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod at a safe distance, 200mm equivalent, shallow depth of field with the foreground grass soft.</camera>
<lighting>Golden hour side-key from the right. Warm key on the elephants. Cool blue shadow on their left sides. The water reflects the warm sky.</lighting>
<audio>The water sounds of elephants drinking. Distant savanna birds. A low sustained orchestral note underneath. No music with lyrics.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 18 seconds, wildlife documentary color, warm golden palette.</output>
<negative>no other animals, no humans, no text, no voiceover (added in post), no music with lyrics, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Elephant herd drinking is achievable because the motion vocabulary is simple (trunk, water, drinking).
Pattern inspired by BBC Dynasties and Disneynature African Cats.
antiPatterns: Do not include predators. The calm drinking is the format.
Do not request running or charge. Stillness is the genre.

prompts.title: Weather lightning storm at distance
prompts.when: You need a 15 second distant lightning storm shot for an atmospheric reel or weather doc.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a weather DP capturing a distant lightning storm from a safe observation point.</role>
<scene>A flat prairie at night. The horizon in the distance shows a thunderstorm cell with internal lightning. Stars visible above. A lone road disappears into the storm.</scene>
<subject>The lightning is the subject. The storm cell, road, and stars are the context.</subject>
<motion>Static wide looking toward the storm. Internal lightning illuminates the cloud structure intermittently. Each flash reveals the cloud shape for a fraction of a second. The storm drifts slowly left to right.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 24mm equivalent, deep focus from the road to the storm.</camera>
<lighting>The lightning is the dominant source when it fires. Starlight as ambient. The road is unlit.</lighting>
<audio>Distant thunder rolling. Wind across the prairie. Crickets. No music.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 15 seconds, weather documentary color, deep blues and lightning whites.</output>
<negative>no people, no text, no music, no close lightning, no other structures, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Distant lightning works because the storm is far away. Close lightning forces specific shapes that models cannot reliably render.
Pattern inspired by storm-chasing documentaries and Tornado Alley footage.
antiPatterns: Do not show close-up lightning. Distance is the format.
Do not include chasers. The empty road is the message.

prompts.title: Waterfall long exposure look
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second waterfall shot with the smooth silk-water long exposure look.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a nature DP capturing a single waterfall with a long exposure aesthetic.</role>
<scene>A medium waterfall in a mossy forest. The water drops about 10 meters into a shallow pool. Ferns and moss on the surrounding rocks.</scene>
<subject>The waterfall is the subject. The water is silk-smooth as if long-exposed.</subject>
<motion>Static wide from the pool level. The water falls continuously in a smooth silk-like flow. Mist rises slowly from the pool. The water is uniform and ethereal throughout.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 24mm equivalent, deep focus across the waterfall, no movement.</camera>
<lighting>Soft diffused forest light. The waterfall catches soft highlights. The pool reflects the green canopy.</lighting>
<audio>The smooth roar of the falling water. Forest ambience: distant birds, the rustle of leaves. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 18 seconds, nature documentary color, deep greens and white water.</output>
<negative>no people, no text, no music, no other waterfalls, no rapid water flow, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Silk-water long exposure look is achievable in video prompts because the smooth flow is easier than turbulent.
Pattern inspired by long exposure landscape photography and Planet Earth waterfalls.
antiPatterns: Do not show people swimming or playing. The empty forest is the message.
Do not request fast shutter water. Silk is the format.

prompts.title: Volcano eruption night glow
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second volcano eruption night shot for a nature epic or doc sequence.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a volcano DP capturing a distant lava flow from a safe observation point at night.</role>
<scene>A volcano cone at night. Active lava flow visible on the flank. Stars above. The sky has a faint orange glow from the eruption.</scene>
<subject>The lava flow is the subject. The cone and the sky glow are the context.</subject>
<motion>Static wide from a safe distance. The lava flow moves slowly down the volcano flank, glowing bright orange-red. A small lava lobe breaks off and falls. Faint smoke rises from the flow.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod at a safe distance, 50mm equivalent, deep focus across the cone.</camera>
<lighting>The lava is the dominant key. The orange glow tints the smoke and the surrounding ground. The sky has a faint warm haze.</lighting>
<audio>Distant deep rumble. The crackle of the lava flow. Wind. No music.</audio>
<output>2.39:1, 24fps, 18 seconds, volcanic color, deep oranges against dark sky.</output>
<negative>no people, no text, no music, no vehicles, no flying helicopters, no close lava, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Distant volcano shots work because the lava flow is a single coherent motion. Close-up lava deforms.
Pattern inspired by BBC Planet Earth volcanic episodes and National Geographic volcano docs.
antiPatterns: Do not show helicopters or tourists. The empty viewpoint is the message.
Do not request close-up lava details. Distance is the format.

prompts.title: Meadow wildflower breeze
prompts.when: You need a 15 second peaceful meadow wildflower shot for a relaxation or nature reel.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a nature DP capturing a peaceful wildflower meadow in a gentle breeze.</role>
<scene>An alpine meadow in full wildflower bloom. Yellow, purple, white, and red flowers stretching toward distant tree-covered hills. Blue sky with one or two clouds.</scene>
<subject>The wildflowers and the breeze are the subject. No people, no structures.</subject>
<motion>Static wide from the meadow's edge. The breeze moves continuously across the meadow, rippling the flowers in waves. Butterflies and bees move through the foreground occasionally.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 35mm equivalent, deep focus across the meadow to distant hills.</camera>
<lighting>Bright midday sun. Hard light on the petals. Soft shadows between the flowers. The colors are vivid.</lighting>
<audio>Meadow ambience: distant birds, the soft buzz of bees, the rustle of flowers in the wind. A single sustained flute note. No music with lyrics.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 15 seconds, nature documentary color, vivid and warm.</output>
<negative>no people, no structures, no text, no music with lyrics, no other landscapes in close-up, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Wildflower meadow prompts work because the breeze motion is uniform across the field. Easy to render.
Pattern inspired by BBC Springwatch and nature relaxation content.
antiPatterns: Do not include hikers or picnickers. The empty meadow is the message.
Do not add music with lyrics. The ambient audio is the score.

prompts.title: Food preparation overhead recipe
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second overhead food preparation sequence for a recipe Reel or cooking short.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a food DP shooting an overhead recipe preparation sequence for a cooking short.</role>
<scene>A clean wooden cutting board on a marble counter. Small bowls of ingredients arranged around the edges: chopped vegetables, herbs, spices, a lemon half.</scene>
<subject>The cook's hands (no face visible) preparing {{dish_brief: a vegetable stir-fry, a fresh salad, a pasta dish}}. Hands perform the prep step by step.</subject>
<motion>Static overhead locked. The hands enter with the main ingredient, slice or chop it, transfer it to a bowl, repeat with the next ingredient. By the end, all ingredients are prepped and arranged. Clean, organized, deliberate.</motion>
<camera>Locked overhead phone or small tripod, 24mm equivalent, deep focus across the entire prep area.</camera>
<lighting>Soft daylight from a window off-frame left, supplemented by a soft overhead LED panel. No harsh shadows.</lighting>
<audio>The knife on the board. The tap of a vegetable being set down. A pinch of salt. The crack of an egg. No music.</audio>
<output>1:1 or 9:16 vertical, 24fps, 18 seconds, food-magazine color, slight saturation on ingredients.</output>
<negative>no face, no other people, no text, no logos, no voiceover, no music, no camera motion, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Overhead food prep is the most reliable cooking format. The hands stay in the frame and the motion vocabulary is clear.
Pattern inspired by Tasty and NYT Cooking recipe videos.
antiPatterns: Do not show the cook's face. Hands-only is the genre.
Do not add voiceover. Audio goes in edit.

prompts.title: Fashion runway front shot
prompts.when: You need a 15 second fashion runway walk shot for a brand campaign or lookbook.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a fashion DP shooting a runway walk from the end of the catwalk.</role>
<scene>A straight runway at a fashion show. Reflective white floor. Soft front-row silhouettes barely visible on both sides.</scene>
<subject>One model walking toward camera wearing {{outfit_description}}. Outfit is the subject.</subject>
<motion>Static locked wide from the runway end. The model walks from the far end toward camera. Editorial pace: confident, slow, eyes forward. Final 3 seconds: model walks past camera and out of frame.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod at runway end, 85mm equivalent, shallow depth of field with front-row silhouettes soft, focus on the model.</camera>
<lighting>Single hard front key from upper-mid (show lighting). White floor bounce. No colored gels.</lighting>
<audio>The rhythm of heels on the runway. Distant show ambience. A single sustained bass note. No music.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 15 seconds, high-fashion editorial color, slight desaturation.</output>
<negative>no audience faces visible, no other models, no readable text on the runway, no music with lyrics, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Single runway walk is achievable because the trajectory is a straight line. Multiple outfits in one walk break the model.
Pattern inspired by Dior, Chanel, and Saint Laurent single-shot runway opens.
antiPatterns: Do not show the audience clearly. Silhouettes are enough.
Do not include close-ups. The full body walk is the format.

prompts.title: Product 360 showcase turntable
prompts.when: You need a 12 to 15 second 360 product showcase on a turntable for an ad or product page.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a product DP setting up a turntable 360 showcase for a small consumer product.</role>
<scene>A clean light-grey studio with a soft sweep background. The product sits on a small pedestal.</scene>
<subject>The product: {{product_description: a perfume bottle, a watch on a stand, a coffee mug, a sneaker}}. Centered, lit.</subject>
<motion>The product sits on a slowly rotating turntable, completing one full 360 rotation in 12 to 15 seconds. The camera stays locked. The reflection rotates with the product.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 100mm equivalent macro for small items or 50mm for larger items, focus locked on the product.</camera>
<lighting>Single large softbox above and slightly in front. Subtle white card fill on the side opposite the softbox. No colored gels.</lighting>
<audio>A clean soft synth pad that builds slowly. A single chime when the logo faces camera.</audio>
<output>1:1 or 16:9, 24fps, 12 seconds, hyperreal product color, balanced exposure.</output>
<negative>no people, no visible turntable mechanism, no text on product, no music with lyrics, no other props, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Turntable 360 is the standard product showcase. The turntable does the work, the camera stays out of the way.
Pattern inspired by Apple, Samsung, and Sony product launch reels.
antiPatterns: Do not move the camera. The rotating product is the motion.
Do not include other props. The product alone.

prompts.title: Mechanical watch movement
prompts.when: You need a 10 to 15 second extreme macro of a mechanical watch movement for a horology brand reel.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a horological macro DP capturing a watch movement in operation.</role>
<scene>A mechanical watch movement placed on black velvet. The balance wheel, gears, jewels, and screws all visible. No case, no strap.</scene>
<subject>The movement itself: gears, balance wheel, escapement, jewels. Photoreal macro detail.</subject>
<motion>Static locked macro. The gears turn, the balance wheel oscillates back and forth at 4Hz (the watch tick). The escapement clicks. Tiny jewel reflections shift as gears rotate.</motion>
<camera>Macro probe lens, 100mm equivalent, very shallow depth of field, focus locked on the balance wheel area.</camera>
<lighting>Single hard key from upper left. Black flag on right for contrast. The jewels catch pinpoints of light as they rotate.</lighting>
<audio>The tick-tock at 4Hz. The faint metallic resonance. Silence underneath.</audio>
<output>16:9, 24fps, 12 seconds, hyperreal macro color, deep blacks.</output>
<negative>no people, no hands, no other objects, no text on the movement, no music with lyrics, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Watch movement macros are achievable because the motion vocabulary is well-defined (tick, oscillate, gear turn).
Pattern inspired by horology brand reels and Patek Philippe / Audemars Piguet showcases.
antiPatterns: Do not show hands or the watch case. The movement alone.
Do not add music. The tick-tock is the audio.

prompts.title: Coffee pour with crema
prompts.when: You need a 10 to 15 second espresso pour shot for a cafe brand or morning routine reel.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a cafe DP capturing an espresso pour into a small cup.</role>
<scene>A cafe counter. The espresso machine's portafilter is positioned over a small ceramic cup. Warm cafe light, soft wood and brass tones.</scene>
<subject>The espresso stream pouring from the portafilter into the cup. The crema forming on top of the espresso as it fills.</subject>
<motion>Static close-up. The espresso begins to flow, a thin dark stream at first, then thickening as the crema builds. The cup fills slowly. The crema settles into a tan-colored layer on top. The pour ends. Hold on the full cup.</motion>
<camera>Locked tripod, 50mm equivalent, shallow depth of field with focus on the cup rim, the stream sharp.</camera>
<lighting>Warm softbox key from upper right. Cool blue ambient from the cafe's distant lighting. The crema catches warm highlights.</lighting>
<audio>The hiss of the espresso machine. The sound of the pour. The cafe ambience in the background. No music.</audio>
<output>9:16 or 1:1, 24fps, 12 seconds, warm cafe color, deep browns and tans.</output>
<negative>no people, no text, no other cups, no music, no barista hands in close-up, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Espresso pour is the single most achievable coffee shot. The motion is uniform and the crema forms naturally.
Pattern inspired by specialty coffee brand reels and Blue Bottle / Stumptown content.
antiPatterns: Do not include the barista's hands in close-up. The pour alone.
Do not add music. The machine hiss is the audio.

prompts.title: Floral bouquet arrangement
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second overhead floral arrangement shot for a florist brand or lifestyle reel.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are a floral DP capturing an overhead bouquet arrangement for a florist brand.</role>
<scene>A clean marble counter. Loose flowers and greenery arranged in piles around the edges. A vase with water at center.</scene>
<subject>The florist's hands (no face visible) arranging the bouquet. The flowers: {{flower_list: roses, peonies, eucalyptus, ranunculus}}.</subject>
<motion>Static overhead locked. The hands pick individual stems, trim them with shears, place them in the vase one at a time. The arrangement builds slowly, gaining fullness. By the end, the bouquet is complete.</motion>
<camera>Locked overhead phone or tripod, 24mm equivalent, deep focus across the entire arrangement.</camera>
<lighting>Soft diffused daylight from a window off-frame left. No harsh shadows on the petals.</lighting>
<audio>The snip of the shears. The rustle of leaves. The florist's breath. No music.</audio>
<output>1:1 or 9:16 vertical, 24fps, 18 seconds, floral magazine color, vivid and warm.</output>
<negative>no face, no other people, no text, no logos, no music, no rapid motion, no cuts.</negative>
notes: Overhead floral arrangement is achievable because the motion vocabulary is bounded (pick, trim, place).
Pattern inspired by florist brand reels and Kinfolk-style lifestyle content.
antiPatterns: Do not show the florist's face. Hands-only is the genre.
Do not add music. The shears and rustle are the audio.

prompts.title: Pouring paint mix abstract
prompts.when: You need a 15 to 20 second paint pour abstract for an art short or brand opening.
prompts.prompt:
<role>You are an abstract DP capturing a single paint pour sequence in extreme macro.</role>
<scene>A clean white canvas. A cup of mixed acrylic paint (multiple colors) ready to pour.</scene>
<subject>The paint itself: liquid color, swirling as it pours, spreading across the canvas.</subject>
<motion>Static macro. The paint is poured from the cup onto the canvas. It flows outward in a viscous stream, the colors swirling together but not fully mixing. The paint reaches the edges of the canvas. Final 5 seconds: the paint settles, micro-bubbles rising.</motion>
<camera>Locked overhead macro, 50mm equivalent with extension, deep focus across the canvas.</camera>
<lighting>Soft top light to highlight the paint's surface tension. Side light to catch the colors as they swirl.</lighting>
<audio>The viscous pour of the paint. The slight thump of the cup being set down. Silence afterwards. No music.</audio>
<output>1:1, 24fps, 18 seconds, vivid abstract color, high saturation.</output>
<negative>no people, no text, no other objects, no music, no rapid cuts, no digital art look.</negative>
notes: Paint pour prompts work because the motion is fluid and the colors blend organically.
Pattern inspired by abstract art films and the Pour Painting trend.
